text: Extraordinary. The monument is worth visiting; it is a magical and magnificent place. It's a shame we could not see the outside due to the barriers (the arrival of the Giro and the next day being a national holiday), but the interior is truly impressive and majestic.
Impressive, beautiful. Visited the Coliseum in February 2026. A very impressive historical site, the scale and atmosphere are really striking, everything left a strong impression.

Book Direct for the Arena Ticket. We visited the Colosseum using the Full Experience Ticket with Entry to the Arena of the Colosseum, booked directly through the official Parco Archeologico del Colosseo website.

After finding many Colosseum, Palatine Hill, and Roman Forum tours sold out the day before, booking direct was excellent value at €24. Some third-party providers appeared to be charging significantly more simply to secure tickets, so it’s worth checking the official site first.

The ticket included timed admission to the Colosseum, entry to the Arena, access to the Roman Forum, Palatine Hill, Imperial Fora, SUPER sites, and exhibitions. The Colosseum entry time is specific, so don’t arrive expecting to enter well ahead of your slot.

The Colosseum itself is extraordinary: fascinating, powerful, and an architectural wonder. Standing inside and seeing the Arena level gave us a deeper appreciation for the scale and engineering of the site.

There is also a free official MyColosseum app for iOS and Android, with interactive audio guides in Italian and English. I found the format a little less engaging personally, but it’s worth downloading if you’re visiting without a guide.

Tip: book direct, reserve early, bring water, sunscreen, snacks, and comfortable shoes.

Even more impressive than expected. At the Colosseum, I was not only impressed by the size but especially by the thought of how old this structure actually is. You stand in front of it and think about how many generations of people have already seen it. The atmosphere around it is, of course, very lively, but as soon as you take a little time and look at the details, the place has a completely different effect. For me, it was less a tourist attraction and more a piece of history that surprisingly still stands in the middle of modern Rome.

Magnificent experience, ignorant staff.. Magnificent experience at the Colosseum; touching a piece of history so important to our country is truly something unique! It's a shame about the experience with the staff, especially referring to the girl in charge of the elevators on the second floor for going down (curly blonde, a bit overweight, time of the incident 11:25/30). I should mention that I am a person with a disability and have a condition that does not allow me to exert physical effort, so I needed the elevator. There were no problems going up; the girl helped me, my mother, and my sister, and she was very kind. However, I cannot say the same for going down, as once we queued, the girl looked at us and exclaimed, "a bit too much." I explained to her that I needed to take the elevator, and she responded, "it's only for special necessities." I explained again (in Italian, and the girl responded in English) that I have a disability and that I NEED to take the elevator. Then she looked at me and my family and exclaimed, "do all three of you have a disability?" with a somewhat mocking expression. In the end, my mother and I were able to take the elevator while my sister had to take the stairs. 100% ignorance.

Beautiful. We did not enter because there was a very long line, but even just from the outside, the Colosseum leaves one speechless. Its grandeur and beauty are immediately noticeable, and it is incredible to think about how much history it holds. It is definitely a place to visit at least once in a lifetime.

Make sure you walk around the Inside - recommend a tour. Somethings are smaller and less impressive when you actually see it, but the Colosseum is not one of those. You can walk around it from the outside for free, but you really need to go inside with a guided tour if possible. We used LivTours to see the Forum and Colosseum. It included access the partial arena floor where the gladiators fought but not the lowest level where the animals were kept but you could see down into that area.

Bathrooms: After you get through the long line (we were able to bypass it with the tour), there are some bathrooms after the entrance turnstile but there was a long, long line for the women's bathroom. Instead if you walk around the Colosseum and can find the back exit which is located down a hill/steps there are accessible toilets (10 am - 7 pm usually) located under the hill/steps. It is open to the public.

The main accessible entrance is located at the Sperone Valadier entrance, which is specifically designed for visitors with reduced mobility. Here, you can enter without stairs and receive assistance if needed.

Once inside, elevators provide access to different levels of the Colosseum. Keep in mind the area around the Colosseum has the original large stone and cobblestone around it which make for uneven walking or pushing.

For our tour, we walked around the main floor then walked up the 50 steep steps to a higher level and then back down to the area floor.

The things that stood out to me were:

\- the Colosseum used to be filled with water for ship battles

\- the elevators used to bring up animals to the arena floor - they could come up from several different locations

\- The shade awnings (no longer there) that were used to provide shade to the upper levels where the common people sat.

\- in the Middle Ages, many people lived in the abandoned Colosseum since property ownership wasn't a thing. There are display cases with some of the artifacts found.
Perfect. Historic place that deserves to be visited. It is good to purchase the entrance ticket in advance because you can face a big queue.

\#1 Attraction on Planet Earth!. What can I add that others have not? It’s magnificent! As is the Forum. Surprisingly breezy and shady inside! Our guide was through City Walkers. Allesandra was the best! She was an art historian who was born and raised in that very neighborhood, in the shadow of the Colosseum, the greatest historic site on Earth. Allesandra was an absolute expert in knowledge of the sites and in managing the crowd and tour. She’s spent her life studying its every detail, and we were so lucky she shared her knowledge with our family. The pace of the tour was fine. We started in the Forum and ended in the Colosseum. We had earbuds. I cannot stress enough how critical it is to bring water and a hat and dress in light, breathable clothing. I witnessed two elderly ladies attempting to haul big heavy bags with them. They didn’t make it. They did not have the stamina to make it through the 3 hour tour. It’s worth the hype. It’s worth the crowds. It must have really been something! I highly recommend City Walkers and of course, the Colosseum itself.

magnificent place.. The monument is definitely worth a visit....

the interior is truly impressive and majestic.....if you stop by in rome, you can not miss this view!

3rd time still amazing!. This was my third holiday to Rome and the Colosseum remains magnificent every time. It really isn't enough to see it from the outside, I would highly recommend going inside as you get to see the underground where all the animals and gladiators were kept. Not only this but there are multiple artifacts and information detailing the history of the colosseum which are well worth the read.

I took my partner who was very happy that there were designated smoking spots inside the colosseum.

We did the roman Forum first and he did make a comment that he found the Colosseum underwhelming after doing the forum first.

I recommend booking a month in advance doing the colosseum first in the morning between 9 and 12am then doing the forum in the afternoon especially if its your first time.

Alternatively if you don't know where to have breakfast and your in a rush do the forum first as they have a café on site!

I would also highly recommend going for an evening walk through the city and walking past the Colosseum at night when its all lit up is an experience in itself, its quiet and safe so you can sit there and marvel while eating an ice cream together and really appreciate the moment.
Colosseum. Very exciting place.. beautiful for its history.. there were about 80 thousand Roma on a day of fight..

Wonderful. Even before realizing it, upon exiting the subway, the impact is almost unreal.

You immediately find yourself in front of this imposing structure that, in an instant, transports you directly to the time of the ancient Romans.

It is simply a fabulous monument that, with its majesty, dominates the entire surrounding landscape.

Utterly sublime. Been multiple times now!! The history is utterly breathtaking!

I have always sorted tickets before going just for ease, and have never had a particularly long queue time!

Fully worth it!!

For 2000 years, a wonder of man. The Colosseum is Rome. Built in the Flavian era by Vespasian in 70 AD and completed by Titus in 80 AD. It is an emotion every time you look at it today; we think of the image of grandeur it conveyed 2000 years ago: standing 52 meters tall (now 48), capable of holding up to 87,000 spectators, with an oval shape and a perimeter of 527 meters, featuring an arena of 3357 square meters. A UNESCO World Heritage site, it is no longer just the symbol of Rome but a national symbol in the world. Absolutely worth visiting.

Must See Marvel. This is a must see.

The queues were quite short and we went almost straight in.

You're left to go at your own pace and wander round.

It's a stunning attraction - beautiful inside and out - and full of history. We took our time to enjoy it as it was the highlight of our stay in Rome.

We didn't go on the arena floor itself because we hadn't booked a ticket that includes that, but we found you could still see everything and there was no pressure to move on.

Absolutely a must-see at the Colosseum!. A guided tour of the Colosseum is a must in Rome; we had a great guide who could tell us many historical and modern stories. Be sure to book a tour well in advance!

A millennia-old experience... with an unexpected touch of modernity!. The Colosseum always takes your breath away; its majesty is indescribable and it is a must-see in Rome. Excellent organization and smooth flow of visits. The most absurd thing happened while I was taking photos: I activated an experimental Augmented Reality (AR) filter on my phone and the application had a major bug, "dressing" the amphitheater in a yellow lace pattern (I attached the screenshot; you have to see it because it’s hilarious!). An incredible mix of Roman history and modern digital short circuits. Highly recommended!

Unmissable.. We went to Rome to celebrate my birthday there. The visit to the Colosseum was a must. A marvel. In my case, having reduced mobility, with accreditation, we had free tickets without lines....

Very hot, but worth it!. We went to the colosseum at around 10am, and it was very busy (which is of course to be expected). It’s a must see when coming to Rome. Please note if you’re going in the summer, there isn’t much shade, so it’s very hot! Drink plenty of water.

It was amazing though to see it all in person and learn the history. Glad we went. We also went to palatine hill after, which was beautiful.

Because of the busyness, it was a little hard to find our tour guide, but once found, it was just navigating the crowd to get inside.

Lots of security checks on our way through, which made me feel safer in the crowds.

Beautiful. We did not enter because there was a very long line, but even just from the outside, the Colosseum leaves one speechless. Its grandeur and beauty are immediately noticeable, and it is incredible to think about how much history it holds. It is definitely a place to visit at least once in a lifetime.

Must see. We toured the colosseum with general admission tickets and preferred that option. We really enjoyed seeing it from the inside and outside and just being able to take it all in without a tour group or guide. The tickets are very reasonable just to enter. We would recommend going if you are in the area!

Try for the underground. Underground is worth doing if you can get a ticket. I got mine by waking up at 1:30am central time to try to get tickets as they dropped 30 days before (failed until 3:30am). I wish there was a better way but that's what you have to do. The ticket line for same day tickets is massive. If you have to stand in line, bring an umbrella for the sun as there is no shade.

There is a special place to enter that's marked outside the colloseum. When inside it's really easy to find the meeting spot. Fascinating tour. Also it brings you to a private area near the arena with no people except your group so you can see some really interesting things up close. There are tons of people at the colloseum so having a quiet tour to yourself is great. The tour concludes in the arena and you then have as long as you want to walk to the 2nd floor or stay in the arena.
Imposing place. Mandatory place to visit in Rome. Imposing, wonderful, awesome. I recommend paying for admission to enter.

The must visit to do in Rome. Wonderful to see and take your time. Queuing is fairly fast but best to reserve your tickets online in advance. Beautiful views on first and second floor. Take your time and enjoy.

Beautiful. It was incredible, and I was amazed…

I can't wait to see you again.
AMAZING. Amazing experience! Worth the money! Legendary tour guide Dennis!

Rome the magnificent. I had the opportunity to visit Rome thanks to Carole Faude Panetta, an unforgettable visit that I would gladly repeat. A unique guide, Mario, to whom I extend a big thank you for this wonderful day.
The history. Wooooooaw this Is the history for all the time gooood
Amazing!. Coliseum was amazing! Surprisingly ticket prices was not as expensive as I thought.

The most amazing tour, thanks to our incredible tour guide Andrea. I’ve been to the Colosseum three times now this was my first visit with a proper guided tour. Our guide was an amazing guide called Andrea Morganti. You can find his details at his Instagram account at andrea\_tourguide\_rome. He gave us the most incredible guided tour. It’s really brought his extraordinary place to life. If I’m going again, I would definitely engage him. Thanks Andrea for an incredible tour!

Excellent Colosseum tour with a well-educated guide!. Don’t think twice, contact David Battaglino for the very best tour of the Colosseum in Rome. David@davidsrome.com. He’s a quick-witted well educated tour guide who truly enjoys what he does. Very insightful.
definitely worth it. Buying a ticket in advance , definitely worth it , crowds weren't too bad
Beautiful. Ofcourse this was a must see for me, happy to check it off my bucket list.
A magical place🌟. The beautiful sunny day allowed us to get to know this wonderful amphitheater making us live unique emotions, considering the costs it was worth it! We recommend!

Great Visit into History. Visited Italy for my Birthday and did the Colosseum visit. We purchased skip the line tickets but beware, even with skip the line you’ll have a wait. We had the normal tour but even without touring the arena floor it was quite spectacular. It was larger than I had imagined. Lots of intricate details to keep you busy for some time or you can just hit the high points. The history and architecture is amazing. Definitely a great part of my bucket list.
Rome. A symbol of the city, visited by all the citizens of the world, the most famous amphitheater on the planet and a symbol of Rome and the pride of its ancestors.

Colosseum is a must-see !. Visiting the colosseum was one of the most amazing experiences of my trip! It was incredible to walk through the same paths where gladiators and ancient Romans once stood thousands of years ago. The history and atmosphere are unforgettable, and seeing the massive structure in person is absolutely breathtaking.

If you’re not doing a guided tour, I highly recommend purchasing your tickets directly through Parco Archeologico del Colosseo, the official Colosseum ticket website. Buy your tickets early because they do sell out quickly! They offer several ticket options, including the 24h Colosseum, Roman Forum & Palatine ticket, as well as Full Experience tickets with Arena Floor and Underground access.

The Arena Floor tickets are awesome because you get to stand where the gladiators once fought, and the Underground tour is fascinating to see the hidden chambers beneath the arena. This is truly a must-see experience in Rome!
great and unique. it was a great unique place and we like this place and will come back
A must see. A must see if visiting Rome , queues not too bad. It get there early
Impressive!. Impermeable! A wonder of Roman architecture.

Colosseum❤️. An unforgettable experience.

I give excellent because when I went there it was free before they put it on charge.

We highly recommend it!. As a huge history fan, I’d been so excited to visit Rome. The Colosseum really stole the show. We had to wait in line for a bit, but it was totally worth it. Absolutely stunning! 👏👏👏

Grandiose 🇮🇹🇮🇹. We arrived from the subway directly on the Coliseum , it is very large and very pretty we went a bit around right side there is space but a lot of people. We made some photo .. next time I would book to be able to go inside for a new experience. See if you went to Rome ..
Excellent experience, but plan well to avoid crowds.. An excellent and interesting experience, although queues can get very long, so would recommend you book for an early start.

To do but especially for the gourmet and mont palalatin which offers a superb view of the city and the forum. Must-see but it is a real ruin and do not count hard any explanatory or playful panels...

The single ticket including forum and me palatine is more than enough, the latter two are much more interesting than the inside of the Coliseum!
7 Wonders of the world. The Colosseum is truly one of the seven wonders of the world and I was so happy I got to see it with my own eyes.

Great experience. Visiting the Colosseum was unforgettable. The sheer size and history of this ancient amphitheater are incredible, and it’s easy to imagine the events that once took place there. Walking through the ruins gives you a real sense of connection to the past. It can get crowded, but it’s absolutely worth it for the experience

Spectacular!. Absolutely amazing! As a disabled person, I have to say that every landmark we visited in Rome was extremely accommodating.

There is a lift to the first floor, which was a very welcome sight. A lot of the flooring was uneven, which was tricky for me, but was absolutely expected for a building of this age.

It was VERY crowded with a lot of guided tours that consisted of big groups.

Great Experience. Did not disappoint, full of history and extremely impressive in person. Tickets released 30 days in advance, but they sell out quick! Ensure you book only through the official site (colosseo.it) - another visitor in front of us was turned away with fake tickets. We purchased the 24H Colosseum, Forum and Palatine Hill tickets (18€ each). Staff were friendly, helpful and efficient. Not too much queueing for 9am entry slot, although it was crowded with guided tours inside.
Excellent. Everything is very clear and easy, they keep in touch with you
Impressive. Even with a lot of research I didn't expect to be so overwhelmed. Even without a guided tour or with an audio guide, you will find corners that fascinate you.
Fun time at the colosseum. It was very fun crowds were big but line wasn’t long provided you buy tickets beforehand.

Unique historical monument. Unique historical monument.

Very well preserved.

Beautiful at night.

I was unhappy to book the tickets for the arena, as I couldn't get the rest. We were allowed to enter the arena in a space of a few square meters compared to the world it had, without the ability to see anything more. It was also the reason I thought to lower my rating but I didn't because I thought that this monument definitely deserves a 5/5.

Breathtaking!. We visited the colosseum with tickets for the arena floor with a tour guide.

It is possible to visit independently, but I found the information that the guide explained to us really added to our visit and brought it to life. Our visit was booked for 5:30pm, and after queuing for a short time to enter, the tour lasted approximately one hour. As it was near closing time, we then had a little while to enjoy the views in relatively quiet atmosphere as many people were starting to leave.

There isn’t much to say apart from the colosseum is just spectacular and an absolute “must see” if you are visiting Rome.

A must-see despite the tourist traps and crowds. It's a must-do, even if it's a tourist classic. Arrival at the top of the hill is kind of a nightmare with scammers and sellers all over the place - please just ignore them and don't engage with them, it will make your life easier.Otherwise, it seems they keep them away from the actual area of the forum, colosseum, and old city area because they don't seem to bother you there.It's surprising how cool it is and how much it affects you to actually see this in person so despite it being a touristy thing, it's something you just have to go see.

Wow just wow. This is right up there with all the places I've been lucky enough to visit. The history is just incredible. We booked skip the line tickets which covered palatine hill and the Roman Forum.

For me this is the number 1 place to visit in Rome. When we visited the temp was mid twenties so bear that in mind as it felt much much hotter.... go in the morning would be my tip. I think this would be hard for people with mobility issues but they do have elevators which helps.

We did the tour with access to the arena and would definitely recommend that. You can also buy tours with the underground but we didn't do that. Looks good though.

If you visit Rome please book a tour of the colosseum rather than just view it from outside.

Fabulous!. Buy online before you go,about 2 months or more before as times were selling out very quickly,as queues can be bad.Ive been before this is my 4th!It will be my last the steps were not great with my knees 😂.Fabulous as always.We included Roman Forum and Pallastine Hill in our tour.

Get There Early. We booked tickets online for 9am. We arrived by 8am and there not many people around. Got to walk around take lots of pics and enjoy. Having a timed ticket you have 15 minutes before and 15 minutes after to enter for your time slot, do be sure you are there. You will also need to present Id and also go through security. All up only took less than 10 minutes to get through.

It was busy but well worth the trip we took our time, there is lots to see and displays have information in English

Amazing history!!!. Amazing history!!! Be prepared to climb stairs and navigating crowds though! It’s probably bigger than you thought and it’s well preserved. It is worth getting at tour to skip the line and listening to the history.

Rome caput mundi. The Colosseum is the symbol of the grandeur of the Roman Empire: Roma caput mundi. It is one of the "Seven Wonders of the World". The only one in all of Europe. It was completed in the year 80 AD and had a celebratory function. Put aside the important practical-competitive-religious-theatrical functions of the largest amphitheater in History. The function of the Colosseum was mainly mystical: it was to magnify the Roman Empire that stretched from Britain to Africa, from Spain to Mesopotamia and beyond, from the Atlantic to the Persian Gulf. And to magnify the most articulate and complex civilization in History. Where Rome arrived, it created Provinces entrusting the government to local governors and creating infrastructure with cutting-edge building techniques. Delivering art, culture and law. Going so far as to recognize and grant Roman citizenship also to "foreign" residents in the "conquered" provinces by fulfilling their requirements. Rome was a bridge between the Peoples of the World. It allowed the fusion of cultures and peoples. Roma caput mundi. Start. Domenico Di Paolo - Isernia (IS)

Awesome piece of History. You should certainly use a tour guide to get a better understanding of the history! Be prepared to walk, climb stairs and navigate crowds! It’s certainly a historic place to visit!

Impressive, but a lot of patience needed for long waiting times. What was built almost 2000 years ago and what still stands today is impressive.

This is despite the fact that it has been handled with far less care over the centuries.

All the better that it is now better preserved and maintained.

Only the mass of visitors, the immense crowd and the long waiting times are unpleasant.

Spectacular. The most famous monument in the world and symbol of the greatness of Rome. The building was intended for fighting, gladiator games and simulations of hunting wild and exotic animals. It consists of four overlapping architectural orders; the first three are formed by eighty arches framed by half-columns, the fourth is divided into squares interspersed with windows. It could accommodate about 50 thousand spectators who could sit in the cavea. Despite the two thousand years of history, the imposing structure still transmits incredible power, you have a blast from the past. Beautiful view of the arena from the 2nd floor
it makes you feel untalented. the movies does not do it justice. at 2000 years it still looks incredible. like a dream. the amount of visitors is overwhelming though .

Attic. We booked attic full experience tickets. There are very few of these tickets sold, and the peace was amazing compared to the rest of the coleseum. There were about 20 of us up there in total and looking down on the scale of the building was amazing. We needed passports to get in and people without id were being turned away. Would book these tickets, from the official site, again.
Must see. Absolutely iconic place to visit. Yes very busy but no real queue with none guided ticket. The small museum section is worth reading. My 21 year old daughter loved it here

Really impressive!. We went around 9am when it wasn’t too busy, with pre-booked tickets to go onto the main floor area. It was so impressive! Whilst it looks big from the outside, it really takes your breath away inside. The ticket also included the forum which was really interesting, covering so much history. Also a great value coffee shop near the exit. Highly recommend.

A timeless wonder that never fails to amaze. The Colosseum is without doubt one of the greatest

ancient structures ever built. Standing at the entrance,

you immediately feel the weight of 2,000 years of history.

The architecture is extraordinary — it is hard to believe

this massive amphitheater was constructed in just 8 years.

Inside, you can almost hear the roar of 80,000 spectators.

A deeply moving and unforgettable experience for any

history lover visiting Rome. Book tickets in advance

to skip the long queues at the entrance.

EWTN Via Crucis. Just like I was in Rome, Italy!!!! The EWTN Via Crucis coverage on my iPhone was very moving for me! I felt that I was in Rome! I was awe inspired by the Stations of the Cross at the Colosseum in Rome through EWTN coverage! Pope Leo XIV carried the cross himself through all 14 stations of the Way of the Cross at Rome's Colosseum on the first Good Friday of his papacy! It was very impressive that Pope Leo XIV carried the wooden cross at Rome's Colosseum ! Throughout the coverage, I place myself in Rome! I look forward for a glorious Easter! Here’s my Easter Sunrise arrangement which I made using the Holy See colors! Have a blessed Easter everyone!

Totally amazing for nerds and non nerds. It was obviously amazing but lots of crowds but that is obvious it was an amazing experience and the queue wasn't that long surprisingly but of course I like it I love roman history the only bad thin is that the things in the gift shop are over priced there was a 3d printed coloseum for 500 euros !!!

But overall amazing experience

History and reflection. It is a magical place, it has an impressive energy, being in a place that was such an important part of history, it is always imposing, walking and walking it fills you with indescribable emotions, without a doubt, I would return.

Amazing. Absolutely amazing! The sense of history is incredible. One ticked off the bucket list!

We wish we had paid for the lower level tour tickets also, but will return!

Be prepared for airport style security, and there is limits of bag size so check before you go in.

Worth every penny of the entrance fee

Impressive architectural work. It's a yes or yes on a visit to Rome.

Hire a guided tour in your language to make the most of your visit and buy tickets at least three months in advance.

Impressing to see what civilization was capable of 2000 years ago.
Great. Fantastic but very expensive. Overall i was very satisfied with my visit but i dont think i would visit again. Must see location.
Must come once. Absolutely amazing, absolutely to see. Be patient, queues are long all year round. Book your tickets in advance.
Must see. Was fantastic being stood where so much history happened. The views were amazing. It is definitely a bucket list item ticked off!
Bellisimo. An amazing experience, Rome is a dream. The energy felt by the Colosseum is very powerful, it was a great experience!
Don’t miss it. This place is unbelievable, it really is a marvel of craftmenship. Absolutely do not miss it and lit up at night is breathtaking.
It’s HUGE!. What an amazing piece of history. We purchased tickets in advance for a self guided tour, highly recommend that vs guided.
Amazing Sight to See. Such an amazing place to see, many tours to take including self guided. You can spend quite some time here taking it all in.

Colosseum. Its grandeur makes you greet it even from the plane; it's a must-see historical site, it looks even more beautiful from the outside, and it's especially lovely in the evening. We bought our tickets online and got in without waiting in line.

Morning tour. I’ll start by saying this is 100% something you should book! Our tour guide was sensational! Every fact every area of the tour was covered I’d highly recommend! Weather was amazing also, over all 10/10 site.
Impressive. Get to the Colloseum early to avoid long queues. Reasonably priced for £18 which included the Forum.

Nice experience. If you come to Rome the Colosseum is one of the fundamental places to visit.

A historical attraction of great charm no doubt, we to visit it well took just over 2 hours and it was a nice experience.

Top Attractions in Rome. It is recommended to buy tickets in advance, in addition to taking photos of the appearance. Note that there are different prices for tickets, package tours to different regions, you can read the reference information in advance and purchase the suitable ticket according to your needs.

Impressive place. Impressive place, lots to see and you can be in there for quite a while if you walk all the way around. It was very busy inside, lots of tour groups holding up the flow of the crowd but was better once you got into the centre of the arena space. The staff were helpful.
unforgettable. Always beautiful especially in the evening with a sunset. You never tire of seeing it, a must-see for a rome

Dream.. We went overnight and it was one of our best experiences in Rome. We were sitting across and enjoying the whole scene. We didn't go in because I believe that all the beauty is more than the outside. Many had a drink or something to eat and doing in front of so many places that has and enjoy this work of art.... It also has countless good places to take photos.
Great attraction. Like many other attractions, it is definitely worth taking this with you but not necessarily going in. There is chaos without equals and it is asked if it is worth it
Cool.. Extremely cool and surreal experience. The Coliseum was really huge!

Fantastic.. I've always wanted to see the Coliseum, but it's never happened, but now it happened that way on my wife's 60th birthday trip. Incredibly cool and worth every minute as history interested, despite enormous crowds and queuing despite pre-purchased tickets. A tip that I myself received is to book in advance, which makes it a little easier and faster. We also had nice weather. It's impressive how much is left after so long.
The Colosseum, a majesty.. Oh, great. How impressive it is to see a monument that was used for so many battles and to know the real use it had. Undoubtedly an experience to live when going to Rome.

Tour of Europe. Went to Rome on vacation, couldn't help but visit this legendary historical site in Italy. The excursions were very enjoyable. We recommend it! If you're in Rome, be sure to visit the Colosseum.

Are you not entertained!? Go with a guide. Booked a guided tour of the Colosseum (Underground, Arena Floor & Spectator Viewing) through "The Roman Guy Tours" which also toured the Roman Forum & Palatine Hill. I would not recommend doing the Colosseum without a guide. There were so many people & the lines went every which way. It would have been hard to determine what line was for what experience. We got to walk past all the lines & our guide made sure our entire party made it past the various checkpoints each & every time. This is also, in my opinion the best way to ensure seeing the Underground portion of the Colosseum. We tried getting lucky on the Parco Archeologico website every day, but the tickets are like gold dust. Our guide was extremely knowledgable about the history of ancient Rome & had the practical experience to navigate us through the busy sights to maximize our viewing experience.

TOP the coliseum. Superb monument this coliseum

Be careful to take the ticks well with the Roman and Palatine forum

It's 18€ on the coliseum.it website

Avoid GYG or Tiqets
Wonderful wonder of the world. We loved the great tourist attraction of Rome

Coliseum tickets at the right price. Exceptional experience to visit this iconic place of ancient Rome

The iconic Colosseum. The Colosseum is probably the most famous site in Rome, and for good reason--completed in AD 80, it is the largest ancient amphitheater in the world with tons of interesting history. Given its size and complicated entry procedures, it's best to take a guided tour, which typically comes with entry to the Roman Forum.

Different tours give access to different parts of the amphitheater, and while it's a bit extra, getting access to the arena floor offers unique views of the subterranean passages and seating bowl. There are some exhibit areas that are worth exploring to check out how the Colosseum has changed over the centuries.

Overall, this is a can't-miss attraction in the Eternal City.

The famous Colosseum. The Coliseum

The Romans loved their entertainment. I discovered, as a result of Gladitorial games, The Atlas bear (Ursus arctos crowtheri), Africa's only native bear species, was driven to extinction.

Flamma (lit. The Flame) was a Syrian gladiator under the Roman Empire during the reign of Hadrian. He was one of the most famous and successful gladiators of his time.

Flamma was awarded the rudius, or wooden sword that granted freedom, four times; but, each time he refused this freedom and chose to remain a gladiator. The number of fights Flamma engaged in is a lot higher than most gladiators. Many have lower numbers like Purricina Iuvenus who fought 5 times or Glaucus of Modena who fought 7 times. Flamma had fought 34 times and won 21 of them.

Julius Caesar gets blamed for the widespread proliferation of gladiator games. He had decided that pleasing the public with more games made himself more popular. It wasn’t always slaves, criminals, and prisoners of war who made up the gladiator population. There are apparently instances where the patrician(upper wealthy) class would become a gladiator. I think this might explain why “the gladiator” became so glorified. Three out of every five gladiators ended up losing their lives in the arena.

A Roman Holiday: Where Faith Meets History. The long-awaited trip to the Vatican was more than just a vacation; for our Filipino family, it was a profound spiritual pilgrimage that brought tears to our eyes the moment we stepped into St. Peter's Square. Walking through the hallowed halls, we felt an overwhelming sense of peace and connection to centuries of faith, a feeling impossible to describe. Transitioning from that spiritual high to the ancient, bustling heart of Rome was an incredible contrast, but one that left us equally breathless. Seeing the ruins of the Roman Forum and the Palatine Hill painted a vivid picture of a colossal empire that shaped the world. But nothing prepared us for the moment we first laid eyes on the majestic Colosseum rising before us—it was simply monumental and truly overwhelming. Standing inside the amphitheater, imagining the raw drama and history that unfolded there, gave us chills, making us feel tiny yet connected to a powerful past. This architectural marvel was the absolute highlight of our Roman holiday, a place where history’s echo seemed loudest. We left Italy with our hearts full, cherishing the memory of a journey where faith and history intertwined so beautifully for our entire family.

EXTRAORDINARY MONUMENT OF ROMANITY. In the center of Rome, adjacent to the Roman Forum and the Palatine Hill, there is this extraordinary Romanesque monument: built almost 2000 years ago it has arrived to the present day, unfortunately with some missing parts. It was an amphitheater used for gladiator shows and other public events used until the 6th century and then became a quarry of material. Polycentric oval in shape, it has a wonderful exterior facade almost 50 meters high in travertine, with a triple series of 80 arches framed by semi-columns on three orders, with an upper attic with Corinthian pilasters crowned by shelves and punctuated by windows: a charm! The magnificent inner elliptical arena had a stepped marble and wood cavea at the top, divided into horizontal sectors by audience category: from senators and families in the lower sectors, to the lower classes of the plebs in the upper sectors. Entrance was via ladders, except for emperors and authorities who had reserved entrances. In the impressive basement of the arena there were several galleries for the custody of beasts, props and elevators. The visit of the monument is perfectly organized, since you can buy the entrance ticket, including the visit of the Roman Forum and the Palatine Hill, also online, avoiding the long queue at the ticket offices and having a dedicated entrance. Price (€ 16,00), organization, friendly and helpful staff, make the visit truly magnificent! The ticket allows you to visit the first level of the Colosseum, turning the entire ellipse: it was extraordinary to admire this wonder from various perspectives, all calmly walked for a time of about 90 minutes. Exciting and spectacular experience of a unique and wonderful place and rich in history!!!! Must visit, super recommended!!!!

An eternal memory of the Power of Ancient Rome. What the barbarians didn't do, the Barbers could. Thus the Romans comment on the lack of a part of the Colosseum plundered by the descendants of one of the most important families in Rome to use parts of it to build their palaces.

Almost 2000 years have passed since its construction and even today the Colosseum

(Flavian Amphitheatre to be precise) our visitors the architectural mastery and the constructive devices that compose it prove the power of Rome at that time. As long as the Colosseum exists, Rome will also exist; when the Colosseum falls, Rome will also fall; when Rome falls, the world will also fall”, this is the prophecy that dominates it.

The queue moved quickly, the staff were efficient and friendly, and there was no messing about at all. Security was smooth, clear signs everywhere, and the whole entry process felt calm and professional.

Inside, the Colosseum was nowhere near as crowded as I expected. We were able to take our time, walk around freely, and see absolutely everything without rushing or waiting at any point. The staff were great — answering questions, guiding people politely, and keeping everything flowing perfectly.

I managed to get loads of photos in the daytime and again at night, and both were spectacular. The whole visit was super smooth from start to finish. It’s one of those places that truly lives up to the hype — the scale, the history, the atmosphere… everything feels surreal when you’re standing there.

Overall, our visit to the Colosseum was 10/10:

• ✔️ Organised entry

• ✔️ Efficient, helpful staff

the first thing to see in Rome: the Colosseum. A must-visit. It is difficult to obtain tickets two weeks in advance. One must plan ahead to avoid buying them from resellers at exorbitant prices. Plan for about 1.5 hours. There is no need to buy a ticket for the arena or the underground part; a standard ticket is sufficient (unless you want to see things up close).

City's landmark, business as usual, this time with an extra. Impressive, yes; crowded, yes; tourist factory, yes and confusing the ticket system because with or without arena or lift; Domus Aurea which I missed it again (open only in the weekends, this was the palace of Emperor Nero and the Colosseum was a reason to erase Emperor Nero works on those days) and the super sites which I made it this time. There are many super sites spread on Palatine hill and in the Forum area; most of them are not in open-air, is space limited and the delicate nature of them makes them kind of restrictive with weekly closing days. Visited them and I was impressed by beautiful frescoes located in many of them; Loggia Mattei ceilings (Renaissance XVI century), Santa Maria Antiqua church (earliest VI century) and the homes of Emperor Augustus and wife Livia (around 30 bC).

Just prepare to walk lots; when we were there, main access of some sites were closed due archaeological works so had to ask the staff alternate access and saw many other tourists turned away because they didn’t purchased the extra ticket to visit these sites.

This is the 3rd time that I visit and still have the impression that I miss other attractions inside Colosseum/Forum areas. Depending if history enthusiast or not, read and watch all info possible because it changes everytime due to closings for restoration/archaeological works and new openings of new sites, plan carefully if suit your personal interests.

A nice place. The Coliseum It is part of the UNESCO sites of Central Rome I visited the Coliseum as part of a group tour that also included Roman Forum and the Palatine Hill

We spent about 45 minutes to an hour visiting the Coliseum after going through security we went up to the second floor and walked around the site viewing the Coliseum and the various seating arrangements We then continue down to the first floor where we walked around the lower part of the colosseum but this did not include the actual underground

Overall we were able to see a decent amount of the Coliseum You could see the engineering features that were included as part of the construction It was a nice visit
Historical landmark of Rome. Because this is probably the main landmark of Rome, I have to give it 4 stars. Prices went up in the last 9 months.
Weekend in Rome. This is a MUST do when in Rome. Very easy to get to. It is a wonderful historical site. So lucky to have to be able to walk around it and get a glimpse of its history.

A can't miss attraction ... but better with a guided tour. My wife and I were booked for a guided tour of the Colisseum and surrounding Palatine Hill area, but because it was an Italian National Holiday and no English speaking guides were working that day, we were forced to tour the site on our own. Thankfully, we were able to keep our skip-the-line tickets and entry time. Otherwise we might still be standing in the enormous line waiting to get in.

Once inside, were were able to spend as much time as we liked getting the full Colisseum experience both on the ground floor and upstairs (which can be accessed either by walking up stairs or by elevator). It's an amazing place, a must-see attraction for every first-time visit to Rome. And we got as much out of experience as we would there thanks to information signs posted throughout the Colisseum, but it would have been so much more informative and meaningful if we had a knowledgable guide leading us.

Colosseum Trip. Went to the Colosseum for a day trip was very interesting the shear scale of the building and grounds. Then walked into Rome centre about 20mins as we have some slow coaches in our group.
Visit. When checking tickets, you will also need to show your ID. A mythical building to visit with the crowd. A huge amphitheatre that could accommodate about 50,000 people, impressive

Worth it. The Colosseum is incredible to see in person, but the experience can be a bit frustrating. It’s often very overcrowded, which makes it difficult to fully take everything in, and access to certain areas like the arena floor or upper levels (attic) is limited. Tickets can also be quite hard to secure. That said, it’s still an amazing and iconic landmark that’s absolutely worth visiting.

Gladly again, impressive and worth seeing !!. We booked through Getyourguide and downloaded an audio guide.

The app itself takes a bit of getting used to because you can only store one sight here at a time.

You walk around freely and listen to the corresponding explanations.

The underground cellars and the lift for the top floor obviously have to pay extra, but despite everything our visit was really impressive.

Unfortunately, we didn’t quite know which paths and corridors to complete in which order, but I think we explored everything important.

There are also some vending machines that are already good to use in the high temperatures.

The Colosseum is easy to reach by metro, right next to it you will find the "Forum Romanum" and after a short walk you will also see the remains of the "Circus Maximus" and from there the "Bocca della Verita" (mouth of truth) is not far away.

There is an entrance control, also take your passport with you!!

Comfortable shoes are highly recommended and pay attention to your training while climbing the stairs;-)
A short visit to Rome. Avoid the Easter period too much crowds and traffic
An exceptional place!. Beautiful monument! Must see! My gladiatorial soul has awakened, however the direction of the visit is poorly indicated which makes the visit less pleasant
For tea in my shoes. You have to have walking shoes. It's very nice.

Amazing!. The Colosseum is an amazing place and architectural treasure. The architectural ingenuity of the Colosseum is quite impressive to see. I had a great time learning about the history and touring the Colosseum in person. Pictures do not give it justice, you must tour this historic marvel and observe it for yourself. I highly recommend that you visit during the early morning to avoid the heat and crowds.

I purchased my tickets in advance on the official website before my trip. My combined Colosseum ticket included entrance to the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill as well. The Colosseum is best to visit on a cooler day and preferably during the spring or autumn months. Having advanced tickets and visiting on a cooler day makes for a pleasant experience here. I really enjoyed walking around the Colosseum.
Pretty cool place.. Pretty cool place. Slightly overhyped and crowded. There is no staff so anyone can do anything. We spent 3 hours here just chilling.
Arrive early. Beautiful inside and out. It gets really busy so get there at opening time. Forget middle of the day!

Piece of history. Enjoyed this and the history that goes with it. Very crowded as expected. We were a little disorganised in terms of getting a ticket and probably would have been better with the full experience. Just standing and looking and imagining it is vast and well worth the visit.
A special experience.. He needs 3 tickets to repair all of his premises
Carpe Diem!. If you love Ancient Rome you will love this. Takes 2 hours to walk around and take it all in. It’s a great starting point for a walk around the city.
too expensive to visit and wait. it's nice but borderline more interesting to see from the outside than to do 3 hours of queuing for the inside

A true icon. Visiting Colosseum is honestly one of the coolest parts of Rome. Wild to think you are standing in a place that is 2000 years old.

I would definitely recommend getting a guided tour if you can. It helps understand what you are actually looking at and makes it way more interesting. Try to go early before the crowds and the heat. :)

Save money at the colosseum in Rome. Don’t bother buying expensive tours. You can buy a ticket for Coloseum, Roman Forum and Palentine hill for Euro18 at the quiet ticket office at Parco Archeologica del Colosseo which opens at 8.30. The ticket office doesn’t sell arena tours. Can only purchase these online. However, when you get into the Colosseum you soon discover that most of the arena floor is removed to show the underground tunnels so the area you can access is tiny. Not worth the money tbh. There is also a very good free app that tells you all about the area. Just scan the QR code on arrival.
Rome, February 2026. The place is spectacular, but the staff's behavior is bad, especially Sara's, ID number 15406. This girl is not qualified to work with people.
Amazing. Only walked around the outside this visit but very impressed, busy at the weekend but still plenty of space. We were told to watch for pickpockets but felt very safe
I really liked it!. Really very impressive building or what's left of it.
Nice and to do with friends. Nice , guided tour , guide many kind and detailed, but unfortunately it rained and we could not do all the tour planned

Be a gladiator or a witness for a day.. Top attraction! I suggest you buy the full experience arena ticket for 24 € from the official site. Ministry of Culture. Access is possible exactly a week before you arrive. With this ticket you can visit the next day (i.e. 2 days of the Roman market and the Palatino) which is very useful when it rains the day you have closed for the Arena and the Colosseum. After the Arena, climb up to the squares.
Tour guide not needed. It was nice but short and overpaid, id suggest u hear about they story then go by yourself thats my review and suggestion.

A dip in ancient Rome. Fantastic Colosseum , a wonderful blast from past history , effective booking service. The only negative note: a very rude attendant, answered me in a very rude way when I politely asked her to repeat in Italian what she had said in English because I did not understand . Too bad! I believe that your work should always be done in the best possible way , especially when you are in contact with the public . In this case I feel that I have not been treated properly. His rude way of responding clashes with the place.
As I imagined and even better. First time I visited it, it was an excellent experience. It was as I imagined it and even better!!! Really worth a visit!!! The kids went mad.

I love it more each time i visit. I was here the weekend before Christmas so you can imagine the crowds, however great ambience and a few policemen with their sports car allowing for photos etc. You don’t have to go inside to appreciate it. For great photos of it, go up to the highest terrace at the Victor Emmanuel moment. On a clear

Night or at sunset. Oh my word!!! Loved it all. The forum is there too

Colosseum. What a place! Has to be seen. There are too many people but to be fair they do manage the entry well.

We did not opt for the colosseum floor but you could see it all anyway.

Was not prepared for the enormity of the place. Ticket included forum and Palatine hill but queues put us off. Maybe earlier than midday on a Saturday would be better!

Oustanding. We've been to the Colosseum before, so we weren't ready to pay 18€ each to revisit the monument. Frankly, it is a bit of a tourist trap - I understand they have maintenance costs to cover and the fabulous lighting at night to pay for, but 18€ is over the top. The Vatican Museums 'only' cost 25€ per person (without the audio guide).

It is outstanding nevertheless, and one can really appreciate it's past from just walking around its towering walls and peaking through the archways.

One negative: the 'hop-on-hop-off' busses don't stop there. You have to go on to Circo Massimo and walk bak some 1.500 meters
Coliseum in the summer. Beautiful place, but always very full in the summer. I missed more restaurants in the surroundings. The Coliseum is a small part of beautiful Rome, I am in love with the city!

First-Time Guide to the Colosseum in Rome. If you’re visiting Rome for the first time, the Colosseum is an absolute must-see. Located about a 20-minute walk from the main train station, it’s easily accessible for travelers arriving on the high-speed train from Florence.

Take your time to stroll around the entire exterior—doing a full 360° walk offers plenty of photo opportunities from different angles. To explore inside, it’s best to reserve tickets in advance. If you forget, don’t worry—official tour guides nearby often provide skip-the-line options.

As with any major tourist attraction, stay alert to pickpockets and keep your belongings secure, ideally positioned in front of you.

For bird enthusiasts, the Colosseum offers a unique bonus: look out for rose-ringed parakeets and, occasionally, monk parakeets nesting along the outer ring.

A visit here is highly recommended for history lovers, photographers, and even bird spotters alike.

It’s great history but rammed with people. Whilst the attraction is great historically it was dampened by the vast number of people you let in at once. You could hardly move about and get pictures, couldn’t get to the exhibition parts as it was flooded with people, didn’t even attempt the shop!!

A Must-Do in Rome. The Colosseum is a must-see. Be sure to book your ticket in advance — it also includes entry to Palatine Hill and the Roman Forum, but the timed entry is for the Colosseum itself. It’s an incredible place, though it can get pretty crowded. The area is huge, but after a while, even when you see it from different angles, the views start to feel kind of similar — it’s still the Colosseum!

Historic site, can't miss it. There a lot of people but we were able to see pretty much everything we wanted. It was a nice experience walking on these walls. Wear comfy shoes, there are a lot of steep stairs.
An unforgettable holiday in Rome. The place is very interesting and beautiful, don't regret to go there but when it's hot again it can be very hot, so don't go out in the sun.
Kind of a sobering place.. Kind of a sobering place considering all the suffering that happened here. We went off-season, and it was still very crowded.
Ticket sales not accessible. Improve ticket sales in the rest.perfect in everything.others aaa have more guides in Spanish.... Or other languages. Beautiful place. For its history and that

Great building. We ( 2 adults + 1 child 12 years) visited Rome.

Our son has had Latin in school for 2 years and of course wanted to finally see the buildings he knows from the Latin book in reality.

So we visited the Colosseum.

Due to the mass of people, we only visited the Colosseum from the outside and once walked around completely.

He was still very impressed and he really liked it.

Lunch time had no lines. Lots of walking and crowds for little kids to manage.

Arrived before opening and a long line for skip the line ! However at around d lunch time this line is next to nothing .

Great experience to see something so massive . Is is jaw dropping amazing ? Maybe not however fascinating to see how they built this

A roman marvel not to miss. Being one of the 7 wonders of the world, photos do not justify the size and scale of the place, it has to be witnessed in person.

after a brisk walk from the drop of point, as you enter the whole area of the colosseum looks marvelous with stoned pathways and some bits of greenary.

there are many sites where you can take photos of the colosseum, but getting inside would be a task of its own as people que up for long hours for the tickets to get in and during summer holidays it would be difficult to get.

the structure looks straight out of CGI movie, and a lovely place to check out in rome.

Timeless grandeur. Your front row seat to the Colosseum’s timeless grandeur, with sweeping views inside and out, each arch and stone whispering the legacy of ancient Rome. Feel the weight of history where gladiators once battled, surrounded by the energy of curious travelers exploring its vast ruins.

Beautiful and definitely worth seeing. Impressive.. In a word, that's the Coliseum.

It really gives you an insight into "ancient Rome".

Also the ride there by bike you see ancient Rome coming back more and more.

Like stepping into the past. Visiting the Colosseum is like stepping into the past! The size and history of the place are mind-blowing, and it’s easy to imagine the roar of ancient crowds and epic gladiator battles. The views from the top are absolutely worth it, giving you an incredible perspective of Rome.

However, it does get pretty crowded, especially during peak times, so be prepared to wait a bit. I’d recommend booking tickets in advance or going early to avoid the lines. Overall, it’s a must-see for history buffs and anyone wanting to feel the magic of Ancient Rome!

Must Seen Building. A must if you are in Rome. Truly breathtaking structure. You learn a lot about the history and the different eras. However, in summer incredibly crowded and overflowing. I booked it in a combination ticket with the forum and the platinum hill and was on the road for 5 hours. So definitely schedule a lot of time if you want to see everything in peace.

A monument that bears witness to Roman greatness.. Exceptional Roman monument, both for its impressive architecture and for its remarkable state of conservation. The tour is fascinating and allows you to measure the greatness of the Roman Empire. The only downside: inside, the absence of the original bleachers removes a little relief from the whole, but it does not detract from the beauty and historic importance of the place.

Cheap comes expensive!. Unforgettable attraction by historical context. Makes pictures and very good views of the city. What a tourist should know before going: Be careful when buying tickets online. This could ruin your walk. Tickets in general are not entrance tickets, but vouchers that need to be exchanged for tickets. And the place for exchange isn't always close. In my case, I stayed more than 20 minutes walking in a burning sun. So, it wasn’t worth buying a little cheaper ticket. You may lose your ticket by delay, have to buy another more expensive on site and catch a big queue.

Place of inexcusable visit with buts.. Death by success and I expected more.

The but... a lot of people and more.

I thought it was bigger.

This happens when something is mythologized and everyone wants to see it.

The place is "awesome", (you have to think it was done 2000 years ago) and by that time it was the most. That's just why you have to go see him.

Another thing: you have to pass a security bow... with how rolly it is.

Roman days. As every tourist is interested in seeing places where there was once a different life and its own rules. It's great that everything is in good condition and we can immerse ourselves in that era for a while.

Amazing experience. Stepping inside the Colosseum was a true delight, especially on a scorching summer day. The shade within the structure offered a welcome relief from the intense heat outside, making the experience all the more enjoyable.

Overall, the visit was memorable and well worth it. However, I would advise against spending extra on arena floor tickets. While it may sound exclusive, the arena itself doesn’t offer much more than what can already be seen from the upper levels. In fact, you get a better perspective of the entire structure from above, and the ground floor lacks any particularly unique features.

It’s also worth noting that much of the access to the tribunes (upper seating areas) was restricted during our visit, which limited the ability to fully explore those vantage points.

Despite these minor drawbacks, the Colosseum remains an awe-inspiring site, rich with history and atmosphere. A standard admission is more than sufficient to appreciate its grandeur.

Fantastic place but areas restricted. Ticketing not clear as you need certain tickets to go to the top. Basement not open. Makes it disappointing if you have visited previously. Last time was 10 years ago and now you have to pre book and queue, only takes. 20 minutes to get in. However it’s a magnificent place and a must see

Must-see and very busy.. Must-see in Rome. The building is spectacular. Tickets to buy in advance (on the right site off). Once on site, you have to be patient given the mass of visitors.

The ticket coupled with the forum is valid 24 hours. We had a visit for 10am at the colisee, and given the heat we did the forum the next morning (no ticket schedule for this part).

Very nice, but little controlled by security.. Very nice and interesting place, but you could keep it a little more like the original and you could also improve the security of the place. I don't want to go to the Colosseum to read all the names of those who have been engraved all over it.

A Must-See – Book in Advance!. An incredible monument, absolutely worth the visit! Be sure to buy your tickets in advance to avoid long queues. With a standard ticket, you can explore almost the entire Colosseum, except the underground tunnels beneath the arena floor.

We also got access to Domus Aurea with the same ticket, which was a great bonus. If you want a deeper experience, you can upgrade your ticket – just ask at the ticket office a few meters from the main entrance.

Arrive early to beat the crowds, especially in high season. The structure itself is breathtaking, and if you’re more than casually interested in history, consider booking a guided tour or using a mobile app guide.

We saw everything we hoped for, and would highly recommend a visit!
Following the Empire's Footsteps. Definitely one of the most iconic structures that man has ever built! The main attraction of Rome . The organization for visiting tourists leaves much to be desired.

Book direct, go very early or late in summer. We booked through the official website. Bots and scalpers get a lot of them so you have to book quick if you want any of the popular options (guided tour, underground or upstairs access etc). A batch of tickets are released 30 days before and the rest exactly 7 days out. So plan for this and book on the day your tickets are released.

We opted for the slightly less popular 24h general access tickets (with the SUPER pass add on for the Forum). We didn't feel we missed out too much not being able to go on the rebuilt arena floor section, you can still see it and you still get access to that level. The underground is probably very interesting but only with a guide. You can still see down into much of the excavations. We also saw a cat hunting in the underground area!

Note: All tickets are 'skip the line tickets' now because all are timed entry - there is still a line and a bit of a crush to get in. Don't bother lining up if it is more than 10-15 minutes before your entry time - we saw half the "line" getting turned back and told to come back closer to their time anyway. The line is mostly just for security and moves quickly.

I actually felt a bit sorry for people with a guide here (opposite for the Forum). Being able to move through at our own pace with a guide book and a bit of pre-reading was great and the crowds are so overwhelming that I wouldn't have wanted to be stuck in a group as well.

Most people seemed to just be dashing past the museum section but it was really interesting. Very good signage here and lots of interesting history of the place and its uses over time. Take the time to read and take in the info here.

We went early (first entry) which may have helped with the crowds a little. However if I was doing this again I would go very late, do the Forum and Palatine Hill in the morning, have lunch/a nap, then go back for the Colosseum in the late afternoon. This is because the Colosseum is mostly inside, while the Hill is large and outside. If you're not planning to do the Forum or not on the same day, then first thing is best because it is a little cooler.

Charm. The usual charm of the capital that the Colosseum fully represents.

A giant always ready to surprise you....

Unfortunately, too much tourism is always a bit stressful.

Wonder of the World has still got it. I had high hopes for this place and I was not disappointed. Lots to do and see. Unfortunately our app did not work so we didn't get the full experience and the company we used were really unhelpful. Having said all that - it was an amazing place and great to see one of the wonders of the ancient world. Modern world? It would still make my list.

Genuine history - but wow what a lot of people. Like much of Rome, you know you are walking in history when you enter the Colosseum.

I bought tickets through the official website and made it through a relatively short queue with no issues - you have to scan your ticket three times before you actually get in. Oh, and ignore any suggestion you can’t take backpacks, you can. You will need to put them through an x-ray machine, but you can take them in.

The lower floor (not the Arena floor) is pretty forgettable, but the second floor has all kinds of artefacts to look at before you get to gaze on the Arena itself. A genuine piece of ancient history, pretty amazing.

But, be prepared for a LOT of other people if you are going in peak season. Like much of Rome (Trevi Fountains, Spanish Steps, etc) it was swarming with people. One good thing about going inside is you get away from all the people trying to sell you bracelets, water, sun shades - if they try to be friendly, just smile and say “no thankyou” because (whatever they say to start with) they do expect money.

Would go again - but off-season!
Massed. A monument that everyone has to see .Impressive.The bad thing is massification maybe you make a limited capacity or you raise the price of admission.You have to look for a way to limit people

Good. The monument itself is amazing. However, the ticket staff are rude and miserable. I witnessed a gentlemen ask a staff member if he could buy tickets from the kiosk and she just said no then ignored him. She could have assisted him and advise on how he could get a ticket.

Stunning historical must see. We loved our visit to the colosseum as we are lovers of history

. As expected it was extremely busy.

PLEASE remember you need ID to enter. This can be a passport or driving licence.

We found it was full of people doing photoshoots on their phones. They would take up viewing spaces for an age to get the perfect shot. They would complain if anyone got in their pictures too. It was awful. We heard people that knew nothing about it and thought it was a football stadium. It shows that many people are heading there for the pictures and not for its historical significance which is a shame. It ruins the experience of those that are truly awe struck by this incredible building.

I understand that many people have their own agenda and reasons to visit which keeps the colosseum alive but it’s a shame that it’s become uncomfortable for those that are truly enchanted.

Beware of pickpockets and tricksters that are rife. Take water as a lot of the areas are not in shade and the heat with all of the visitors is immense.
Visit to the Coliseum. I really enjoyed training and imagining the events and events of the time. A place full of history, unmissable destination in Rome

Incredible colosseum, overwhelming crowds. The Colosseum stands as an absolutely incredible testament to engineering, with a remarkable degree of preservation over two millennia, allowing its rich history to be keenly felt. Unfortunately, the excessive number of tourists detracted so severely from the experience that I would not consider a return visit.

Nice palce. The moment you walk in, you can almost hear the echoes of the cheering crowds, the clash of swords, the sheer energy that once filled this grand amphitheater. It’s awe-inspiring to stand among these ancient stones and imagine the stories they’ve witnessed.

Worth visiting. It was very busy, book the timeslot early morning, once your inside its more enjoyable.

Surely worth a visit when visiting Rome.

A Wonder!. There's very little to say that hasn't already been said. My spouse and I found our visit to be enjoyable overall. The views of the interior from ground level were just okay; it was very crowded there, and we were limited to a very small area. The level above, however, was expansive and had spectacular views of the entire interior! We were too late to be able to purchase tickets which would have allowed us to climb to the next level and walk through the subterranean chambers, but there didn't appear to be anything we couldn't see from where we did go. I was there 50 years ago, and it was great to see how it compared to my memories. It was just as awesome.

Great place but you need a guide. We visited here but made the mistake of not having a guide. So as incredible as it was and breath taking we could have got so much more from our visit. Definitely get the guided tour we wish we had. Very very very busy and hard to navigate but still amazing

What to do if you're in Rome. Lots of control with CNI and gantry. Many people, despite the regularization by schedules. Waiting in line to take pictures. Grandiose from the outside, you have to imagine for the inside (without guide).

Must see of Rome. Must-see monument of the city of Rome.

Bcp of people, organization not in focus as in all major tourist places as soon as there is crowd.

A must see. This is one of those enormous achievements that just have to be seen to be believed. Altogether astounding. The atmosphere there is terrific

Amazing it's still standing!. Well if you come to Rome, you've got to see the Colosseum; so you need to plan ahead and prebook your tickets. It is one of those tourist attractions that is always heaving with people. We pre-booked which allowed us to jump the queues and go straight in through security at our allotted time.

When we visited there was quite a bit of scaffolding around the structure which funnelled everyone on to the one side near the entrance. Once inside it was quite difficult to pick up the route to follow which takes you through the museum exhibits. There is a lift for anyone with mobility issues.

There are viewing points around the building at various points and levels with opportunities for photos. It is amazing how big the arena is and how much has survived over the centuries, so it's well worth a visit.

It remained difficult to pick up the route again when leaving the building- rather like a maze, it seemed like there were signs for several exit points. This was not our favourite site in the city but as I said, you still have to visit while in Rome.
To do. Of course to do during a stay in Rome. Mythical and historical monument. There are many people but to visit anyway

Imposing architecture by the Romans. The Colosseum, the iconic highlight of Rome, really is overwhelming. The landmark absolutely impresses you the moment you step inside and take a look around you. As you look onto the arena, you almost are able to imagine the gladiators that used to fight in this very place. However, I recommend you to get yourself a guide, as it was significantly less interesting without it, you kind of were just there for the pictures. I did some research in advance, so I had background information, which enabled me to understand a lot of things. I recommend you to do that too, or just get guide. In conclusion, a really imposing piece of work done by the ancient Romans!

Keep your head cool and avoid scammers. When you walk to the Colosseum, you will be faced with a crowd of merchants and scammers, keep a cool head and walk forward purposefully. In front of the Colosseum on the left side there is a small hill where you can take nice pictures and there are not many people there.

Excellent tour. Loved Palintine hill and the forum especially apart from the steps. The Colosseum was spectacular, point down for being overcrowded, it was packed but to be expected really. A really worthwhile visit.
a story. Long line to buy tickets, very crowded inside, but it is always the Colosseum an open-air book of ancient history and architecture.

Ticked a box. Have wanted to see the Colosseum for ages and when we did, we felt a little disappointed. Don't get me wrong, the Colosseum is amazing but the magnificence is the outside. Inside was far too crowded, there was only one set of toilets, which had a bigger queue than the Colosseum entrance and a little cafe wouldn't of gone a miss! We brought our tickets from the official Parco archeologico del Colosseo website for €18 each which also grants you entrance to the Forum site opposite, so is really good value. Remember to take ID as they check it against the name on tickets.
