Anyhow when we were in the colosseum, the place is extraordinary, it just made me think of the movie Gladiator, the views were great and we got some good photo’s, it’s a good attraction.

To do especially out of season. Ticket booked on a ticket site with audio guide on the phone. It's better to skip the queue even if it was pretty quiet at the tills at the time. There must be a lot of people, but it is still acceptable in March.

The audio guide is not useful because too much info and not always related to the place insitu. It's better to watch and enjoy it.

The meaning of the visit is confused.

It is nevertheless a magnificent monument especially from the outside I actually find.

To access the forum and Mount Palatine you have to go out and go in front.

Walk round. This is a very busy visit, lots of people and queues to put up with.Lots of work going on to repair the damage due to the age of stone etc. Very uneven walking and steps up and down. Worth the visit but need plenty of time

The classic. Exciting. Just seeing from the outside the heart beats harder, but coming in... is amazing!!! However, if you want to visit the underground or the upper part, you have to book well in advance (we tried to buy a month earlier, in the winter - that is, in a calm period - and we only got the basic ride). It is understandable that the visit time is limited, but we only had twenty minutes to walk in our modality. And that spoiled a lot of the experience for me - I was anxious about the schedule... A compulsory stop in Rome.

Arena do Coliseu. I liked it but I thought it was better

And more imposing on the outside than on the inside maybe because I thought it was more like the movie Gladiator

Rome. The Vatican Museum was awesome. It takes at least two hours to see the exhibits satisfactorily. You have to get a ticket electronically to save the queue. The same applies to the Colosseum. It is worth visiting the Castle of Angels. Transportation is good.
Some place. Excellent as per usual. A bit annoyed at the queue times and the attic tour wasn’t advertised very well. Worth a look. The amount of people selling tack is very disappointing also.

Timing is important. We bought timed tickets for 8:30am for Colosseum and Forum (joint ticket cost about 18 Euros per person). Visited the Colosseum first. At 8:00 am the queues are already forming and the tour groups (and their leaders) starting to get into position, but the wait wasn't too long and we moved quite quickly.

There's more to see on the inside than we expected and maintenance and restoration works are ongoing. Very helpful info boards at various places. We used the lift (yes. there is one, but it is tucked away out of the main area and not generally available). There are lots of steps between the entrance level and the upper levels though, so be prepared.

By the time we came out the whole area was absolutely heaving and very busy (it had also got quite a bit warmer), so pick your time carefully.
Beautiful. A monument to see in his life.of the world the organization well.many people but it did not stop us from seeing everything

Beautiful memory. Must-see in Rome, too bad that the choice of language is limited to Italian and English. It would have been interesting if there had been some reenactment photos, to help imagine the forward

Relaxing. Great service, very happy to accommodate everything, down to booking trips etc. Hotel room comfortable, balcony views of the Colosseum amazing.

Just disappointed that the bar top balcony wasn't open for drinks. Weather was great, and ok that the pool was closed, but it would have been nice to sit up there and soak in the atmosphere. Husband not happy cos the beer wasn't on tap. His problem!
Wonderful site. Better plan to go. On site no ticket sales. And if we can scan signs to check in, when it's crowded, seats aren't guaranteed. Otherwise magnificent site, majestic!
Get ready to go back in time. Spectacular monument, magical atmosphere. I highly recommend to visit it, it's just a shame you couldn't move for the indescribable amount of people.

History that shakes. History that gets to shrink. I chose the visit without a guide because I had read a lot about the Coliseum and its history, the goal was on the last day of the trip, could not take long. Still had to visit Mount Palatino and Roman Forum

Essential. The Colosseum is one of the wonders of the world and a visit is essential. As we had visited it before, we do not want to enter. But when we arrived there was no line at all and we decided to go in.

It should be noted that there are no tickets to visit the Colosseum alone. It is mandatory to buy a ticket that allows entry to the Roman Forum and the Palatine. I don't think it is the ideal, but it is to earn some extra money. Many people like us who wanted to to go to the Colosseum only have to pay a higher price.

Once inside, there are no words to describe its grandeur. A monument built almost 2000 years ago. Unbelievable.

Essential view to learn a little about the history of Rome

Don't miss it
Buy online tickets and come early. Be prepared to climb large steps and be mesmerized by the age old colosseum and its wonders. Better to come early as Qs tend to get longer during the day.

Good.. Incredible historical monument

I liked it, it's a fantastic place with a lot of history

We caught some queues but it was pretty quick to get in.

Disappointed. The tour of the Coliseum was done with a guide for us.

I didn’t expect that. It is an impressive monument from the outside but I was disappointed from the inside.

Lots of people, unbreathable

Fabulous Coloseum - shame about the Guided Tours. The Coloseum itself is utterly fabulous. Totally magnicent. However…

Trying to book as individuals for any of the special bits, such as the underground tour or the floor of the area is far too hard. I was told by an official on our visit all the big tour guide companies snap the slots up first.

This brings me on to guided tours. There are way too many and the groups are often too big. It’s time to make changes to ensure the visiting experience is as good as it can be for everyone. Either make all entry by tour only, or have hours of the day when individuals can move around without struggling through endless guided tours.

Finally - toilet facilities are scarce so go at the bottom!

Disappointing, plan ahead but beautiful. Booked tickets on the official Collosum website which gave and allocated time slot to visit. You have to be in the queue to go in at the exact time otherwise you will be turned away. Our daughter is 18 and I didn't want to risk booking a free child ticket as you have to take your passport to go in. There is a discount ticket which is 2 euro for 18-25 year olds but it's just for EU citizens as the UK is no longer in the EU we got caught out here and were made to buy a full price adult ticket which they would not refund the 2 euros already spent. This resulted in a very disappointing day. This was our second time visiting and we really didn't enjoy our time this time around. We wanted to show our kids the history of the Roman empire but there is nothing to show as an example of what the place was like or what the Romans looked like. With modern technology and AI this could be easily done to help imagine what it was like. They had a screen which we stood and watched thinking it may spark something but it was a complete p\*\*\* take as it was just a video showing how they installed the lift!!! The place was crowded and there are lighting cables laying everywhere. The tickets included 24 hour access to the forum and palatine but you could only use this for one entry. Nothing to say how long it would take to see it all. We didn't have enough time to see all of it before our time slot at the Collosum. The forum is run down some of the signs are worn away so can't read them. The plastic wooden effect decking broken and not very good for disabled or people with pushchairs. I was very disappointed as this was the part of Rome that I really loved on our previous visit 20 years ago. We also walked around at night and the lights are really badly positioned and don't light up the areas properly with some things not lit up or lights shining in your eyes. Everything is gated and migrants trying to sell you tat everywhere. Rome although beautiful and lots of history is not what it was. It's dirty and I didn't feel safe especially at night.

Overpriced and extremely rude staff, apart from that, the Coliseum is very interesting.. Overpriced and extremely rude staff, apart from this, the coliseum is very interesting, although the ticket only give to be 30 minutes at the bottom and we no longer have access to the rest.

When it rains, it's not a pleasant place.. It is THE attraction of Rome. Only we were unlucky. It's been raining there the whole time. You can barely take pictures because everything is open so everything gets soaking wet. you have to go through security and can barely take off all the wet stuff with the cold and rain so everyone is in a hurry and security is hardly patient with you. Reservations were difficult because we wanted to enter the tunnels and tried all day but the tickets never became available. Beautiful building, worthless everything around it with that rain.

Poor quality of recorded English!. Amazing that the English in the audio guide is so bad that it is difficult to understand! It would have been so easy to let an English-speaking person do the recording. Stunning 2026!

Interesting but too many selfie gladiators!. Whilst it is really interesting… sadly it’s also stupidly busy and full of people taking ridiculous selfies/pouting/wedding shots/ selfie sticks etc. sadly it seems all about the individual rather than the actual ancient wonder! I’ve been before, and it is so much more busy now than it was. They also seem to charge for all the ‘extra’ bits as well which they never used to. Sadly I won’t be rushing back.

Family visit – mixed despite the beauty of the site. We visited the Coliseum with our family (2 adults and 2 children aged 11 and 8).

Booking made on a site we usually use, everything went very well on this side. The meeting point was clear and convenient, and our taxi dropped us off right in front. The explanations in French for how to proceed and where to go were very well done.

However, once they arrived in front of the Coliseum, it got a bit complicated. Despite the designated entrance on the ticket, inside you must mostly follow the movement of the crowd, with the impression that everyone goes to the same place. It’s not very intuitive.

We booked the “Full Experience” ticket including the arena floor, the 1st floor, the Palatine Mountain and the Roman Forum. But the lack of clear indications on the spot has disoriented us a bit.

The audio guide is good in French, which is good, but we found it a bit sketchy and not always very fluid, especially with children.

The site remains impressive and full of history, it is obviously an emblematic place to see. However, we found that there was a lack of presentation and support to help visitors better understand and fully enjoy the experience.

A nice visit despite everything, but which could be better organized and more immersive.

Palatine hill not to miss. I suggest to buy ticket in advance online, as soon as possible. Saves time and effort. Online it's possible to get a ticket exactly 1 month before. To get guided tour that's tricky online, as all to tours sell out fast. But, even with the 24h visit ticket it gives you an option to see the Arena at scheduled slot and Palatine Hill and Roman forum visit are included (18 euros) - you can visit Palatine Hill & Forum Romanum 24h before or after scheduled slot for Colosseum. One admission only. It's worth it, especially walking the hill and exploring it. If weather suits you it's wonderful thing. You can get audio guide as well ad it helps navigate place and history.

Had to buy tickets twice due to delay. It was my second time visiting the Colosseum.

Impotent construction from ancient times. There were fewer people than last time I believe it was because it’s winter time and a week day.

We had some issues with the tickets. We bought them ahead of the trip thinking we would be less burden to the ticketing staff and to avoid queuing on the day. However, we were delayed as I thought the tickets were booked to a different time. We went there as soon as possible and were told that the tickets were no longer valid (didn’t even scanned them) as they were time bound, but that we could visit the Forum if we liked, and that if we wanted to visit the Colosseum we needed to buy a new ticket. We bought new tickets so that my relative who was visiting Italy for the first time could visit the monument.

We wish to get our money back for the tickets we didn’t use. We were told to send an email out…let’s see what happens…

Disappointing. Poorly valued. Lack of information. Bof.... We went to Rome and the Colosseum in January 2026. Fortunately out of season although it remains very busy and it is necessary to book in advance. The basic ticket to access the first 2 levels is sufficient.

Overall the visit is average.

While it is a major monument in Rome and the world, the presentation is frankly not terrible...

The audio guide, from your phone is only in Italian and English! For the rest, you're on your own! I assume that European funds have not allowed translations into French, Spanish or German... And for the few panels of this and from there I think they date from the time of the foundation of the Coliseum....

You can find more info by visiting the coliseum of Nimes! And I found the one in El Jem in Tunisia more spectacular. So yes you have to have seen it but you quickly forget it

Don’t bother! Observe from outside!. I rarely write reviews but I felt compelled to write this one.

Don’t give the city of Rome your money to see the Colosseum. It just isn’t worth it. It’s overpriced, overcrowded, the staff are rude and there is no system when you’re inside. There are no signs so you walk round in a daze.

I love history and Rome is fantastic but you are simply unable to absorb this amazing structure properly due to the vast number of people they allow in. It’s all about MONEY and not about the history. It’s unsafe in there due to the crowds and it’s very clear that Rome doesn’t care about tourists nor do they care about preserving the history of this great city. It’s about profit. Make the most money off bleeding tourists dry, ram it full of people and leave them dazed and confused like cattle on their way to slaughter.

Believe me, you will get more from seeing the Colosseum from the outside than you will inside and you’ll also…definitely…retain your sanity.

And don’t even get me started on the Vatican!!! Awful place.

Site was good but not the management.. We found the colosseum itself interesting and impressive. It was easily to get to on public transport and the queuing and entry seemed to work well. However the booking system was very stressful as getting tickets was a mad scramble to get them into the online basket before it kept constantly changing the availability. Also the security was so rude and rushing everyone. The guard one side of the metal detector was saying "go, go, go" but then the other guard shouted at me because he stopped the person in front of me to search them. Also they didn't tell us the rules for the bag scanner till last second then yelled at us to be quick and they shoved our stuff through with such little care that my mothers property was damaged by being stuck in the mechanism and they didn't even stop the machine. Was a shame because the other staff seemed organised and helpful and professional. The toilets were not suitable for the number of visitors and the shop was way to squashed for the number of visitors also but was reasonably priced.
Colleseum rome. A tick on the bucket list. Overpriced entry ticket as it was basic. You had to pay extra to visit all. Expect to queue. There is only a restroom at the start and you go one way.

Beautiful, but expensive and far too many people. We wanted to see the Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine Hill, so we did. we booked well or in advance with get your guide. But the price was high, we also had a ticket for 8.30 in the morning, we got there well in time and then had to wait until 9.30 before we could enter. Okay, we've seen it, the history is very interesting, but there's nothing else to say about it. I would say: also visit Ostia Antica, also beautiful, but less crowded and cheaper.

Complicated visit. The outside is very beautiful as is the inside.

We have booked tickets online on the colosseum rome ticket website. Although digital is very convenient, we had a hard time figuring out what to do by following the instructions in the email. This indicated to find a certain entrance to a monument by geolocating on the map said building. As it made no sense and even in French we were asked to staff who confirmed that we had to go to the Arc Constantin.

Finally, we went to ask someone else who then indicated the entrance and the line. The problem then arose when showing the tickets. The two emails received in the box had no value. However, the real tickets were sent in spam (look for error).

We finally visited the Coliseum completely saturated with people at 10am. Written explanations are in English, Italian or Chinese only. We briefly went around and left for the Roman forum with the certainty that this visit had not brought anything to our stay.

Excellent sight, but far too overcrowded. The closseum is one of the top sights of Rome. regrettably, it has deteriorated over our last visits. The area id ovecrowded by myriads of fale and trash sellers. In addition, it would not hurt to clean up one of the city's top income sources from time to time. Inside,it is cattle time as myriads of tourists are hurled through the location. A pity.

Hot and crowded. Was a pain to get tickets on their website, even with timed entry the que was very long, and you need to carry your passport to enter. The structure itself is impressive, but it was so crowded that it is difficult to enjoy it at all. Agree with some of the others- taking it in from the outside was enough. Also, not a fan of carrying my passport around. Perhaps in Nov or Dec when it is not miserably hot the experience would have been more enjoyable.

Get a pic from outside that's enough!. Its over rated. Well I really doubt if it's still the same that it used to be. Too much structural changes. There are fake sites that have tie up with colosseum that sell entry tickets to arena at high prices but only email you the entry ticket . Always search for official site for every attraction in Rome. Daylight robbery I would say, allowing sich fake sites and fooling tourists. The attraction itself is not worth it.

Give the tour guide a miss and don't go whe too hot. An amazing peice of history and defiantly has the wow factor. But way o overcrowded to the point u were pushed and shoved just so people could get their photos. Limited toilets, 20minute queues. Didn't see anywhere to get refreshments inside the colosseum and it was horrendous with a tour guide in 32\* heat who had you standing directly in the sun for 10 minutes a time talking and could hardly understand due to broad accent. Outside on the actual grounds no refreshments at all apart from a water fountain. But if u go up stairs to main road there's a few cafes.. if u can get in !! No seating areas at all om the grounds but u can sit on cold marble like steps. For an attraction that rakes in over £1000000 a day they could do a little bit more to make it comfortable the public when it's scorching outside.

Must-see... but perfectible. Unavoidable but disappointing visit on some points

The Coliseum is still a once-in-a-lifetime visit, but the price of admission is really high for what it is. Inside, the shop is overpriced and spoils the charm of the place. Outside, you have to remain vigilant because there are many pickpockets. Finally, the explanations available in French are very limited, which is a shame for a site of this importance.

Colosseum in summer. Rome always worth a visit.

For this reason, of course, you must have seen the Colosseum.

Only in the summer do others want to see the 1000send of course. Seeeeeful full about it. You should plan a whole day for there to be a lot to see here with the excavations all around.

Tired. Ofcourse the coliseum is beyond impressive but the infrastructure around it is tired, ageing portacabins for toilets that have not changed since my last visit 20 years ago. 1.4 billion euro every year could make the experience more comfortable

Let's downsize.. The Colosseum: imperial glory by day, tourist shack by night.

18 euros to enter, plus another 10 to not die of heat without a bottle of water. Inside? Broken stones, faded captions and a perfect echo for visitors’ blasphemies.

Out? Squatter centurions, selfie stick sellers and hordes of tourists treated like walking ATMs.

Every now and then they even do the haute couture shows: because nothing says ‘respect for history’ like a red carpet on the remains of an arena where you killed yourself to entertain.

But rest assured: 'we are enhancing the heritage'. Yes, I do. With tickets, private events and zero dignity.

Something of a disappointment. Sure, the Coliseum is impressive but, it is very limited what you can see. I had bought a ticket to go into the arena area, which was a joke, you could only go in about twenty meters and really saw no more than those who paid without this attraction

Rome Trip. I visited Italy last september and don't get me wrong, i like history and vintage architecture, but i honestly don't find myself visiting things like this. It is the Colosseum, the one that everyone posts on Instagram when they go in Rome, but it's just not for me.
Must have seen more but also not. First of all. You must have seen it but not as spectacular as the Roman Forum. Entrance control fully exaggerated

Beautiful but Unorganized. It was a nice view but I thought the place was very unorganized and confusing to navigate around. Overall, it is a nice place to visit and learn about the history of the city.

Sight-seeing.. The Colosseum is undeniably a must-see when visiting Rome. The sheer scale and history of the structure are awe-inspiring, and it's incredible to stand in a place with so much ancient significance. The audio guide and signage provided some helpful context, making it easier to imagine what it was like during its prime.

That said, the experience was a bit hampered by the massive crowds and long lines—even with timed tickets. It felt a little rushed and hard to fully enjoy the atmosphere with so many people around. Also, parts of the site were closed off during our visit, which was disappointing.

Overall, it’s absolutely worth seeing for the history alone, but I’d recommend going early in the morning or booking a guided tour to make the most of it.
Amazing experience. Too much visitors was there when I visited that place. But sightings was amazing, great opportunity to observe ancient things

Ruined experience. It’s fascinating. The structure and design is fascinating to look at, the monument itself is spectacular when you re In front of it. However, our experience was ruined by a Chinese tourist who repetitively moved us out of view of her selfie. Extremely rude and pushed her way to the front and actually moved my husbands arm and pushed him back out of her view. She was there for ages trying to get the right shot. People taking selfies and people with selfie sticks ruin it for others as views become restricted. There should be a one way policy around the coloseum. Literally shoulder to shoulder and in the heat it is very uncomfortable. Toilet queues are long, limited spaces to sit and take a break. A lot of security to get through too which is a good thing but be prepared to queue. A lot of staff that just stand there, they could manage the crowds better. It took us about 1.5 hours to complete, you could spend all day there but it was just extremely busy and hot.

Terrible for babies and breastfeeding. Review with a baby.

The actual colosseum is fantastic. A wonder of architecture.

I visited with my baby (8months) and I just found the whole experience very difficult.

They suggest baby carriers rather than prams. Take your pram. It’s a nightmare in a carrier as there’s nowhere for babies to shelter from the sun whereas a pram would have the sun shade. It’s like going to a football match, shoulder to shoulder with people so I think a pram would be safer for baby.

The floors were smooth enough for any stroller and there are lifts and if you can find them separate baby/pregnant/disabled exits.

The staff were tired and unhelpful. The signage was crap.

Toilet queue was massive.

There are little to no benches.

The website advertises a place for breast feeding. There is no signage to this and when I asked they said it doesn’t exist anymore.

I know it’s an old structure but I feel the capacity of people entering is too much and you can’t really enjoy it because of how busy it is.

The official website is confusing too. I bought a ticket and didn’t get access to what I expected. I asked if I could pay the extra €4 or upgrade or buy a new one and was told no.

To do. We were a little disappointed that a lot of the ground floor is hidden due to work.

Nevertheless, you must discover this place which is a must.

The staff in charge of the entrances a little unpleasant

Run away if you don't like crowds!. I was there 37 years ago. I couldn't find the magic. It's crowded, crowded, crowded. There was an exhibition on Gobekli Tepe... nothing to do, weird, why the choice? I put 3/5 because it remains an impressive building but I wonder if it is worth going into...

A Bit Disappointed. We had a tour of Colosseum on our own, without a guide. But we booked our tickets via Tiqets days before our trip to Rome. It was a wise decision because honestly, if we were there without any prior preparation, we wouldn't know what to do, where to go, how to find the ticket office: it is that crowded, even in March!! However, Tiqets office was quite far from the Colosseum (nearby the Roman Forum entrance), so the walk was a bit exhausting and on top of it, though we bought the tickets with arena access, the day we were there, we are told arena access is not possible due to overbooking. We are still waiting the reimbursements for that part of the ticket.

Anyway, other that these 2 setbacks, entrance to Colosseum was easy thanks to Tiqets tickets: we didn't have to wait in the queue (entrances were time based anyway), security checks were pretty swift as well. But the Colosseum itself was a bit disappointing, especially having been to famous El-Jem in Tunisia where you can access all parts of the arena which makes the experience all the more unforgettable. In Colosseum all you get is the scuffle of masses.

So, in case you're not that much into the Colosseum, watching it from outside will also suffice. Palatine Hill and Roman Forum are definitely much more interesting.

Interesting - One and Done. We felt the Colosseum was more interesting from the outside, and possibly better preserved. We were on a tour, and the guide was helpful in describing how it might have been. March 2025 it was in various degrees of renovation, as is much of everything in Rome. The crowds were significant, even in early March. I enjoyed walking around it in the early morning and at night when the crowds were gone. It was interesting to learn the Romans removed the marble that, no doubt, made it all the more impressive. They used it for the Vatican. So there's that.... I'm glad we went but it's definitely a "one and done". We did go in the Gladiators Entrance which was interesting to see the perspective from the "floor". Since modern stadiums are so large, it didn't seem as imposing. But for that period of time, its quite impressive.

Colosseo ticketing scam is rife. Staff is enforcing exact name match with ID.. Got denied entry because my online ticket has my initials instead of my first names. The young man insisted that my exact full name should be present on the ticket, as it appears on my ID, regardless of the ticket validity, of my proof of payment, etc.

I went to the booth to buy another pair of tickets, talking to staff about this nonsense. They insisted that I buy new tickets, even if my valid ticket had a minor inconsistency on the full name part. Then, I was able to enter without the staff even looking at my names. It does strongly feel like the colosseo staff is aware of scamming/scalping websites leveraging the colosseo's popularity, and actively participate in that scam. They also know about the many online scalpers but don't bother taking them down or closing their APIs to them.

The site of fantastic. What engineers did 2000 years ago made me forget how present-day Rome residents are abusing their legacy.

Very degraded interior. There is no audio guide in French, nor anything described in French. You have to master English to learn things on the spot. The exterior is impressive. The interior is very degraded. There are no more bleachers. You have to look at the models to image the site before the damage. It remains a beautiful visit, for history, but not to learn more.

Colosseum rome. The colosseum this time round was nice but not as good as 5 years ago. The trust or government have cottoned on to its a big money earner and try to get you through as fast as possible. The staff are not as polite or intrested in any sort of help it seams they are only there for the pay cheque and your interrupting their conversation. You are now put on a one way system that directs you out out out as the staff say out. They have closed off a lot more of the areas that are not on the one way system I understand that in some areas they are doing workes and that's OK but in others areas it's now staff only where you use to be able to go and admire the engineering and beauty. And this one way system I keep harping on about with a young family half way round wanting to use the toilets we made our way down near the end and asked to use the toilet as you can't just walk to them only at the beginning is this posible. We were told go out the exit. I asked do we then go back in the entrance they said no just get out I did explain we had only done half the experience and just needed the loos and their reply was it's tought you have to go out. I took the family the wrong way round to get back to the begging being shouted at that I was not aloud I ignored this and continued your rude i get rude then at the beginning I asked if we could just exit as we wanted to leave meet with no you can't you have to go all the way back round the one way system. I'm not say don't go it's beautiful but plan you visit if you need the loos go back down the wrong way to the beginning and don't forget to get your tickets to the underground gladiators area and the plebian / plebs (poor person) view. You use to be able to get on to the stage but this is extra now like I said earlier they are now charging for every little extra. Take ear phones to do the talking points a member of staff i asked to hire where they use to do it just said no and pointed to a QR code. I will go again in the future but plan a bit or lot better and hopefully a lot of the building renovation will be done. Just a little note Palatine Hill a lot of the rooms with statues are ticketed so again plan before it's a long way back to the start to get these. So in a nutshell plan plan plan

Lost in Rome~. The historical sites (Colosseum, Roman Forum, Trevi Fountain...) are magnificent but the problem lies in the signage in Rome. Everything is announced at the last minute: you will find the exits before you have even started the visits, but indications of the direction of visit or precise places are almost non-existent (or so, but once you arrive in front of the monument having managed thanks to Maps...).

It is heartbreaking that such a beautiful and famous historic city has such deplorable signage...

Make Sure you have photo ID. We visited the Collosium recently. Please dont do what we did. Waited in the queue for entry tickets for 30mins got to the kiosk and we were asked for photo ID for entry.

I had my drivers licence with me but my partner never had any photo ID, so we were refused entry. Carry photo ID for entry guys.
Great location - absolutely worth seeing. Very nice but of course tourist magnet, therefore always very many people. So you have to bring time and patience.

Be careful where you buy your tickets!. The Colosseum is a must do visit - but be careful where you buy tickets

The Colosseum itself is awesome, as are the Forum and Palotine Hill. Be CAREFUL how you buy your tickets though. The basic €18 ticket, available from the official website, enables you to choose a time and skip the line. Don’t be fooled into thinking you’ll get something different by paying more (unless of course you have a guide, and I’m sure they are very good). I paid €30 to Rome Tickets which looks like an official site - for the €18 ticket and nothing more!

Difficult route due to the crowds. The place is unmissable of course. It’s beautiful but the route inside is very complicated when there are people and yet the tours are booked on time slots.

Overall the visit is not pleasant and therefore we move quickly and without pleasure.

Be very careful booking tickets online from official site, very rude staff. The Colosseum is without doubt one of the wonders of the world , incredible place but out visit was spoiled by ridiculous pettiness and very rude staff. I’d booked two Colosseum experience tickets online at a cost of 48 euro . The tickets were both in my name , a clear mistake as my wife and I were visiting. On presenting our tickets at the entrance a very rude arrogant member of staff said there was nothing that could be done and told us the buy another ticket .We went to ticket office and appealed to the ticket seller and again the girl was very rude , unhelpful and arrogant . Who ever runs the staff at the Colosseum take a look at your staff , on the main young , bored , unhelpful, surly and arrogant. Apparently you can change the name on the ticket but not less than 24 hours in advance, no good when you turn up on the day with your tickets , be very careful booking online

Squashed in. After touring Castel Sant Angelo the day prior, this place as a disappointment in terms of the overall experience.

Obviously it is tremendously busy and I couldn’t tell you how to make it less chaotic than it is, but be under no illusion, it is chaos. Be sure to book a ticket in advance and take your passports as these will be checked.

People are just totally crammed in - I get it, the place is huge and can fit plenty of people but it was just so unpleasant. I was glad for decent security screening (airport style).

The views from the upper level across the arena are good, excellent really, and there is full access up there, but again it is packed full. Tour group upon tour group shuffling about with headphones in.

I’m glad I’ve been and I’m glad my kids have seen it, it is worth doing but the experience wasn’t fun and I can’t foresee a circumstance that I would go back.

Tip - if you are also doing Forum and Palatine Hill, head down the street a little for access there. There’s still a queue but not as big as the one closer to Colosseo.

SUPER MASSED. It ran at the beginning of the 90s of the last century and my wife and I visited the Coliseum inside practically alone for a few hours and nowadays visiting Rome on 3/10/2024 you see the collapse of tourism that there is not to take a step and dirty Rome as never and we complain about (Barcelona ) for example we do not visit it is not worth it.

It's better to turn around and try to take some pictures if you can and not get stolen.

We are going wrong in all of Europe, we must put tickets quotas already for global tourism.

In Rome they already charge 200 euros per coach and next year of the Jubilee 500 euros minimum they will charge us.

Colosseum: true wonder. Coming to Rome and not visiting the Colosseum could be a shame. I decided to visit it (this is the fourth time I come to Rome). The three stars are attributed solely for the cultural-historical aspect, if not for that they would have been 0.

A real "carnaio", really too many people, all lined up with schedule schedules. Prices not exactly cheap. But then surcharge for every minimal variant (third level visit, entrance to Palatine exhibitions, etc.) If I had known earlier I would have done something else, which I would suggest..

Nice trip bad Award!. We had a tour of the Colosseum basement. Nice tour guide a little hard to understand. GetYourGuide took an unreasonable overprice!! The ticket cost us kr 1080 but can see it has only cost them 240 as the price should be. And it was exactly the same tour that could be purchased at the Colosseum. Years!

very expensive tourism in Rome. Tourist monuments are to be seen but the Awards do more than hurt especially with the family.... and if you do not take the options you cannot see everything... it is abused

Good tour to take - different guide or tour company (Carrani Tours). This tour was good to do, however the guide made it extremely long and drawn out. She had no sense of humor nor did she read her audience’s at all - long explanations when clearly the group was asking for something different (the entire group!). She barked orders and was impatient with questions. I wish I got her name, however she didn’t offer it during the tour (Asian woman, tour date 9/4/24 14:45 tour). She shared she’s been doing this a long time and it showed not in great ways.

Train your staff in customer service!. Extremely rude staff who would not let us in! We had no photo ID (but weren’t aware we needed it). We showed bank cards, boarding passes etc as ID but were merely waved away and they moved onto the next person without even letting us try to find a resolution. What on earth do you need photo ID for anyway? The tickets were on my phone, in my emails, in my name and I had other proof of who I am. Absolutely ridiculous after paying a lot of money for tickets! The staff need training in customer service! We eventually got in by calling home and having photos of our driving licences sent. The audio guide is abysmal and the signage and info in the colosseum is poor! It’s a must visit purely for the history but be prepared to educate yourself and don’t forget your photo ID as clearly the staff need to know you’re safe to enter! (Even those with a police record as long as the queue can get in with valid ID!! - what nonsense!!)

Like everything in Rome you have to pay. It is an amazing site definitely worth going. The only issue is the amount of people who bug you for tickets and won’t leave you alone and the ln there’s the price like everything in Rome you have to pay and it’s not cheap for a good visit to the colosseum your going to pay nearly €100 for a couple.

Attico access was completely amazing! Spectacular and stunning. Quiet and peaceful at the top. Purchasing tickets was very stres. We managed to get tickets to the Attico- upper levels of the Colosseum and honestly it was just amazing.

The scale from the top was remarkable. It was so quiet up there and we weren't rushed at all. The staff on the gate were great and despite our tickets being somewhat staggered times they allowed us entry together.

What an amazing experience!

The reason for two stars is due to the appalling booking system. We literally sat all day 30 days before trying to get tickets every 20 minutes with the site constantly crashing, then having no availability- thus the staggered timings.

Then I found tickets online from a secondary tour operator for over £100 each, so ticket touts are profiteering on the tickets due to a loophole where you can change names once. This should be banned by the provider as I assume bots are being used to buy tickets- which is why its virtually impossible to get them.

Saying that- if you can- its brilliant!

Experience doesn't match magnificent building. The Colosseum is the greatest building in the world in one of the greatest cities in the world. And yet this experience is so poor. Driven by profits and ramming as many people through as possible. The owners should invest in a proper experience, they badly let this down.

Work to do;

\- Metro station next door is horrible and dirty. A shambles for the most important station in Italy.

\- Colosseum booking process is a madness scroll of hundreds of websites, some of which are just scams. Thousands of confusing deals on tickets being advertised is just ridiculous.

\- Mad busy. I expected it to be busy of course, but the lack of direction is astonishing. Follow the crowds is the only real way to get round.

\- Totally lacking an immersive experience. Where is the education, where is the view of what this looked like when it was built. 1 TV showing a video on loop is not an immersive experience. Go to Ephesus Museum in Turkey and you will experience what the Colosseum should be like, at minimum.

I would of course go again. This place is incredible. But the modern experience is simply unacceptable.

Dishonest business practices. Didn’t realize my reservation meant that I had to go to the Colosseum first, so I walked around the Roman forum for a couple hours, then was not allowed into the Colosseum, unless I paid them another 18 euros. As they pointed out, it said on the reservation (in tiny letters) to go to the Colosseum first. Don’t understand why they don’t have a sign up when you enter telling you go to the Colosseum first, or the people checking you in inform you, perhaps to generate extra revenue, This scam soured me on the whole experience.

Majestic, but not for the handicapped.. On the Colosseum and the Roman Forum Palatine nothing else can be said that is majestic.

Unfortunately I cannot give the same Rating on how the “park” is run due to embarrassing discrimination with handicapped girl.

Too bad, it left a bitter taste in my mouth

poorly organised, mass tourism. far too many people even taking wire-cut tickets

no/little explanation beyond the mediocre audio guide

very disappointing

Check ticket carefully. My rating is for the ticket sales not the site, that is amazing and well worth the visit. However, we bought our tickets on the day at the forum entrance around 10.15, and were told our colleseum time was 12.15 but be there about half hour before. As I cannot walk great we did a little of the forum expecting to be able to go back afterwards but we couldn’t and this was not explained at all at the ticket booth😡 if we had known we would have walked to the other side and do the colleseum first as the forum is a good 2-3 hours to get round. We complained but to no avail, and had to pay again to get back in

Colosseum. Far too many people, in front of guided groups

You don't get to see much due to the many groups

Rude staff

Poor regulation to get out

On the whole disappointing

But still something to see

Take photo ID. I’ve wanted to visit the Colosseum from being a small child and couldn’t wait to visit. Having waited in the queue ( myself and my daughter had pre booked tickets) we showed them to two young men manning the entry to then be asked to provide ID. We didn’t have any on us as we hadn’t seen anything about bringing ID on the booking app. My daughter said she had proof of purchase on her banking app and one of the men said that would be ok, for my daughter to show him and him snigger at her and say “no” photo ID only !!! At this point people were walking by and looking at us which wasn’t nice as my daughter was upset. Luckily my husband was at home and could send us photo ID from home. The other man let us in straight away. This put a damper on our visit. The colosseum itself was fantastic but very crowded and the amenities weren’t good either.

Ruined by rude staff. I enjoyed my visit to the Roman Forum I felt underwhelmed with the Colosseum. I booked the first time slot.

However, My digital ticket did not say I had to bring my passport so I did get turned away at first.

I do believe the staff could handle it better rather than being extremely rude and abrupt. The staff member I went to first kept saying “passport “.

I then explained I could log into my bank to prove my identity. He said “yes show me” , he stood and watched as I logged into my banking to prove my name matched the ticket, he then did a chuckle and went “no”.

All this whilst people were watching me.

The second member of staff still wasn’t the nicest but explained it better to me, he allowed me to stand to the side and get a photo of any photo id I had. I eventually showed a picture of my drivers license.

The staff are really cold and unwelcoming unfortunately it put me in such an upset mood and tainted my experience as I felt I had been made a spectacle of.

Exterior (and night) beautiful, Overrated interior. From the outside, it is exceptional at night and beautiful by day. On the other hand, the interior is very disappointing, especially because of the crowds! Too many people! We're literally walking over each other, and yet they added slots to regulate... but no, still so many people, and it was late October. So, to see something, it’s complicated: you have to wait, you get pushed... Not very pleasant as a visit. Instead, I recommend taking the time to visit the Roman Forum and the Palatine Mountain, which are much more beautiful.

Absolute snorefest. This trip was incredibly underwhelming. When I was told that I was visiting the colosseum I was brimming with joy, yet the tour guide was so uninteresting that I could’ve fell asleep right then and there.

Unless you wish to see the building and the inside, I highly recommend avoiding this attraction.

An incredible experience…if not for the nasty rude staff. The Colosseum is something that needs to be seen in person, in order to fully appreciate.

Pretty incredible to witness this structure from the inside and outside. There is also plenty of information made available inside the experience for customers to fully appreciate the significance of this structure. A must-see in Rome.

===

While the Colosseum was an impressive feat (once you do get the tickets and come at the right time), the staff managing it was the exact opposite.

First of all, when we first arrived, it was raining heavily, and it was physically difficult to see where the ticket office was in the heavily pouring rain. Amongst the chaos, one of the staff members at the entrance shoved us into the pre-security screening area. Assuming we needed to get our belongings screened before proceeding to buy a ticket, we completed that step. Once in, we tried to buy tickets. To our surprise, we somehow passed the point where we could buy tickets, but before the point where they scanned tickets. When we asked nearby staff how to buy tickets, instead of helping direct us, they told us we should have already had tickets, and they suddenly became hostile towards us.

Once realising we may be in the wrong place (and not being able to buy the tickets online right away), we asked if we could exit so we could find the booth to buy the tickets. One staff let us out while simultaneously accusing us of forcing our way in and cheating them. When we tried to explain what happened, they just immediately cut us off, told us to get out and ignored us. I’ve never witnessed such incredibly rude and nasty staff in Europe. Especially given the fact that it was THEM who got us in that area in the first place, and we maintained being polite to them. The staff treat the customers like scum. They could not care less. These people give Italians the bad attitude reputation that they unfortunately carry. Shame on them.

Disappointment: without Audio Guide. Tickets purchased online with forum visit included but please note no Audio Guide in English and Italian only. It remains a magical place but no story to learn! Very disappointed!!!
Guide didn't show up. No guide, promised our money back minus the entrance fee, still waiting, able to go on our own. It is probably a good tour, just not for us.

Basic ticket no longer allows a meaningful view of the site. Unbelievably awful relative to how it used to be.

The basic ticket is now restricted to a route that actively prevents you from actually seeing the Colosseum!!!!

You used to be able to access the Colosseum floor level and appreciate the sheer scale of the stadium above you.

Now... you're bottled into a route that winds you up and around the exterior of the building, in narrow confines, being pushed and shoved through a route that has been designed to feel claustrophobic throughout and where you can't see any of the "exhibits" due to the over-crowding (most of them are filler anyway, much less impressive than the building you're being prevented from properly seeing).

At the very end of your route, you'll have a chance to fight a massive scrum of people for a chance to see the Colosseum how you used to be able to see it.

The "pay extra" tickets allow you to see what you used to get for standard price.

Essentially, elitists have taken over the building and created an experience that is far more pleasant for the rich and actively harmful to the poor. The whole thing is now money-grubbing and plutocratic by design, but I guess the conservators have captured some of the truth of what a visit to the Colosseum would have been like for the Roman plebs - crowded, uncomfortable, and you can't see Jack Sh1t.

Skip arena only tickets. When we were booking tickets only the visit arena timed tickets were left. Skip this! It’s 20 minutes of standing on a platform to look at the lower part of the Colosseum. It was mis described! Save your $20
Sunday walk in flames. Monument just to see it on the outside, inside is a scam. Its preservation is terrible. However it is a good place to walk quietly. Especially on a Sunday afternoon.

Packed to the hilt. Needs an overhaul for the visitor numbers .It is packed to the hilt. Needs proper shops selling drinks, ice lollies and gifts, instead it relies on men down on their luck selling water, fans and silk umbrellas. The money this attraction is making must be colossal so employ these men in the shops, more enjoyable for the tourist and you are helping these men that are down on their luck. Tragic this money making pit is not more charitable.

Chaos. An astounding piece of history.

Let's squeeze the tourist to the maximum in Rome. Like all of Rome, it is something incredible and beautiful. But the need to squeeze tourists economically overshadows it.

Great wonder, it's a shame she's Italian.. Very bad organization when entering. There are no tours in Spanish. Too many very funny African vendors, they don't even respect the person, they physically attacked me and the police don't even notice. The coliseum always under reconstruction. There are no signs and the native people are very rude to tourists no matter where you come from.
Meh.. Yes, there it is. So not amazing. One gets more from photographs. Entirely not worth the effort to schedule tickets, wait in line, and tour the thing.

It's not worth waiting in line.. It is more beautiful on the outside than on the inside, if you are not interested in hiring a guide to explain the history to you, it is not worth waiting in line for 3-4 hours. Because they only have 5 people selling tickets. Chaos really!! And if you have the Roma Pass you have to have booked months in advance otherwise you will have to wait in line 😅. The forum and palatines are a lot to walk around! You can also see it from the street and it is the same! And like everything in Rome full of people!

Book in advance or you might not get in..... I've given this only 2 stars as we actually didn't get to go in....the queue was about 3 hours long and when we got to the front they had sold out of tickets so we couldn't go in.

Book in advance and you'll be fine!!

Training of employees in dealing with disabled people is recommended!. Interesting sight. The necessary infrastructure for disabled and chronically ill people to visit is also available. The staff working at these facilities should be trained. When the relevant entrance ticket is shown, which has already been checked at the cash desk for eligibility, it is inappropriate to look up and down and back to see what is wrong with the person. There are also illnesses that are not immediately apparent to outsiders. This annoyed us throughout the entire visit. Instead of taking the elevator, we took the steep stairs.

Disorganised chaos.. Absolutely chaotic. Poor organisation and signage. Too many people allowed in at once and groups just cause even more delays. The bottom tier is extra but they don't make that clear at all when you buy a ticket. Staff unhelpful when you are not sure which was to go. I actually wouldn't recommend the stress.

Disappointed in new policy. We had this tour booked 11 months in advance for our group of 13 people with Perillo tours for today. We had the skip the line pass too. Last night at 7 pm we got a call from our travel agent that there’s a new policy in place where they no longer accept the skip the line or honor this as of June 1. So for everyone who has this already booked beware. We were unable to go into the colosseum now as we couldn’t wait the 2-3 hours online due to our whole day being booked with other tours. We were able to walk around the outside but honestly we were very disappointed. We really wanted to see the inside and were upset with the way this was handled. As for the colosseum it’s an incredible landmark, even just from the outside. The area around is very crowded and very dirty. It’s a shame this beautiful piece of history has so much trash surrounding it.

Tour Operator cancelled Tickets 3 days before Entry Date!. We booked entry tickets to the Colosseum to Include the Forum, Palatine Hill plus we added the Colosseum floor to our tour. The tour operator was RoamMad. Three days before our tour was to commence, the tour operator sends an email stating that he was unable to procure our entry tickets!!! It was very important to us that our son and 17 year old grandson visit the Colosseum. I then had to scramble to find replacement tickets. These tickets sell out quickly and tour operators buy up all the tickets, and charge premium prices for the entry tickets, even without a personal tour. I was able to only find 2 Colosseum entry tickets for just our son and grandson, no Colosseum floor entry tickets available. It is INCREDIBLY frustrating to think that we have our tickets and then to find out, the tour operator obviously either over sold the tickets they had, or decided to charger a higher price for our tickets. Do NOT book any tickets through tour operator RoamMad!
Don't use GetYourGuide. Do NOT use GetYourGuide! They canceled our very expensive colosseum tour at time of check in (we were early) and were completely overbooked which they told us. Use anyone else

Crowded and not very interesting. Disappointing experience. By far the most disappointing experience in Rome.

Okay, it's huge. Okay, it's impressive that nearly 2,000 years ago people managed to build something on this scale. But here's the problem: there isn't all that much left of it.

Rome is packed with historic buildings that are more beautiful, better preserved, and far less crowded, yet tourists seem determined to ignore them while flocking here as if the Colosseum were the only thing standing since antiquity.

However, if you're planning to visit, here are some extremely useful tips:

1\. Buy your tickets well in advance. We tried to buy ours more than a month before arriving in Rome, and the only tickets available were for Roma Pass holders. As a result, we spent €130 on two vouchers that we used only for the Colosseum and for five or six metro rides. In other words, we managed to turn public transportation into a luxury experience and wasted about €100.

2\. Be very careful where you buy your tickets. The ticket market around the Colosseum sometimes feels almost as competitive as the gladiatorial games once held inside it, therefore, starting with 2026, authorities have cracked down on countless intermediaries who were reselling tickets at inflated prices.

3\. Pay close attention to your entry time. This is not a mere suggestion. If you're late, you can say goodbye to your visit.

4\. Be careful with the combined ticket, which allows you to visit both the Colosseum and the Roman Forum. Many tourists do not organize their time well, the ticket expires and they are left to admire the Colosseum only from the outside.

5\. Read something beforehand or book a guided tour, otherwise, you'll spend most of your time looking at walls, arches, and more walls, trying to imagine where all the excitement is supposed to be.

6\. If you want to visit the entire building, you will need additional tickets, so if you buy your tickets online, be careful which options you choose.

7\. Bring water. If you don't, you'll end up buying it from street vendors nearby. Paying €5 for a small bottle of water is a wonderful way to experience ancient Roman engineering and modern Roman entrepreneurship at the same time.

8\. Speaking of street vendors, don't buy the garbage that they are selling as "power banks". If you think you'll need a portable charger, buy a proper one before your trip.

9\. Most importantly, keep an eye (and, even better, a hand) on your wallet. There are countless teams of professional pickpockets operating both inside the Colosseum and in the surrounding alleys. Even worse, the nearby metro station hosts one of the most efficient pickpocket crews I've ever encountered. I didn't take any photos, but a quick search on social media will show you plenty of examples of them at work.

Conclusion:

If you're a fan of Gladiator, you'll probably love the experience.

If you have plenty of time in Rome, it's certainly worth seeing once.

But if your interests lean more toward palaces, churches, museums, galleries, or simply exploring the city itself, and especially if your time is limited, you can admire the Colosseum from the outside, take a few photos, and move on. Despite its legendary status, you're not missing nearly as much as you might think.

Last Minute change. I booked a Colosseum tour for 8:30 AM, but one day before the visit I was informed that there were no more tickets available for that time. I was told to either choose another date or accept a 4:30 PM time slot.

Because of this last-minute change, I had to completely reorganize my plans in Rome and missed other attractions I had already planned to visit. Due to the inconvenience, the company promised me a partial refund/discount, which I never received.

Even though I was eventually able to visit the Colosseum, my experience with the logistics and customer service of this company was very disappointing. I do not consider this professional treatment toward customers.

Avoid Viator. The experience may have been fantastic but due to the incompetence of TripAdvisors own company Viator we missed our tour. Customer service is appalling and they refuse to refund meaning you're hundreds of pounds down

Booking via official website not possible. You'd like to say something to the Colosseum that requires you to get in at all. Booking through the Colosseum's official website is a disaster. We wanted to have the full ticket with everything in it for 28 euros. the tickets will be activated one week in advance, every 15 minutes. Within seconds, all tickets are gone (untrustworthy third-party vendors who work with technology) and even when trying to get remaining seats throughout the day end with error messages. If you think you support such dubious third parties there, you should punish by not going there.

Colosseum. Ticket price on site Viator arrival at the meeting time the ticket did not correspond to the same time our entry was scheduled half an hour before at 2:30 p.m. we had an entry for 3 p.m. nobody notified us of the change the queue is a long arrival at the entrance level we were refused saying too late very disappointed soon to a problem of information organization must improve

Beware of Headout. Its a SCAM!!!. If there were an option to give zero stars, that is exactly what Headout deserves.

I had meticulously planned a once-in-a-lifetime family vacation to Rome from California, something my family—especially my kids—had been eagerly looking forward to for months. Like most travelers, I did my research and found platforms like Headout, Viator, and others claiming to be authorized sellers for major attractions. Given how quickly tickets to iconic sites like the Colosseum and Pantheon sell out, I booked well in advance—over two months ahead—paying nearly $100 per person for the Colosseum guided tour and about $25 per person for the Pantheon.

Everything seemed legitimate. I received confirmation emails, created an account, downloaded their app, and had all tickets readily accessible. Headout’s instructions were clear about the meeting point and arrival time, and we followed them precisely, arriving early and checking in as instructed.

What happened next was beyond shocking.

We were bluntly told that our tickets had been cancelled—no explanation, no prior notice, no alternative arrangement. Just like that, our long-awaited Colosseum visit was gone. My children were heartbroken. What should have been a highlight of our trip turned into a complete disaster.

And it wasn’t just us.

There were multiple other families standing there—confused, frustrated, and equally devastated—who had also booked through Headout and were being told the exact same thing. This clearly wasn’t an isolated incident; it felt like a systemic failure or, worse, complete negligence.

Headout’s customer service was utterly useless. No accountability, no clear explanation, no urgency—just scripted apologies. They couldn’t even offer us an alternative slot for the Colosseum, not the same day, not the next day, not at all. Instead, they casually suggested a substitute tour of Palatine Hill—as if replacing one of the world’s most iconic landmarks with “some nearby ruins” was an acceptable solution. It was frankly insulting.

Unfortunately, the nightmare didn’t end there.

The same issue happened with our Pantheon tickets. Again, cancelled. Again, no explanation. Again, no resolution.

After wasting precious vacation time arguing with their support team, it became painfully clear that it was pointless. All they could offer was a refund—something they are obligated to do anyway. But a refund does not fix ruined plans, disappointed children, or lost experiences that cannot be recreated.

What price do you put on a once-in-a-lifetime family memory that was completely destroyed?

I strongly caution anyone considering Headout: book at your own risk. Having confirmation emails, app-based tickets, and advance bookings means absolutely nothing with them. We had everything in place, and still, we—and many others—were turned away.

Their customer service is cold, indifferent, and completely lacking in empathy. There is no ownership, no responsibility, and no genuine effort to make things right.

I am currently disputing the charges and seeking additional compensation, but the case is still open—and frankly, I am not hopeful.

Avoid Headout if you value your time, money, and peace of mind.

Very stro.... I was at the entrance of this place full of unkind staff

(not because the place was bad) but because of the disorganization of the staff that the ticket was at 1pm and they let us in 1:40pm but other than that, I had just entered and inadvertently I did not take off my multipurpose knife unaware I passed by the metal detectors and one of the staff very little of the trade told me to throw it or get out in a bad way, I bewildered by her behavior not open to listening and civilized speech I told her to be quiet and she replied with insults

The place was also nice but on the staff we are in a terrible place😭😱

DO NOT BOOK WITH GET YOUR GUIDE!!!. I booked my visit to the Colosseum 3 months in advance through the Get Your Guide platform. The day before the visit I receive an email telling me that the Colosseum is unable to issue my tickets. And so my visit is off. Tickets were no longer available at the official ticket office and through tour operators prices were skyrocketing. Get your guide ruined my holiday in Rome, it is not a professional and serious platform, it should have informed me well in advance, not the day before. Never again!

Don’t bother to go inside scam. Anyone that pays to go inside the colosseum is a fool it’s really not worth it, pick pockets, ticket scammers, rude staff, dirty, crowded, uninteresting, take a picture from the outside that’s the best part of the site and go straight to the gardens infront…

THE worst customer service. Do not assume you are getting in the Colosseum if you book tickets in advance.. I booked 2 tickets direct from the official website on 2nd Janiary 2025. I booked flights and hotels and was looking forward to our amazing trip to Rome, for my wife's birthday on 3rd April. I received a confirmation email at the time of booking, and was advised my tickets would be sent a couple of days before my visit.

Two days prior to the visit, the venue cancelled the tickets and blamed me for not completing my personal details page at the time of booking. This was a complete lie.

On contacting them, they confirmed I had booked the tickets correctly and that they had been fully paid for. They then told me there was no longer space on my allotted date/time and the error was theirs. They stated that there was no other available spaces that entire weekend and they could not offer me an alternative.

They provided no explanation.

How can that happen on an automated booking system? I have spent the best part of £3000 in flights and a hotel, for my trip to the Colosseum now to be cancelled 48 hours prior.

Their customer service has been disgusting. They have finally offered a refund, but that's not what I wanted. I have been denied a chance in a lifetime visit to this historic site by complete mismanagement and an awful company.

Just because you have booked and received confirmation, don't expect that to mean anything. Its a lottery.
Disappointment. I booked 2 tickets online but we were turned away because I made a mistake on our first names. No refund of the €36 I paid in CB. Outrageous. Tourist factory.

do not take tickets with HEADOUT. Beware of the HEADOUT scam not booked on site

the coliseum is superb......

ALREADY THE PACKAGE IS FREE FOR CHILDREN AND HEADOUT BILLS YOU

We book several months in advance a visit for the coliseum in Rome 2 days before it shifts us the time of the appointment from the morning to the afternoon. we are asked to arrive at 2 pm instead of 11 am but impossible for us because we have a plane l afternoon.

Despite many texts and email no reply. we show up at 11am and the local correspondent obviously has no ticket for us and confirms our refund. today Headout refuses to refund us because he told us we had to present you at 11am, something we did while their text says presented you at 1:45pm???

We also made the Vatican with Headout without any problems.

the problem comes when he decides to unilaterally change a ticket, you have no concrete answer to your request c is an AI who answers you and tells you that the error is on your part therefore no refund. there is no service reachable and no one answers

Horrible dirty DO NOT VISIT. Enough is enough with the Colosseum! This ancient relic is nothing more than a dirty, crumbling eyesore that’s been hanging on by a thread for far too long. It’s a massive safety hazard, falling apart piece by piece, and yet we keep pretending it’s some kind of sacred monument. Newsflash: it’s old, worn out, and frankly, it’s time to condemn it!

Why are we wasting resources trying to preserve a ruin that can barely stand? It’s a breeding ground for decay, with crumbling stones, graffiti, and years of neglect. Tourists come expecting grandeur but get dust, rubble, and disappointment. The Colosseum is a relic stuck in the past, dragging Rome down with it.

We need to face facts: this ancient structure is no longer fit for purpose. It should be demolished immediately to make way for a brand-new, state-of-the-art facility that meets modern standards of safety, accessibility, and technology. Imagine a venue that can host world-class events, concerts, and cultural experiences — something Rome can be proud of, not embarrassed by.

It’s time to stop romanticizing decay and start building the future. The Colosseum’s days are over. Let’s tear down the crumbling mess and rise up with a modern marvel that truly represents Rome’s greatness today.

Timo tikest. Watch out for the company tikest, who sell you tickets to the coliseum, for public transport, then these QR codes, no one knows them and blows your dough.

Don't fall like me

Disappearing Tickets. What an appalling ticket sales system.

First you need to bypass the rip-off tours to find the actual Colosseum, be up at the crack of dawn for when they release tickets and then find the right bit to buy tickets. On two consecutive days I’ve tried to buy tickets to visit the Colosseum with friends during our stay in Rome. We wanted to see the underground area as well as the arena and upper floors. Each day I was poised ready to click to buy the tickets but before I could click they disappeared. In a panic I bought what I believed was the right ticket to see all but the underground!

Basically I have wasted €72 euros as the no compassion ticket “unhelpful ticket service” are only interested in telling you about the terms and conditions! Maybe they are angling to start selling travel insurance, they nearly have as many clauses!

BE WARNED AND BE CAREFUL

Don't book an arena visit to the Colosseum. Do not book a visit to the Colosseum arena. What a hoax. For EIGHTEEN euros you can look around the paved part of the arena. That's it! No further. You look from above a bit into what lies below. At the entrance, your ticket is meticulously compared to your passport. Not an exact match and you have a problem with the bossy ladies. In short, disappointing, not worth it, far too expensive. Even if it is a combo ticket with forum, Palatine and Imperial forums. Another Colosseum ticket may be worth it, this one definitely NOT.

Never again. We booked and paid for the entrance but when we arrived we had to queue , which is a mileage thing despite having an entry time...Maximum disorganization

We abandoned before arriving at the entrance because we were soaking wet after an hour of queuing and passed the entrance time by 10 minutes.

REVIEW OF TICKET AGENCY - TIQUETS RE COLOSSEUM. Review of services offered by Tiqets, Booking Agent, Rome attractions -

It is not advisable to book attractions using this agency, it is just another overpriced agent unwilling to listen to reasoned information when a booking fails to work as planned. I was in area of the Colosseum earlier this week - assuming I can use the services of Tiqets to enter the venue, spaces were available for 3.15pm, booked the paces at 2.45pm with ample time to reach the entrance, not knowing I needed time to go to a particular area close to the Colosseum and get the pass for the venue, arrived at the venue at allotted time and was just humiliated by staff of the Colosseum as some kind of idiot - not really very good experience - this is not good for Tiqets or the venue - I guess who cares no shortage of tourist we will not miss upsetting one tourist? This was a big disappointment on my visit to Rome. Tiqets as a booking agent offers little compassion and is only interested in selling over priced venue tickets and should not be used on any occasion in any city/venue. The Colosseum as an attraction is spectacular but needs staff that can assist people, particularly seniors like myself who wish to visit this venue - it is not good to humiliate any person particularly at my age. Hope this helps those who may need assistance - BOOK DIRECTLY WITH VENUES OR THE OFFICIAL VENUE SITE. Michael Zurich Switzerland

Directions? Not existent. Little and poor information about the building and historical events.. According to the online bought tickets you should arrive one hour before your scheduled entry time. Once you get there they don't let you in. You must wait until the booked time comes up. No idea why they make you wait. During December the visitor numbers are very light but crowd control is enforced without reason. There aren't any benches for handicapped people to sit and rest!

Once you start your tour you will be caught in a chaotic signs and inefficient guiding system. The flow of visitors is not smartly guided, you will feel lost at times. No information available about what you will see. Minimal fractured information about the building and how events were staged. You will not see a reconstruction or model or pictures how visitors over 2000 years ago were seated, just as an example.

All in all very poor organisation, presentation of artifacts, and in general basic information. effective

What a scammer that ticket office. What a crook!!!! They warn of scammers on the streets but the official ticket Office of the Coliseum can also do something. I had given my wife's call sign and not the one from her passport. We had to buy a whole new ticket!!!! The fact that if you book a day in advance you can no longer adjust yourself did not interest them. Real bad guys. So beware: fill in the passport names!!!!

Do not book tours on MSC GRANDIOSA. I could not visit it, it was open day, I went with the excursion of MSC GRANDIOSE , I paid 133€ excursion to Rome, as soon as we arrived we stopped at the coliseum to go to the bathroom, after taking several photos I told the guide that I was going to the bathroom, when I left the excursion was no longer there, I called the phone. that I provide the guide, nothing did not take it, I asked a police car told me that he did not understand me, concluding 150€ taxi return to the port civitavetia without seeing anything, claim in the department of excursions sell smoke, no solution, conclusion 133+150 taxi without seeing anything, I do not recommend excursions from MSC

Worst Colosseum Ever. Now I rarely ever give terrible reviews. But this place was possibly the worst of all tourist attractions we have ever been to (and we’ve been to a lot).

Now this is somewhere I have wanted to go all my life, but just never got round to it… but on a road trip through Italy so had it on the list.

So… you have to book in advance. Be very aware of this. There will be places they can get you a ticket, but it’s up to 10x the entry cost. The Colosseum doesn’t care about this… is all about the money to them. (Disappointing as it’s the only place in Italy we found like this).

So unless you hit on or pay and extortionate amount you aren’t going to get the best tickets with the best views or access… we resigned ourselves to this. That’s ok.

But we managed to get tickets for 3.15 on a Sunday. We arrived on time to be ushered to another gate and entered at 3.20. Despite speaking with someone on gate, someone checking tickets, someone else checking tickets, someone on ticket desk and someone on security, no one mentioned they were closing soon.

Took a few photos and kids went to toilet and by the time we went to go upstairs (3.45) all gates and lifts were locked. Staff explained they were closing.

A number of rude, sarcastic staff claimed we arrived too late, until I showed them time stamped photos… then they just rolled eyes, walked off sniggering and staring as a group. We were not the only visitors shocked by the attitudes of staff, indeed it was a talking point by a number of people we spoke to as we left.

Tried to email their team and got a very nasty email as a reply, again lying about time we arrived. I said I had footage (of us waiting for a lift!) and they replied saying I was breaking law if I posted it!

Certainly a hostile and angry bunch of people there, don’t quite understand why as have never experienced anything similar like this at any other tourist attraction anywhere in Italy.

ENROMA.COM = SCAMMERS. NEFARIOUS Service! I booked a tour guide for the Colosseum of Rome, unfortunately I was cancelled at the last minute due to extreme weather conditions including also a civil alert with which consequently it affected that I could not make the guided tour since the flight in which the flight company relocated me, arrived ONE DAY LATE after the booked guide, the company Enroma.com has refused to postpone my visit, obviously has also refused a refund and also refused to make a report to claim my airline

TERRIBLE.. DO NOT BUY BOOKINGS AND TICKETS FOR THE COLISEUM FROM TIQETS.

This morning at 11 15 we had an appointment to visit the armed forum and Colosseum. Once I find the agent they inform me that my ticket has been cancelled. The ticket had been booked and paid 305 euros for seven people 10 days earlier online.

I complained immediately asking for reimbursement and asking that they immediately find me an alternative but the stable manager of the agency liquidated me saying that they had already started the request for reimbursement.

In Rome there is a lot to see and true but if you leave Savona and you make 7 hours drive to predict the Coliseum and spend that amount you want to go home satisfied.

Anyway, it's 7:31 p.m. and the refund hasn't arrived yet.

Do not rely on TIQETS. EXPERIENCE TO FORGET

.

Well paid and never seen the Colloseum. September 3rd I was in a big queue for the inspection of the colloseum; when it was my turn, the man from the inspection said; you are too early, I can't let you in, go do something else. In the afternoon, a little later than what was on my ticket, I was in line for the 2nd time, to which the man said; you are late, you should have reported at 1 pm exactly; in short, half a day gone and money gone.

Line of 3 hours. You have to improve the ticket office and the whole line of 3 hours under the sun before entering maybe putting harrowing and tiring shade areas especially if you have children or elderly people

Colosseum security is rude and hostile. I had a mini-wine bottle in my purse and they took it away (as they should altho I did not know it was ILLEGAL) but they started yelling at me and threatened to throw me out and call the police. I offered my bad for further inspection but they were not listening, they called the police. The police arrived and said everything was alright and we shall enter the premises but the security kept on yelling at us. It made our trip very unpleasant.

Bad experience at the Coliseum. Catch tourists in the furnace; you have to show up 15 min before the time indicated on your ticket so many tourists get turned away when they come early and have to wait on the ground where there is a little shade; really unworthy as conditions.

They treat you like cattle.

Inside you should better take a guide (avoid Viator because big scam!) otherwise you probably pass by the monument .....

Frankly I much preferred the palatine and the Roman forum right in front because quieter!
