I don’t recommend the Coliseum if you don’t like being drowned in the horde of tourists.....
Over crowded. It was a complete Zoo with thousands of people spoiling the experience. I now know what over tourism means. It is amazing but try and go off season!

Buy from Colosseum's website not here!. We were fooled by TripAdvisor when we bought a ticket. It said they had a ticket and I bought them for me and my girlfriend. When we were to pick up those tickets the day we were going to see, we were told that those tickets were sold several weeks ago. Buy in good time and buy from Colosseum's website.

Do NOT book with Viator. Colossuem itself was fantastic - but there is NO signage. No one to assist. No one to help. No one to tell you what lines to line up in. No one selling tickets. No admission point. No toilets outside. Nothing to do while lining up.

Our admissions meet up person from VIATOR was late and it was an absolute waste of time waiting for them. All they do was give you a QRcode - why can’t they just email it ???

Awful experience.. Never trust the people on the way out when my 8-year-old son had to go to the bathroom on the way out we were told to go to the bathroom outside because the one inside was so far away and we were told that with the tickets 🎟️ we could go back in. When we tried to get in again they told us no, that we were not. We were very little time inside, experience of grief. They don't have claim sheets. Quite a mess, we feel cheated.

Be on time or no entry!. Make sure to arrive at the time allocated. Ticket line was over 2 hours, stated time for entry 2pm. Unfortunately, having arrived in Rome that morning had not changed my watch to local time - they would not let me in - even as a solo visitor. Surely they could show some leeway!!!!

A hoax!. A complete fraud!!!!! Do not buy anything through this operator, it charges more than twice for tickets and for minors who when you get there do not pay!! They don't serve you tickets, you have to buy others and they don't give you back

Viator ( Dreamtours ) colosseum tour. Paid for a guided tour 6 Adults and 3 Kids and met agent for ticket's before tour and entrance to site .

Tickets where sent via WhatsApp and we were refused entry as ticket's where invalid

We rang guide and he apologised and said he will refund

The colosseme staff where very good and we rebooked with them

Travelling from Ireland and prepaid with Viator ( Dreamtours ) its adisgraceful that this happened

Do not use as very stressful

Lorcan Dublin Ireland

POOR MANAGEMENT. MY REVIEW IS PURELY ABOUT THE MANAGEMENT.

In Rome with the family, in the first days of January.

Unable to make the reservation online.

At 10 am in the pits you have to queue for more than 2 hours just to buy your ticket, which then does not allow you to enter immediately, but to have to queue for another kilometer.

Too bad because my daughter's wish was to visit the Colosseum from the inside, she had to be content to see it from the outside

Bad service ever!!!. If possible, I wouldn't give a single star! Worst service ever!

We booked tickets for the Colosseum through Viator (€179 for 3 adults!) and showed up at the announced redemption point 15 minutes before! The queue was so long that it took us more than 45 minutes to get to the only employee on site, who unfortunately had to tell us that our tickets were not there! Instead of dealing with the matter, we were literally left behind and other customers served; only after decisive inquiries on our part did she try to find a solution. At 11.15 we finally got a mail with the tickets and 1 minute later another one with the note our tickets had expired! I have to say that I especially Considering the price, have never experienced a worse service!

So the visit to the Colosseum never happened for us despite almost €180 and I will never book with Viator again! What a shame!

They are not responsible. 0 recommended unless confirmed tickets at checkout. We bought our tickets three days ago and on the day of booking they have been cancelled citing a system problem. We have not been given any solution except the refund of the money and have been wasted a full day of travel to Rome. Company nothing serious.

An exemplary tourist trap!. You have to buy a ticket to get in. I have no problem with that. But if you go even 5 minutes past the time written on your ticket, they won't let you in. In a city like Rome, where transportation and traffic are terrible, delays are inevitable. The staff is completely unhelpful and treats you like a dog. They want you to buy a ticket again. There's nothing this stupid! With the same ticket, you can enter the Palatine Hill or the Forum Romano at any time you want, but you can't enter the famous Colosseum! I've never seen or heard of such a ridiculous practice anywhere in the world! If we were to say let's buy a ticket again, there's an incredible queue there too. I don't think tourists are treated like dogs anywhere else in the world. Rome is already an exaggerated bubble. And every corner is full of tourist traps. And the most magnificent of these is the magnificent super uber Colosseum.

Tickets cancelled. Tickets were cancelled 30 minutes earlier, such a cheeky thing.

And had to queue for 3 hours, thanks for that.

..

Parco archeologico del Colosseo. Do not use' Parco archeologico del Colosseo' to purchase your tickets. Received the wrong date and time. The time/date was nowhere close to what I selected. Was not able to use tickets at Colosseum and my wife and I had to purchase new tickets. The company will not refund me now

Do not worth. Arrived one hour late from the entrance time, I had problems with traveling inside Rome, I understand my fault. The place was empty at that time, they could have allowed me to enter but denied.

Just walked 10 steps and someone was selling to me a tour for 80 euros per person and I could enter anytime in that special case.... my question is, why they can enter at any time if I pay such high amount?

The place is nice and bla bla bla, but there is that old tourism scam running in parallel and that is fine, a bunch of tourist every day, so why they would care with one family of 4 people?

Wonderous!. This was a sad experience for my spouse and I. The day we chose to visit this monument to the ancient "Roman" arena, it decided to rain cats and literal dogs! We had a hard time even getting out of the Hop on/off bus and had to buy a raincoat poncho to safeguard our belongings . That apart, we were unable to take proper pictures for memory either due to the incessant rain. A not so memorable outing barring the fact that we were in Rome, that wasn't built in a day, so maybe there is a next time for us....

date errors - no after service. Terrible service - we noticed a date error on the tickets before we went to Rome but was told we could not change the date - gave us several phone numbers to try to contact the local provider - none of the numbers worked - only an answer message and no one returned our calls so we lost the full cost of the tickets over £300 - all they are interested in is selling the tickets

do not recommend. signed up for 12pm tour. told to arrive 15min early at tourstation office, which is a 20min walk from colosseum. It took over 20min to check in. had to pay $1 for bathroom. given 2:15pm tour time and told to "go walk around". this changed our plans for the day as we were expecting 12pm tour time. would not recommend.

Amazing place, Terrible experience. My wife, child and myself purchased our tickets for this attraction weeks ago and we arrived around 7:45am for our 8:45am entrance time. We asked several different people about the queuing system but could not get a straight answer. we finally found the correct place to queue around 8:00am but when 8:15 came the place was an absolute circus with no organisation at all, when the staff finally arrived they decided that the thousands of people needed to move back 4 metres which then caused more chaos. This is hands down the worst managed mayhem to a beautiful attraction I have ever witnessed.

What made the day worse for me was when my wife. Who is physically disabled and cannot climb stairs without assistance asked to use the elevator, she was greeted with “sorry but this is only for disabled people” I am not sure if this is something which is seen across Rome but we have never been put in the position to explain ourselves before. Hidden disabilities are actually a thing. This has left a very sour taste in our mouths and I believe needs addressed and staff members better trained to handle situations like this.
In need of renovation. Really run down, building in a bad state of repair, air conditioning really poor. Good to see they cleared up the blood after the last show.

Staff very rude!. When we arrived, the staff machine wouldnt pick up my ticket bar code, so I was sent to talk to the staff at the number 10 window. That man was so unhelpful and rude, and let 2 Italian ladies go in front of me , even though I was queueing and waiting for me turn. It seems like they get angry at you, because you dont speak Italian.

Horrible experience, at the end we went in, but couldnt wait to get out, it ruined the visit to this beautiful place.

Staff here is racist. The staff who’s working here named Amenda I think. She’s super racist, so rude to Asian like me thought I couldn’t speak English. Yelling at me and asking who am I? ?? I mean I’m a tourist who paid for the ticket I deserved to experience everything like others. Who am I??? She’s acting like she’s the boss here…but she’s just a staff or tour guide. Honestly, as an architecture student I know more things about this building than her. She thought she’s superior😓

Don’t expect the workers to be friendly, nor compassionate.. I took my 83 year old mother and my 82 year old father for the first time from the U.S. today 5/26. I had bought our tickets online. We were so excited! We got through the line after waiting 30 minutes or so. The guy checked our tickets and let us through with a sticker. We walked a ways taking pictures. Then we got to another point, where they were checking tickets again. Unfortunately, it was my fault, but we came a day early. Our tickets were for the 27th. I said can we please just go today (since the place is an absolute zoo) plus we almost missed our time, since taxis aren't as easy to get as they say. The lady was so callous and cold. No come back tomorrow, or purchase new tickets! The line for new tickets would take at least a hour or more to stand in line and mom can’t do that! There were hundreds upon hundreds of people there. Two more elderly people wouldn’t make a difference, plus we had tickets, bought and paid for, but just for the next day! I’m not sure what kind of people turn away 2 people in their 80s?We left and saw some other sites. We will return tomorrow, but unfortunately my mother will have to stay behind, since she has health issues and can’t make the journey two days in a row, plus she has to conserve her energy for the Vatican! It just broke my heart that they are more concerned with making money and following the rules, then using their common sense and compassion to allow my mom to experience what she flew all the way from America to see! Shame on you!

Disappointing. I booked a tour with Get Your Guide but apparently I managed to book a tour without a guide and I also paid for a ticket for a 17 year old which apparently is free I was very disappointed as we had no one to explain the history and we ended up on a self tour the sights were amazing but lack of a tour guide was unforgivable this company must know that the reason you book through them is to get a tour with a guide. What was most surprising was we had been on a tour with them through the Vatican the day before which was excellent

Tripadvisor is terribly unreliable.. Tripadvisor is terrible. Never book through them.

You pay double the price. In addition, she canceled our reservation the night before.

The customer service was incredibly rude and lazy.

Respect the tourist. Nothing to say about the beauty of the Colosseum and the forums which deserve 10 stars.

The negative opinion is due to the behavior of the entrance staff.

Presumptuous and rude in general to the point of being offensive and rude if you allow yourself to negatively underline your behavior.

A similar situation has never happened.

The tourist must be respected
Dissatisfied. The tour as well as another tour through trip advisor was canceled at the last minute. When one travels as far as I did for these tours and then can’t get another one can ruin a trip.

Atrocious disappointment. Purchased skip-the-line ticket. When we arrived in front of the Colosseum, we couldn't enter because the tickets were sold out.

????

Like other families.

Fraud?

Awful people working gate with no understanding. Turned us away and then wouldn’t let us in. No care for customers.. Showed up. Told leave. Came back. Wasn’t let in. Staff not understanding. no excuse for this poor management.

Basically. Guy at gate said we didn’t have ticket. Sent us away to get tickets. Turns out we had them all along. Then came back and they wouldn’t let us in because our entrance window had passed even though they turned us away at the right time. Travel all the way to Italy and almost didn’t get to see this the colosseum. Gate man and woman had zero care in the world for us our situation. Offered no resolution.

Apparently current management is known to be terrible and is about to be replaced.

From my perspective this should have happened yesterday.

Had to pay a huge fee to do a tour because no other tickets available this busy week.

Quite unacceptable. Beware if you go. And don’t be late! People who work there do not care at all.

Hopefully new management is better.

Can’t be worse.

Too crowded and unorganized. Right now it seems that their business is in the guided tours (€41 pp), if you want the tickets buy them online (€18 pp), if you decide to buy them there it may take more than 2 hours to get it at the ticket office.

On the other hand, the organization is non-existent, everyone is sent to the same place despite there being other alternatives, so the crowds that form inside are shameful, since the recommended capacity is far exceeded.

If you want to enjoy the experience, it is best to go first thing in the morning.

The most beautiful and interesting thing is to see it from the outside.

SCAM DO NOT USE. AWFUL. do not use this website. It is a scam. We were denied entry at the colosseum even though we had paid for the all access ticket. AVOID AT ALL COSTS. I was left very upset. The ticket agents scanned my tickets and then denied my entry so they are claiming that the tickets were used even though we were not allowed in unless another ticket was bought.

Disrespect for tourists. I purchased a guided tour of the underground for myself, my wife and my ten year old son. I arrived at the meeting point on time and the employee who helped me left us waiting. I lost the group and I had to run after them. But the worst part was entering the Roman forum. Total disorganization in the queue, with groups accompanied by guides jumping the queue all the time. And the Coop employees are condescending about it. After at least 30 people passed by, I didn't accept another group and went in. The employee started yelling at me, and a security guard came and took me out, along with my crying son and my wife. They told me to go to the end of the line. I justified it, I apologized for the stress, but they continued to treat me badly. I bought the ticket on the official website and felt completely disrespected. About 4 employees treated me very badly, harshly and shouting, in front of my son and wife. The visit is interesting, but the company should be aware of this disrespect.
Waste of time. Like being cattle herded to market. Wish we’d just looked at it from the outside to be honest. There was not much to see inside and it was a faff getting in and out again.

Pick up place for tickets shut.. I booked a Skip the line Coleseum Roman Forum and Pallentine Hill entrance ticket.

We had to collect tickets from an address near the colleseum before our entrance time 10:00. We arrived early- the building was closed. We returned at 20 mins to our entrance time ( the place was about 10-15 mins walk away) still no one at the building. I then had to call the Ancient Roman Tours office at my expense on a foreign phone. Once I spoke to someone he texted my tickets through- not sure why they couldn’t have done this to start with and save us a journey.

Other than this our trip was fantastic. We got audio guides at €6 each and spent over 5 hours wandering around all 3 venues. It’s a pity it started in a stressful way .

A nameless brothel. Aaaah the Italian organization... a world... but a world... what's the point of making time slots if it's to be all on top of each other like sardines in a box that's too narrow.

A happy mess made up of screams and selfies, groups of 50 bewildered plebeians with no autonomy of their own blocking the corridors, stopping at the top of a staircase to take photos even if it meant blocking any exit… a mess… but a mess… we ended up leaving in order to return at another probably calmer period to savor this place which undoubtedly deserves a little more tranquility. When will we recruit intelligent personnel in Italy capable of understanding that making figures is good but making sure not to make each visit a permanent nightmare is better?

Very bad, I don't recommend it. Rude and lazy staff, 5 minutes late, they left us out with rudeness and insistence

P.s. we are a school group, I don't recommend going to Milan
crumbling and old.. Much ado about nothing. not even got a roof? when they finising it. needs a bit of maintenance, seems to be parts missing.

Unfriendly reception at the entrance. The tour of the Colosseum was

impressive, but the joy of it was significantly dampened when we entered: an extremely unfriendly guard at the entrance, a mafioso type, refused entry to all visitors and sent them back with harsh words, even though the tickets said that you should arrive 10 minutes early.

Waste of my visit to colloseum. We never visited the Colosseum as the ticket deliverer never turned up. We phoned the deliverer and never answered the call. So we missed once in a lifetime visit to the Colosseum took this up with Tripadviser and till today nobody came back to me

City of Wonders Rome Tour Cancelled at the Last Minute. Trip planners BEWARE. I booked a private tour of the Colosseum/Palatine Hill for my family of 7 back in March 2023 for close to $1000 with City of Wonders - 7 months before our trip. These companies make you pay in full at the time of booking. Last weekend was our scheduled tour and 10 minutes before it was to start, their customer service line in Ireland called my cell phone to cancel the tour!!! These companies are getting too large and really do not have contingency plans in place when they make mistakes. People like us travel from all over the world and make plans. Cancelling at the last minute ruins the trip and these companies really don't care. My recommendation is to hire a tour company that will give you your guides contact information from the very beginning so you stay in contact with them as your trip gets closer. With these large companies, you are just a number and not important. City of Wonders Promise is "Our team is dedicated to making sure every experience is a great one." Well that can't be farther from the truth. Thank you for ruining our Rome weekend.

Drifting area. I ask for more cleaning, more checks. For the rest he has a lot to tell. Let's take care of history. We have to take care of places like the Colosseum. It's filthy, dirty and they absolutely must take action.

GetYourGuide….avoid booking with this company!!!. Avoid booking through this company at all costs, unless you want to be disappointed!! I booked in advance tickets for the Colosseum tour for my dads birthday with my family, we flew to Rome specifically for this trip, only to be told a few hours before the tour that it had been cancelled, due to a “political event” which didn’t happen or interrupt any of the tours going on, despite us seeing groups from the same company going straight into the venue in front of us.

Customer service were rubbish and unhelpful, and gave us no alternative or option for us to attempt to turn round this already awful disappointing day. All I received was an email to say the tour had been cancelled with no actual reason. Shocking, you put faith into these companies (and pay extra for the service) to guarantee bookings, and to be told it has been cancelled with no actual reason is completely unacceptable, and has ruined our family trip to Rome!!!

No communication so it didn’t happen. I booked the tour on my phone the night before. The tour said it would pick up at your hotel. When I entered my hotel, it didn’t show up. I guess it wasn’t on their pick up site list. But they never gave a list. I tried to contact them via the “Contact Tour Operator” messaging on the Trip Advisor website to ask them where to meet a couple times before the tour time. I also called their contact phone number and left a voicemail message with my phone number. I went to the Colosseum in hopes that they would tell me where I could meet the group. They did not contact me at any point. I asked for a refund in the Contact Tour Operator messaging and still haven’t received communication from them. A list of pick up points or ANY communication would have been helpful.

Do not use Tiqets or Omnia if you visit Colosseum. Do not book with Tiqets or Omnia. They changed the time multiple times a day before my scheduled time and I never got to see the Colosseum or a refund. Several others in my tour had a similar experience. I'm sure the Colosseum is amazing from the inside, but the ticketing situation is a travesty.

150 Meter Line to Buy Tickets - READ REVIEW SO YOU KNOW HOW TO PLAN AHEAD. PLEASE READ SO YOU CAN PLAN AHEAD: Buy your tickets for the Colosseum ONLINE weeks before you arrive in Rome. Otherwise, you will stand outside in the rain, sleet, snow, sun, heat, wind and weather for HOURS and HOURS just to get a ticket to get inside. There is usually only 1 single person at the ticket office selling tickets. There are no covered areas to stand under and be out of the elements. We tried standing in line for about 1 hour with horrible heat and sun, until we finally just gave up. All we got were photos of the outsides. There are tour groups offering a way to skip the lines. Some are real and cost more than a regular ticket, but worth it. But some are scams so be careful. Keep your valuables in a safe at the hotel or leave at home. Hold on to everything and zippered pockets are the best for pants as they have more protection. Lots of bottled water being sold in the area and you will need it. One (1) Euro is the going price and that is very fair for cold water.

Beware of CoopCulture Official Coliseum Site.. I bought the tickets directly from the official ticket seller, called CoopCulture. The purchase went through my credit card and I received an authorization from unicredit but no confirming email. I sent in a request for tickets and help form 4 times. Never received a reply. I checked both email and spam folders constantly. I saw several complaints about this on other sites. We are going directly to the ticket office tomorrow an hour in advance of our entry time and hoping we get in. Buyer beware! (I had better luck with non-official vendors for the Parthenon visit this morning). CoopCulture—where is your phone help?

Viator - Avoid. We were booked at 4.30pm due to our train coming into Rome at 3pm. However, our train was late which had a knock on effect to getting to hotel and the meeting point of the tour.

We were on our way and the guide called me at 4.15pm which is the first contact I’d had. I explained we are running about 10 mins past the start time and she said it was ok but send my location so she could track me which I did. Messaged again at just before 4.30pm to advise we’re 4 mins away and then called when we arrived. No-one was there so I called the guide and she said they went and started. At no point did the guide say they needed to start before.

I understand the voucher says arrive 30 mins before start time but it’s not that obvious and I had to look a bit harder for it.

No refund is being issued, not even partial.

Worst service in the tourist sector. I don't understand what kind of employees who work in the tourism sector not only give wrong directions (my colleague and I had already bought the ticket but they sent us to the queue anyway and when they realized the mistake, they didn't even apologize ) and do not know how to speak English, this being the universal language, nor do they make the slightest effort to try to understand it.

A shame! I don't intend to go back.

Very poor experience and will never use them again. Booked, confirmed and paid for a sunset tour of the colosseum. The guide did not show at the appointment tour time and we were 35 minutes early. After a phone call and on line,chat with ‘Get your guide” I was told the tour was reschedule to 45 minutes earlier. I was sent supposedly sent an email which I never received nor did they attempt to reach me by phone. We obviously missed the tour and not only that they would not issued a refund because they said did not cancel in the required time even though they rescheduled the tour, ridiculous. Adding insult to injury it was then too late to buy non guided tour tickets. Do not trust them. Very poor customer service. I will be stopping payment on my credit card.

If you don't think the Roman Forum is a version of pizza, you don't need this tour guide. An audio guide would have been better.. German-speaking guided tour of the Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine for €55. Very superficial, little content, difficult to understand German, tour guide was very small and did not have the usual ability to recognize people, so she often disappeared in the crowd and we wasted some time trying to find the woman again. Rome was supposedly founded in 700 BC.

Extremely over crowded. Very over crowded. Skip the line tickets will still see you queuing for over an hour. Overly officious Colosseum staff. Avoid in busy season. Walk around the outside, take a few photos, then get out of there!

Disappointed in Italy. We set up our skip the line tickets to the colosseum a few months ahead of the trip. On the day of the reservation (3 hours before the reservation) I get a message that we were cancelled. There is nothing we can do now. The place is booked through our trip.

Building magnificent ( from outside. We visited on 2nd June - Republic Day.

Queued for hours to enter at 2pm only to be told ....even though advertised as FREE on a public holiday we still needed to queue at office for a FREE TICKET ! inorder to enter.

Staff unhelpful.

Signage poor.

Overrated Tourist Destination. Overrated. Too crowded. You can’t even take a decent photo because it’s like being inside a sardines can, could barely move and the lines were too long. I regretted coming here. The photos you see online are 1000x different in reality.

educative and amazing experience with our tour guide. This was my 2nd time visiting Le Louvre. I chose a different tour guide and the experience was amazing. It’s great to have a different perspective so our new teacher gave us the a great experience.

And after we had to tour inside Le Louvre on our own the pieces were a beautiful scenery to behold.
Giant. Even if you don’t spend hours in the museum, you can still see the insane dimensions of the house from the outside. The place in front of it with the pyramid is also impressive.

The Louvre: huge, beautiful, and totally worth it!. Just got back from the Louvre and my feet are killing me – but it was totally worth it! 😅 I went to Paris and visited this legendary museum, and it completely blew my mind. It's so massive that I got lost a few times (which is actually part of the fun).

The Mona Lisa is iconic, but don't rush past everything else to get to her! The Egyptian artifacts, the apartments of Napoleon, and the giant paintings are equally amazing. My advice? Go early in the morning, grab a map, and just enjoy getting lost in centuries of history. A solid 10/10 experience!
Visit to the Louvre.. A must-see in Paris! It is a mandatory stop in the city; it is large enough to explore in a single day, and the entire museum is quite interesting and beautiful.

A special visit. The museum is huge and needs a lot of time for those who like this kind of programming. It wasn’t my first time then, I just did what I hadn’t visited before. It’s worth a visit, but align your expectations with what you can see in a single visit with time running.
Must see!. Get timed-entry tickets! This museum is a must see!

Louvre museum. Booked a guided tour to Louvre. Time 1:30 The ecperience was great. Met with our guide outside by the arc and did not have to queue to get in. He explained a lot of things on the way to Mona Lisa. We knew 1 hour was not going to be enough to see everything. We continued on our own afterwards for another 4 hours and still did not manage to finish the while museum

Visiting the Louvre is like traveling through millennia of history and art. The rooms are grand, and each piece is remarkable.. The Louvre is a unique experience in itself. The collections are vast and impressive, from the Mona Lisa to the Egyptian antiquities. The architecture of the museum is as beautiful as the works it houses. One could spend entire days there without seeing everything. A must-visit if you are in Paris.
Louvre Tour with Anna. Anna our tour guide was awesome!!! Very knowledgeable and friendly. Clear instructions and informative with great stories. Highly recommend her.

Excellent.. I recommend booking online... there is always a line, even for those with online reservations... but it moves... we will return for the third time... each time there is not enough time to see everything.

Simply the largest museum in the world.. It is difficult to navigate within this enormous museum, even when one looks at the paper guide brochure. My interest was to reach the gallery that contains the Italian paintings, a place I was able to access after quickly walking through a series of rooms and hallways for 20 minutes. This is located on the first floor (second floor in strict terms), towards the Denon Wing (that is, on the south side, facing the Seine River).

The three paintings by Caravaggio are a marvel, especially the Death of the Virgin, which was originally created to be exhibited in the Church of Santa Maria della Scala in the Trastevere neighborhood of Rome, a plan that never materialized as it was considered in poor taste by those who commissioned it.

It is possible to buy tickets at the box office, but only if there is low attendance and depending on the available slots; however, in practice, the Louvre is usually at its capacity limit, so it is most advisable to buy online in advance.

The Louvre. An unforgettable immersion in the heart of History and Art!

Note: 5/5

Whether one is an art enthusiast or simply curious, the Louvre Museum remains an absolutely essential stop during a visit to Paris. The place itself, an ancient royal palace, is just as majestic as the collections it houses.

🏛️ What I loved:

The richness of the collections: From ancient Egypt to Renaissance paintings, the variety and beauty of the works are breathtaking. Seeing the Mona Lisa, the Venus de Milo, or The Raft of the Medusa "in person" provides a unique emotion.

The architecture: The striking contrast between the classical architecture of the palace and the modernity of Ieoh Ming Pei's glass Pyramid is a visual marvel, especially at sunset.

The organization: Despite the global influx, the museum is very well structured. The area maps and guiding apps greatly assist in navigating this artistic labyrinth.

Great even with children!. It is very convenient to purchase online, choosing the time slot and reducing the wait to enter. Even just walking through the halls looking at the masterpieces is a pleasure for both adults and children. We spent two hours there, and it was wonderful for introducing and approaching children to classical art.

Giant. Much bigger than we thought. The ideal is to take at least 4 hours to see the details calmly. It is important to give you a basic study, at least, about what to see inside, many works with many interesting and relevant meanings.

Unique. Museum that takes your breath away. Except for the Mona Lisa I thought was bigger.

I highly recommend. If you go to Paris you have to go.

the king of museums. in my opinion and not only one of the most beautiful and important museums in the world and absolutely the one with the most annual visitors. Inside you will find everything so if you want to visit in depth you need at least two days, or make the selection of what you want to see. useless to run through the corridors as many do not appreciate anything. I focused my attention on the works that interested me most and certainly the most important such as the Mona Lisa , love and psyche , the Venus de milo.

I also really enjoyed the jewelry room and the Italian art gallery.

I recommend everyone to buy the skip-the-line ticket online, so as to avoid very long queues at the ticket office and also security checks will be much faster.

only flaw that I would like to emphasize are the directions and paths not well signposted inside, already it is a huge place and it would be useful to everyone not to wander unnecessarily around the rooms.

choose what to see and enjoy it that is a place that gives real emotions, do not just go to take the famous selfie with the jolly

Morning at the Louvre Museum.. The museum is excellent, particularly suitable for painting and sculpture enthusiasts.

There are no queues at 9:30 a.m., but if you arrive later, it is advisable to prepare for an extended wait.

A single morning may not be enough to explore the entire collection, given the vast amount of works on display. It would be useful to improve the organization for viewing the Mona Lisa, as the crowding prevents an orderly flow of visitors.

Keep in mind that restaurants inside the Louvre have high prices.

More than a museum. Visiting the Louvre was an unforgettable experience every corner of the museum felt like stepping into history.

The atmosphere was truly magical, and the fashion exhibition was absolutely stunning. The combination of art, culture, and style created a perfect harmony.

I was impressed by the creativity and elegance of every piece on display.

A must-see for anyone who loves art and fashion!

A journey into history and art, not to be missed. A visit to the Louvre as a couple, a simply extraordinary experience. As Italians we focused on the Italian area, exciting and rich in masterpieces, and on the Egyptian one, fascinating and imposing. Huge but well-organized spaces, impeccably maintained works. Museum that takes time and curiosity, but pays off handsomely. A must, even for those who think they “don’t love museums”. Spoiler: Then change your mind.

The best museum in the world. This museum I've been there more than 15 times and I still haven't seen everything

This museum is exceptional even the best.

I don't think there will ever be a better museum in France but in front of the whole museum the Mona Lisa is nothing!

Whether Greek or Egyptian mythology this museum is incredible!

The biggest but especially the most beautiful!
Wonderful.. Really the most important and varied museum in the whole world. There is very little time to tour this great museum and see so many relics and works with so much history.

Best Louvre experience ever!!!. We rated this a five despite the fact that the Amazeum had a strike and we could not enter it for two hours and 15 minutes after our designated time. Our guide was fantastic. She never panicked or quit having a great attitude or spirit about her. Because we were meeting so very early 9:15 in the morning and it was snowing cold outside. We got to wait inside that was always the plan in a place that was just great so we didn’t really mind waiting at all and the miracle was when we got into the museum we were the very first people to go all the way through. It was like having a completely private experience with no other people in the museum for at least 45 minutes.
Beautiful Museum. the admission fee was worth it, stayed for about 2 hours and it was a peaceful art viewing

Louvre Lover!. This was my first visit to the Louvre and

WOW!! I seriously could have spent an entire weekend there; what a magnificent experience it was! Below are some tips that might be helpful if you are planning your first visit to the Louvre.

Before You Go:

-Portable Charger: Bring one and bring the charging cord! I was so happy I brought my Anker portable charger; between all the pics and videos I took, I definitely needed it halfway through my visit.

Having the charging cord was nice, because I was able to plug it into the portable charger and keep the charger in my crossbody, so I could use my phone without having to hold the charger as well.

-Shoes: Wear comfortable shoes, you will do lots of walking. The museum is huge!

Museum Tips:

-Date: Wednesday, January 28th

-Time Slot: 9am (purchased in advance)

-Wait Time: Arrived at 8:50am, waited in security line for 20 minutes. The line was pretty long; it went all the way down the corridor and around the building, but it moved pretty quickly considering how many people were waiting. They give you a 30 minute grace period; but just to be on the safe side, I’d recommend arriving at least 20-30 minutes before your time slot to give yourself enough time to get through security.

\*This was in January, so you will want to allow yourself extra time during peak season.

-Audio Guide: I recommend renting a headset. I was there solo, and it was a nice way to drown out noise and really learn about the art.

-Mona Lisa: If you want to see the Mona Lisa, get to the museum early and make that your first stop. The painting is located in the Denon Wing on Level 1 (Second Floor) in the Salle des États (Room 711).

-Time Spent: I spent about 7.5

hours at the Louvre and took a few 5-10 minute breaks. I was just so thrilled to be there; I wanted to experience as much as possible, so I had absolutely no desire to eat. l only stopped for restroom and water breaks. Speaking of which...

-Staying Hydrated: Food & Drinks are strictly prohibited in each of the three Wings where the art exhibits are located. I spent an average of 2.5 hours in each Wing, and I was not prepared to be as dehydrated as I was. It’s easy to get caught up in the excitement, but don’t forget to take a water break when needed.

I hope you have a wonderful visit! :)

A MUST SEE WHEN VISITING PARIS. My wife and I took a Louvre Express guided tour recently that was arranged by the concierge at our hotel. I had visited the Louvre once before in the 1970s and I was eager to visit once again. The guided tour, while not cheap, was a good way to gain entry to the museum without waiting in a long line. After the approximate 1 1/2 hour tour, we were then free to visit the entire museum on our own. This is a very large museum which contains thousands of works of art from many different eras. It is impossible to view every part of the museum in one day unless you only take a cursory interest. If you are very interested in art, you will want to allot more than one day for your visit. I highly recommend a visit to this museum when visiting Paris.
Such an magical experience!. It was our dream to visit the Louvre one day, and it was such an amazing landmark of Paris!

Beautiful and impressive. For the building alone, the louvre is an absolute must-see! But what a collection! Wow! You can walk around and get lost in this for days, but it will help you most if you prepare your visit well. We asked Chatgpt for a route along the works we wanted to see and that went fine 👌
Louvre experience. Huge and a bit overwhelming, but amazing to see in real life. The Mona Lisa crowd was crazy, but the art and history inside are definitely worth it.

The Louvre Museum, ultimate experience👌. Excellent museum. It is huge, so please, mentally prepare for it. It'll take you at least 3 hours to go around with slow pace. Many say you need 2-3 days to absorb and view all the art. Waiting lines are not big due to slotted times. It is easy to orientate once you get the map. It takes few minutes though, to figure the map out. Some areas are intermittently closed, so if you have specific art to admire, please plan accordingly.
AH, LOUVRE. Beautiful museum, I felt truly transported by the art. I absolutely want to return; we had a great time!

Cultural treasures that leave one speechless. Describing the Louvre is almost impossible due to the variety of exhibited objects and departments.

We booked the first time slot in the morning and had the least crowd of visitors. I suspect that most people only come to take a quick selfie with the Mona Lisa and then leave the building. Otherwise, I cannot explain why, despite the multitude of treasures, the Louvre was not overcrowded, even though we were there on a weekend.

In addition to the exhibits, I am always impressed by the buildings themselves. Unfortunately, we only had a few hours this time; one should actually spend a few days here.

I also enjoyed the extensive culinary offerings and the many seating options in front of the exhibits.

Magnificent. Nobody needs to tell you that this is a magnificent museum, and one of the most popular in the world. The collection is incredible and breathtaking. Naturally, it will be busy, and preparation is crucial.

I booked the tickets well in advance, and the queue system worked well. I am not sure how likely is to get in there without booking online during peak times. I did see people in the queue for those without the tickets, but it was early in the morning.

Once inside, it does get really busy. It all depends on how you consume your art. If you are going to study most pieces in detail, one day is realistically not enough, and you could find it irritating and perhaps impossible to do. Booking a guide might be a good idea. If you were going to have a walk inside and embrace it for what it is, it would be an easier option to do. Highly recommended that you book the earliest tickets possible.
Incredible place!. I just loved it. Safe location, close to station Palais Royal - Musée du Louvre

Highly Recommended. I was primarily visiting to see the Mona Lisa but was kinda surprised that I was more captivated by other paintings. I was surprised I recognised some of the other paintings. This place really exceeded my expectations.

I believe there is not much to say: it's the Louvre after all!. I believe there is not much to say: it's the Louvre after all! Tip: on the first Friday of the month, admission is free. Secure tickets online in advance.

An absolutely magnificent museum!. The Louvre is simply impressive, both in its size and in the richness of its collections. Each room offers a discovery of famous paintings, majestic sculptures and fascinating historical objects. Seeing iconic works like the Mona Lisa or the Venus de Milo in real life is a unique experience.

The architecture of the museum is magnificent, with the contrast between the historic palace and the modern pyramid that gives the place a lot of charm. Even if it is crowded, the organization remains correct and the visit is well worth the visit.

Set aside time and good shoes, because there is so much to see! A must visit to Paris, which well deserves its 5 stars.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Wonder. We stayed at the Louvree after making the reservation several days before. To avoid the queues at the main entrance, we went early to the entrance below the Pyramid in the shopping centre. You must already know what you are going to see. The selfie in front of the painting of the Mona Lisa is the main focus but there are the real and painted apartments of different eras such as "The Wedding of Cana" by Paolo Veronese, in front of the Monnalisa, the Venus de Milo, the Nike of Samothrace, the Love and Psyche of Canova, the Virgin of the Rocks by Leonardo, the Liberty that leads the people of Delacroix, The Raft of the Medusa by Géricault, the Coronation of Napoleon by David. The history of art.

Guided tour a MUST. It's a must do while in Paris. DEFINITLY get the guided tour. Makes it easy to get in and the guide will be full of information! We stayed after the guided tour and explored by ourselves.

A Masterpiece. What can you say about the Louvre that hasn't been said before....I'll try to keep this review full of useful stuff

1) get a ticket in advance and be prepared to queue to get in

2) Take a brolly or a sunhat - you will be queuing for a long time in the open air

3) although we saw people in wheelchairs.....lifts were difficult to locate and there are a lot of steps....a lot of steps.....

4) you can buy drinks and food inside quite easily

5) whatever you do get a map and make a plan...unless you are an athlete and are wanting to spend 7+ hours there you won't even come close to seeing everything. We didn't have a map and got lost in ancient Mesopatamia

6) if you are going to see Mona Lisa then don't expect an intimate viewing..... sorry

7) There are hundreds of masterpieces that people were walking past in their single mindedness to see the Mona Lisa.....in the Denon Wing make sure you look out for other Da Vincis!!!

Louvre Museum ... not just the Mona Lisa. Reservation required, good access timing, once inside... a delirium. You have to go knowing what you want to see.

Remember the Louvre is not just the Mona Lisa... or rather the selfie in front of the painting of the Mona Lisa (how to destroy art).

There are the royal apartments, precious artifacts and paintings from different eras and of splendid workmanship such as "The Wedding of Cana" by Paolo Veronese, of 1563 a 70 m2 canvas that then look and yet is there in front of the Monnalisa!!!!

Or even the "Death of the Virgin" by Caravaggio painted in the early 1600s, room 712
Practical advice. Reservations required. Find out which rooms are closed and decide from the start what your visiting priorities are.

We skipped Mona Lisa for other collections. Ticket reservation in advance is strongly recommended! We were lucky to enter Louvre Museum via the underground entrance with shorter line and less waiting time than the Pyramid one. The museum is super huge with thousands of exhibitions. We spent 4 hours there and could see the collections of Ancient Egypt, Greek and Roman, the Venus of Milo. The line at Mona Lisa was insanely crowded and we decided to skip it! There are many more interesting collections and objects at Louvre to explore I believe!

Overwhelming. The best time to go is when it is less crowded after five o'clock when most are eating dinner. This is a massive museum and you simply cannot see everything. But of course, the must see is the Mona Lisa. They have an excellent collection of antiquities orientales.

Beautiful building & fabulous museum. Recommend buying tickets in advance, entry times clearly displayed on ticket displayed at queue points. Staff check tickets in line. Airport queuing system that moves smoothly once your time slot opens. Very busy in entrance, queues at toilets move quickly & there are other toilets throughout museum. Cafes are at entrance area & prices are as expected in a major tourist attraction. Plenty to see, building is beautiful in its own way & with addition of all the museum displays it’s a full day with lots to see. Very busy at Mona Lisa when we visited & it was a push & shove to get to the front. Not as well organised as other exhibits & uncomfortable to experience. Sure there is a better fairer way for all ages to get a chance to see this exhibit. You can stay all day & go from floor to floor It’s worth a full day’s visit.
Amazing!. I recommend the family visit very suitable, our children 8 and 12 years old loved it! Super passionate Franco-Italian guide who extended the tour for our greatest pleasure!
A lot to experience. Now one of my favourite museums. The building itself is exquisite

Fantastic help. We had ordered tickets through the official site , which is a must as there are many sites that are not good .

( price difference etc. ) Also useful with avoiding queues and there is also often a time slot.

One of us is disabled , and with his card the entrance was free as was an accompanying person.

We were helped fantastically, and what a great service there, all compliments!

We also have deep respect for all the staff there with that huge flow of tourists.

Recommended

A must-see visit. Very nice visit to the Louvre.

The place is impressive, both for its architecture and for the exceptional richness of its collections. The historical dimension of the museum, housed in a former royal palace transformed into a museum after the French Revolution, is immediately felt.

Little anecdote: before becoming a museum, the Louvre was originally a fortress built in the 12th century under Philippe Auguste to protect Paris, you can still see its remains in the basements.

The works are numerous and varied (paintings, sculptures, antiques), with emblematic pieces such as the Mona Lisa or the Venus de Milo. The routes are well organized, although crowds can sometimes make the visit more dense.

Allow about 3-4 hours for a first visit targeting a few major works. To see the entire museum in full, it would take several days, usually between 3 and 5 days.

In short: a must-see visit, both cultural, historical and impressive.
Super happy. An icon place of Paris, it is impressive and the energy you feel being here is overwhelming and recharges good things

30,000 Masterpieces and One Emperor’s Fancy Apartment. The Louvre is the iconic museum of Paris, and a must-see if you love art, history, and culture. Yes, because of the name recognition, it's going to be busy. So, buy your tickets (well) in advance, and I recommend going first thing in the morning. The museum staff do their best to control the crowds and to keep the lines moving efficiently.

There are (quite) a few must-see pieces at the museum, so, try to plan your trip ahead of time so you're not just wandering aimlessly. I don't think that you realize the actual size of the Louvre until you're actually inside. I mean, they say that there are over 30,000 work spanning 9000 years of history, and they're not lying. Even if you spend three days, I think that you might not see everything in the museum. The crowds are intense, but everyone seemed respectful of each other, so, we didn't really have a problem.

When you're there, I suggest visiting apartments of Napoléon III. The Louvre was originally intended to be a fortified palace and he made it his personal residence. These have been kept as they were, and the grandeur is amazing.

This is worth a second visit, but, perhaps on your next trip to Paris.

Great experience but really busy. I am a wheelchair user and I was really impressed that they offered a disability discount, companion free ticket. I didn't realize how big it was but ended up going twice. My daughter wanted to see the Mona Lisa but the queues were ridiculous. As soon as the staff seen me on the mobility scooter they took me & my daughter to the front of the queue. Towards the end of the day my mobility scooter unfortunately ran out of charge, security very kindly let me charge it enough for me to get out and call an uber. Had a really good experience

A Memorable Experience. What an amazing place! My 18-year-old grandson and I were in awe. Highly recommended. I had booked tickets online via the Louvre official website. We only queued for around 10 minutes. The non-ticket holders queue was long, but seemed to be moving fairly quickly. Staff were pleasant and helpful.

Top tip for seeing things without crowds – go early!. OK, let’s be honest. Everyone knows what this is all about and has read tons of reviews… So I’ll give you some tips on what we did.

We had a time ticket for 9 AM, we’re in line by 8 AM and had about 100 people in front of us… that said when the museum did open, we were in within about five minutes.

Headed straight towards Mona and had an unobstructed view for at least 10 minutes.

After that, we started to hit our other top spots… The museum felt quite roomy and uncrowded until approximately 11:30 AM.

Top areas for us to see where Monet paintings, Marley, horses, Napoleon, Apartments, and a few other random pieces. Other things along the way were considered bonus and we were done all of our top pics by noon. By that point, it was insanely busy.

Would recommend the Early ticket and lining up early as well as having an organized list of what you want to see. Keep in mind although you’re organized unless you’ve been here multiple times before you will still get lost!

Overall, a great time and with a well organized plan, you really can take in a lot without crowds

Grand, overwhelming (in a good way), and absolutely worth i. I visited the Louvre Museum and it was truly an amazing experience.

The first thing that stands out is the sheer scale—the Louvre is massive and incredibly grand. From the architecture to the endless galleries of artwork, it feels like you’re walking through history at every turn. The paintings are stunning, and even just moving through the space is an experience in itself.

It was very busy (which is expected), but I was still able to get in the same day. They also have lockers available, which made it much easier to explore without carrying bags.

Seeing the Mona Lisa was a highlight. I will say—I expected it to be much larger. However, the presentation, security, and the historical importance surrounding it made the moment feel special regardless of its size.

Helpful Tips for First-Time Visitors:

Arrive early or be prepared for crowds

It gets busy quickly, especially around the Mona Lisa

Don’t try to see everything

The museum is huge—focus on a few key sections or highlights

Use the lockers

Makes navigating much more comfortable

Plan your route in advance

It’s easy to get overwhelmed without a plan

Take time to appreciate the building itself

The ceilings, hallways, and architecture are just as impressive as the art

Final Thoughts: The Louvre is more than a museum—it’s an experience. Even with the crowds, it’s absolutely worth visiting and something that stays with you long after.

Virtual trip. I visited the virtual tour of the Louvre Museum in Paris and it was very interesting. The building was originally built in 1190 as a fortress by King Philip II of France to protect the city. Later it became a royal palace, and in 1793, during the French Revolution, it was turned into a public museum.

The museum has many types of art like paintings, sculptures, and ancient art from Egypt, Greece, and Rome. One of the most famous artworks there is the Mona Lisa by Leonardo da Vinci.

In the past, kings and members of the royal court lived there. Today, millions of people visit it every year. I liked the virtual tour because I could see famous artworks and learn about the history of the museum.

The tour made it easy to find the French and Italian paintings and sculptures, but once it ended and we tried to locate Napoleon’s Apartments and the Egyptian section, it felt like a maze.

The viewing system for the Mona Lisa lacked organisation. A single-file queue would work much better. Instead, it was very crowded, with people pushing forward and some staying at the front for a long time. That said, after entering the room at 5:50pm, I reached the front in under 10 minutes, which wasn’t too bad - you just need a bit of patience.

Level 2 was shown on the map, but the lift button for this floor didn’t work. It may have been closed due to ongoing repair work, but there was no signage or information, which added to the confusion.

The magnificent Louvre. The Louvre is one of world's finest museums, and it contains some of the great masterpieces of western art. With a collection spanning Egyptian, Roman, and Greek antiquities, Islamic art, European painting, decorative art, sculpture, and drawings, it is also the largest museum on Earth, and there is no way to see all of it in a single visit.

I bought a regular ticket a few weeks in advance for the first entry and queued at the Carrousel entrance, which seemed to have a reasonable line. Entry was smooth, and once I got in, I headed straight for the "Mona Lisa." Many others had the same plan, and it was already fairly crowded (though less so than later in the morning).

I continued exploring using Rick Steves' free audio tour, which provided a nice overview of the highlights in a bit over an hour. While not comprehensive at all, it provides a good introduction, with stops at "Venus de Milo," "Winged Victory," the "Coronation of Napoleon," and "Liberty Leading the People," among others.

After viewing the French Crown Jewels, I wrapped up my visit with a brief foray to the lower level to see the building's medieval foundations. Because of the Louvre's enormity and what seemed to be a lack of signage (even in French), wayfinding is difficult. This is complicated by the immense crowds that appear by mid-morning.

Despite that drawback, the Louvre is still a must-visit attraction in Paris. If I visited again, I would probably take a guided tour to get more in-depth commentary. Either way, be sure to arrive right at the opening and understand that you will only get to see a small fraction of this magnificent cultural institution.

In awe of history!. The Louvre is an immense museum and can become quite crowded. I suggest paying for private access and a guided tour; this significantly reduces wait times, which can reach several hours during the spring and summer.

It is worth spending as much time there as possible after your guide leaves, so be well-rested before you arrive. It is a truly worthwhile experience.

Aphrodite, Pausanias & Praxiteles.. Aphrodite, Pausanias & Praxiteles.

This copy of Praxiteles' Aphrodite of Thespiae, commissioned perhaps by his favourite model, the courtesan Phryne, with whom he apparently fell in love, is mentioned by Pausanias as part of a group of « Cupid, Phryne, and Aphrodite » in the 2nd century AD.

Its style can be detected in the resemblance of the head to that of the Aphrodite of Cnidus, a work by Praxiteles in his youth, around 360 BC.

The partially draped Aphrodite of Thespiae (frequently repeated in the Hellenistic era, such as the Venus de Milo) is a prelude to the completely naked Aphrodite of Cnidus from around 350 BC.

The Venus was discovered in 1651, during the diggings for the construction of a fountain. In 1681 it was given to Louis XIV and for a time decorated the ''Galerie des Glaces'' of Versailles. Seized during the Revolution, ended in the Louvre Museum.

Iconic, Overwhelming, and Absolutely Worth It. Visiting the Louvre Museum is exactly what you expect it to be: busy, impressive, and unforgettable.

It is crowded, so planning matters. Having a timed entry ticket is a real advantage, and getting in line a bit early definitely helps. Security moved somewhat quickly considering the volume of visitors, and the process felt organized and efficient for a museum of this scale.

One practical tip that worked very well for us: you will need to check bags and jackets, and the cloakroom is directly in front of the entrance stairs. It functions much like a hotel safe system and was very easy to use. This made walking the museum far more comfortable and stress free.

The art itself is phenomenal. There is no other way to say it. The collection is staggering, and you could spend days here and still feel like you barely scratched the surface. For a realistic visit, plan on four to eight hours depending on your pace, interests, and tolerance for crowds. Even in a single day, you can see an incredible amount if you prioritize.

The Mona Lisa room was packed, as expected, but the flow was managed fairly well. You will not linger long, but you will see it, and the experience feels orderly given the demand.

The Louvre is not a casual stop. It is big, busy, and intense. But it is also one of the greatest museums in the world, and the scale and quality of the art make the effort completely worthwhile. Go in with a plan, wear comfortable shoes, and embrace the experience.

The Louvre exhibits sculptures, objets d'art, paintings, drawings, and archaeological finds.”

In other words you could literally spent your lifetime trying to study/enjoy what the museum holds and never make it through it all. As my pictures show you book a timed entrance ticket and stand in the freezing cold (for about 60 - 90 minutes) to enter. FABULOUS MUSEUM BUT BOOK EARLY AND IN ADVANCE. The Louvre Museum is a fascinating place to visit that gets very busy very quickly. I was there for the first appointment time at 09:00 and saw what I wanted to see and left at 10:30. Upon leaving, the queue was around half a mile long to gain entry. Why people just turn up without a ticket instead of booking an appointment for quick and easy access online is astounding. So this is the first thing you should do! Even after doing this you should arrive half an hour before your scheduled time and join the correct queue. Museum lovers could easily spend all day in here but I went to see certain masterpieces hence only being in there 1.5 hours. Another must do is to plan what you want to see in advance online and navigate what rooms they are in so you're not trapesing all over the museum looking for things you want to see. I was also told to get there for the first appointment at 09:00 and go straight to the Mona Lisa. I did this and was able to get some great photos without everyone's head in the pictures. However, most people take their photos and then leave the room so it wasn't as congested as I thought it might of been. Other masterpieces that are a must see are The Caryatids, Venus de Milo, The Winged Victory of Samothrace, Venus and the Three Graces Presenting Gifts to a Young Woman, The Grande Galerie which houses 5 paintings by Leonardo da Vinci, The Raft of the Medusa, Slaves, and Cupid's Kiss. I also visited here at night after closing as it's nice to see all lit up too. Being organised and early is key to enjoying this museum, so be sure to take my advice and not fall into the same traps as lots of others.

Francis I, the one of Chambord & hosting Leonardo da Vinci in the last 3 years of his life in Amboise, this became the residence of French kings. Its historic importance is beyond measure. Louvre. Business with family to enrich culture more at sunset, the view just becomes beautiful I highly recommend

The amazing Louvre Museum❤️. It’s always been my dream to visit, so I decided to take my mum on her 69th birthday. It was very easy to get to and because we had already purchased our tickets we didn’t have to wait in a very long line. The museum itself is very beautiful and very big. My mum has mobility issues so I knew that we wouldn’t be able to stay there too long but in the two hours that we were there we were able to see a lot of amazing artwork including my favourite the Mona Lisa by Leonardo da Vinci. They have quite a few places where you can stop off and eat and rest. I would definitely recommend a visit to this amazing museum.

Treasure of humanity. I would say that going to Paris is not going to the museum, you missed one of the wonders of the world. You have to dedicate at least one day of your holiday, it is really worth every moment!!!!!

The Gauntlet of Art Museums. I have been through many museums in my lifetime, but I have never visited a structure as labyrinthine as The Louvre. It just goes on and on and on. The crowd size varies depending on what you want to see, and this will directly affect how much time you spend on your tour. When my wife and I wanted to see the Mona Lisa, we found ourselves crammed into a room with at least fifty people. Overall, we were there for at least three hours. The staff were friendly. For in the insane amount of art that we saw (and things we didn't get to out of pure exhaustion) Admission was totally reasonable - under 20 euros.

Even more magnificent than imagined!. 10/10 would recommend.

The artwork from the masters is truly a sight to behold. We couldn’t see everything in one day, would probably plan a part 1 / part 2 next time we visit.

Focus Your Visit. One of the world's best museums. I recommend that you plan your visit for what you want to see and I also recommend that you avoid the greatest hits like the Mona Lisa because you will not be able to get close to them due to the big travel groups. But come for the the other treasures and concentrate on what you are most interested in.

Louve Museum. A must to visit. Buy your tickets online and get there 20 minutes early to line up. Check online for what allowed. No pack backs.

There are guided tours, audio machines for self guided tours and maps or a QR map.

All explanations are in French.

Start with the Mona Lisa. Signs direct your there from the start.

Then take your time to view the wonders of sculpture, painting across the centuries. Masterpieces everywhere. You could stay a week. So look online and check what you really want to see and do them first. It’s kept hot inside so wear layers.

Lots of stairs but there are lifts in discreet corners.

There a Metro link below the museum.

Now go and enjoy.

Try 2 other doors for entry, not the main doors. Reservation in the morning if possible.. Coming on top of the Paris bucket list. Make sure to book online early morning. If you prefer to enter through the side doors instead of the main entrance, you will enter much sooner. Create a list of must-see priorities for your family-couple-solo trip (use chatgpt). This will be a max efficient excursion or it will be tough to tour a huge area, especially if you have a family with kids.

Great Museum. One of the most beautiful museums in the world. The glass pyramid in the front is fantastic. A day is not enough to see the entire complex. Entry lines are too long even early in the mornings.

A Museum Full of Charm and Wonder. No words can truly capture the charm of this museum. I’ve visited twice — once with my family and another time with friends — and each visit left me amazed. I always wanted to stay longer and explore every detail.

The experience is truly unique, and I think everyone will have the same feeling I did. A must-visit for anyone who loves history, art, and inspiring spaces.

Contemplation with calm and lightness. This museum takes a lot of time, unless you of a quick pass in it just to see the main arts. I would say the ideal would be to reserve a day to do it calmly.

Louvre Museum. To get to the museum you can do it by metro, Uber, taxi or bus, the transport system in Paris is excellent, the cheapest way is by metro or bus, when you arrive in Paris you can buy the transport card and recharge it according to the number of days you will stay.

I recommend you to buy tickets to the museum well in advance and directly on the museum page, I did not and although the tickets cost 22 euros in the museum, since they were already sold out I had to pay 48 euros for each ticket.

To tour the museum I recommend going with comfortable clothes and shoes suitable for walking, also eat well before entering, since the museum is very large and you must walk a lot to tour and enjoy it.

Worth seeing treasures. A fantastic place to be a bit closer to the history of Paris.

The Louvre Museum, with its historical works and interesting exhibits, should be on the agenda of every Paris visitor.

A map should be booked in advance and it takes a day to explore everything.

It would be absolutely necessary to mention comfortable clothing to wear, as this makes this museum stay much easier. You have to be good on foot.

How old is that?. Let me tell you about The Louvre—it’s like the ultimate treasure hunt, but instead of pirates, you’re dodging tourists! You walk in, and the first thing you see is that giant glass pyramid. It's like someone took a giant cheese grater and made it art!

Once inside, it's a maze of incredible artworks. There’s the Mona Lisa, and people act like she’s the queen! Everyone is trying to get a picture, and I’m over here thinking, “C’mon, she’s not even smiling!”

And then you have everyone rushing around like it’s a race against time. It’s an art lover’s dream, but also a cardio workout if you want to see it all. Seriously, wear comfy shoes!

In short, The Louvre is magnificent, a bit chaotic, and a must-see. Just remember to take a deep breath and enjoy the art. It’s worth the hustle!
Amazing experience. An amazing memory shared with my family. Tickets were easy to book and a good price, which made this trip affordable for us.
Must. See.. Truly one of the greatest on Earth. You'll need much more time than you think; one could spend weeks to see and understand everything held in the collection.

The architecture!. This was our first time visiting Paris and we only had a few days, hearing about the crowds and length of time it may take to try and view a good portion of the Louvre we decided to not go inside, on this occasion, but instead to enjoy the grande scale and detail of the outside, next time we will go in, we were very tempted but seeing the queue was a bit off putting when we still had so much to do and see, but I would still highly recommend, even if just enjoying the outside view.

A Massive Collection. With slightly better queuing arrangements than Versailles, the Louvre is another full day if you intend to do it any justice. Dropping down through the impressive pyramid entrance to a central atrium, there are three "wings", each with hundreds of rooms filled with antiquities, artefacts, statuary - so many things in addition to the art collection that is its "main" draw. Just don't expect to see the Crown Jewels...

Some points to note. Firstly, there are an amazing range of eateries on site - we had some lovely cakes in the Richelieu Wing and Sandwiches from the Atrium, all very good quality. Second, if you want to see the Mona Lisa, bring your mosh pit gear - its a bit of a scrum to say the least. Third, be prepared to just wander - some of our favourite things were not the ones we'd planned to see but things we just happened across. In fact, you may find as we did that the building itself is as much of an attraction as its contents!

Jacques-Louis David. On the occasion of the bicentenary of his death in exile in Brussels in 1825, the Louvre Museum offers a new vision of a personality and a work of exceptional richness and diversity. The exhibition highlights the strength of invention and expressive power of Jacques-Louis David’s painting.

This exhibition offers a journey through David’s work, from The Oath of the Jeu de Paume to Napoleon’s Sacre, via The Death of Marat. It highlights the narrative power of his compositions and their lasting influence on art history.
The Louvre didn't disappoint.. Great museum! We were able to see the Crown Jewels two days before they were stolen, and they shut down the museum to the public! Hopefully, the crooks get caught.

A must do if you have time, and you are brave. I've been going to The Louvre for more than 28 years now, and I've never been once disappointed ! Yes, it can be crowded, and yes the downstairs mall can be a bit of a tourists trap, but the whole place is an experience on itself.

I would recommend to book with a guide to visit this enormous place, or choose ahead of hand the area/time period you would like to visit, because you wont be able to do the entire thing in one single day !

The Mona Lisa is really overrated, and smaller that you would expect, especially next to the giant masterpieces that surround it !

The entrance is free for everyone under 26, and even if the fee can be a bit high for a big group, you know a place like that need to pay for the maintenance of such a building with so many worker. You pay for the entrance, but mostly for the experience.

However, beware of the waiting time to get into the building (especially since the heist), because security is really strict and you might wait a while outside. So if you go in the winter, scarf and coat mandatory, and in the summer, don't forget a cap, bottles of water, and a good amount of patience and sunscreen

The collections are huge. The Louvre Museum is an exceptional place, both for its artistic richness and for the beauty of its architecture. It is a true journey through the history of art, from ancient civilizations to the most famous masterpieces in the world.

The collections are huge: it takes several visits to discover everything. The Mona Lisa, the Venus of Milo, the Raft of the Jellyfish, the Victory of Samothrace... each gallery has a different emotion in store.

Early pays off. It is worth booking admission to 9.00. Even at the Mona Lisa you can take a relaxed look. And in some rooms we were all alone. On leaving at 11.30 it was already pretty crowded everywhere.

an incredible experience. Our visit to the Louvre Museum was an unforgettable experience. The building itself is impressive, and each room is full of artistic treasures. We loved discovering famous works like the Mona Lisa or the Venus de Milo, but also many lesser-known and equally fascinating pieces. The organisation of the museum is excellent, the staff is welcoming, and despite the large number of visitors, everything is well managed. It is a place that must be seen at least once in your life!

A Golden Tip for a Quiet Louvre Visit. If you want to enjoy the Louvre in a special way, here’s a golden tip: buy your ticket for Friday at 6:30 PM. This is the only day of the week when the museum stays open until 9:00 PM, and surprisingly, it’s much less crowded — many tourists don’t know this!

It’s the perfect time to take beautiful photos, admire the masterpieces at your own pace, and truly enjoy the museum’s atmosphere without the rush. An unforgettable experience.

Louvre museum. This museum was so amazing and fascinating. Each room just kept getting better and better.

Julien was great, unparalleled knowledge of art making all feel comfortable. The tour flew bye as a result.. Julien made each of us feel as if we knew each other forever. Great tour guide and person with a wealth of knowledge and it came through while on the tour.

Reflections on glass and history – the Louvre at its most magical ✨. Standing in front of the Louvre pyramid is like standing in the middle of a dialogue between history and the future. My tip: book your ticket online, arrive early in the morning and get lost. For here the visit is not just about seeing art, but about experiencing how creation permeates the whole of humanity's story.Reflections on glass and history ✨

A must. As cheesy as it sounds, the Louvre is a must see. I have been several times and have yet to see everything! Definitely book queue jumping tickets, it will save you heaps of time. Queues can be quite long!
