We travel a lot and visit many different places, so I’m used to the idea that caring for different people is a basic standard, not a special request.

Also, when we got lost, the staff were rude to us and never showed us the way out.

Disappointing and hateful experience. Having intentionally chosen a day when the rooms of Egyptian Antiquities were marked open they were closed!

Moreover, the signage is deplorable without a clear indication of the room numbers, without suggesting routes to move from one helper to another.

Hateful experience.

Easier to steal Napoleon's jewels than buy a ticket. They have to streamline the purchasing procedures on last minute tickets. I went one day in late November midweek and there were few people, with tickets available online, almost immediate entry. You have to address yourself, create passwords, tell him if you are a man or a woman and I don’t know how much else because taken for fatigue I gave up.Obviously on the masterpieces of the museum (maybe even coming from raids made over the centuries in Italy) so much hat.

Chère Directrice générale du Louvre, voici une évaluation de notre visite chez vous.. Dear Director General Laurence des Cars, you have the opportunity to create positive things for your guests and make the Louvre an experience of a lifetime.

It's not like that!

\- 2 hours waiting time. Had I booked tickets not for 11:00 but for example 14:00, we would not have seen anything at all until closing 18:00 after admission 16:00.

\- I was horrified that there were very few safeguards of the artworks: I could go very close to the works. What if I had a chemical in a bag with me and destroyed a Da Vinci? Of course I would be in custody now, but the work would be destroyed.

There were very few guards there, I have already visited many museums, e.g. in Florence the Palazzo Pitti, there was at least 1 guard in each room. Many other museums, most of which had 2 guards. We really said, Too bad they stole from you, but it's definitely your own fault!

\- By the way, I had to get close to the works, as the small signs were otherwise not readable, even through the (faded) writing. Translations? In short supply! And that in a museum that visits the world. Continuous English would be a minimum standard.

\- And by the way, by far more magnificent works in Florence, which are also presented thematically and artistically coherently. In the Louvre, a William Turner is simply "clapped" somewhere between.

And the hype about the Mona Lisa? It is worth looking at much more across the street, the unsecured masterpieces of da Vinci hanging "in front of your nose", such as John the Baptist, Virgin and Child of St. Anna, Portrait of a Lady of the Court of Milan - who can definitely "take on" the Mona Lisa. And no attention is drawn to these masterpieces.

\- Ok, and others have described this in the same way: there is not a minute of leisure to look at a work, because crowds of Asians pass under their noses photographing - why the hell do you go to a museum? The only sense of it, I was there. Then check out the website and stay at home. Of course, you can't "prevent" that.

But: the dolls, which can be photographed in front of the works in a body-hugging and posing manner. FOR WHAT? Influencers do not belong in this framework of a world heritage!

Not to mention the "ladies" with heels. This would not give them access to any museum I know of to avoid breaking the wooden floors.

With your pictograms, you point out that it should not be spoken out loud, it was incredibly loud in your museum.

You can handle these points with guards.

\- Orientation / signage? Again, you offer a joke and we can (!) Read plans (professional background).

\- and then after standing outside in the freezing cold for 2 hours I was very annoyed that the supposedly diverse offer of cafes, restaurants, tea rooms was a joke. 2 great restaurants closed! Cafe? Standing coffee who didn't have coffee or tea. These stations were in the grand entrance hall, and shall we guess? Warm up there and drink something warm: Misrepresentation. And it's impossible for that not to begin to work for thousands of guests.

The one possible restaurant only had access by reservation!

\- Then inside: we are put back in line to get to the individual wings of the house. After we were checked outside twice and again in the entrance. Who's supposed to come into the inner area that wasn't controlled? Is there an unguarded side entrance, well, it probably should have been known better.

My inquiry to the visitor se

Cataclysmic. This museum is an organizational and human catastrophe... I was at the end of October at the museum for the first time very excited and ended up disgusted... The museum closed half of its rooms at 6pm despite a night shift until after 9pm on the pretext that they did not have enough staff for the rooms. We were virulently escorted from one room so that we could finally find our executioners eating haribo in one of the showrooms. The staff is a terrible step possible, hangs out on his phone, does not know how to inform you. Not to mention that the museum is a real maze. The After Sales Service offers me two tickets to apologize for the Service (when I have 3h30 drive to go). They don't necessarily want to refund you because the ticket has been scanned.

If I could give 0 I would just do it for human experience!

Disappointment trying to view the Mona Lisa. We were disgusted by the efforts to see the Mona Lisa. We expected

To walk past on a walk bye queue!… No it was a free for all and anyone that got to the front just stood there!.

Children and short people couldn’t see a thing, it’s an organisational disgrace. Complete waste of time and money.

Far too crowded and an awful visitor experience.. Appalling experience. There is no attempt to manage the crush (at the Mona Lisa); and the museum is rammed with tour groups intent on getting from A to B without a care in the world for the other visitors. This was certainly the worst paid-for 'visitor experience' I have ever had - and what a dismal experience it was too. The lack of any attempt to sensibly manage the flow of visitors through the pinch-points in the museum is just unbelievable - in places there was such a crush that people were fainting and having panic attacks.

Nice museum but incompetent in controlling. Charlie Hebdo, make sure you do a cartoon for this theft.... But instead of wasting the millions of euros you earn from tourists buy yourself an alarm system....

Useless customer service. Booked for 10am on Monday 13 October,

Night before noticed an unhelpful “museum may open later” statement on their website landing page, no times just this random statement.

Got there around 0930, museum not yet open, zero information, people queuing everywhere,

Joined what was possibly the timed entry queue, but who knew if it was, the staff had no interest to help anyone.

After a further 90 minutes the museum was still not open and zero information on when/if it would open. By now there was a queue of thousands, and as we had another timed entrance elsewhere at 1230pm we abandoned it, as even if we got in the thought of how overcrowded it would be was horrific.

Reading other reviews it seems late opening is not unusual. Given this is probably no1 or no2 Paris tourist destination I am both appalled and disgusted.

Personally, save yourself from the disappointment of the Mona Lisa and go the Musée D’Orsay instead where it’s a beautiful building with great art, an organised entry system and pleasant/informative staff

not allowed in due to “problem”. we arrived at the entrance at 1 pm, sunday, october 19 and were told to comeback tomorrow. there was a”problem” and no one else was allowed in. so technically we didn’t see anything inside the louvre. maybe tomorrow? 🤷🏼‍♂️

Chaotic shambles. Had timed tickets for 10am. Arrived to find museum closed no explanation no information on when would open no staff

Thousands of people in long queues not clear what to do

Gave up after two hours queues huge by then and stunned not open

Extraordinary for the presence of works... but the organization must be improved. And it was just any Monday morning.... The museum is extraordinary. But...we bought the ticket online weeks before we arrived. We arrived 10 minutes before the scheduled entrance. The museum, for a disservice, was opened after about an hour. Visitors crowded in at 9, 9:30, 10, 10:30. We did about 2 hours straight inside. We started the tour. Signage is lacking. Insufficient bathrooms. After waiting 1 hour for the bath, it was closed for lack of water. Directed to other bathrooms, there were over thirty people in line because many of the toilets were closed. That's a shame! We are in the most important museum in the world! It is necessary to improve the ordinary organization.

LACK OF EMPATHY WITH A SERIOUS MEDICAL SITUATION. Because of a very serious family medical situation, I had to cancel our trip to Paris. I have tickets for Monday, October 13, 2025. I provided the medical documentation, and yet the museum has refused not only to refund the amount of tickets, but even to offer a voucher for a future visit.

I deeply regret the lack of empathy of the Louvre Museum for a very serious family medical situation.

I understand that there are rules, but I expected a minimum of humanity and flexibility in exceptional circumstances.

I love art and the Louvre, but this experience with their Customer Service has been very disappointing.
Fraud. You've come up with how much you charge customers for a package. I want you to compensate me. Otherwise, I'll go to the consumer attorney. Fortunately, I paid 29.50 for 2 fries.

Crowed, chaotic and sweaty. Chaotic crowed and sweaty

As an art historian i want to give my honest opinion: Terrible experience!! Of course rhe art is amazing and i would give that an 11 star on itself. But the Louvre is extremely busy and hot. It feels like walking on a festival, you can't walk freely, it is extremely crowded.. There is no airconditioning and almost no toilets only at the entrance, and even the amount of toilets was way to little for the amount of visitors, also no running water and no soap dispenser that worked.

You are in lines from the entrance until you leave; lines to enter, lines to scan the tickets, lines for the toilets lines for every important art object, lines to ask for imformation etc etc .

The Louvre has billions of money but no decent toilets : they should let less visitors come in to make the experience better and also build more toilets and more staff to help people find there way. Which leads my to my last point;

There are almost no maps to be found in situ which is extremley important for such a big museum. Maps are rarely to be found during your walk in the museum, only on certain points, which makes jt hard to get to the other wings and levels.

My initial plan was to stay for a whole day so i could see enough but i left after 2,5 hours, and i was exhausted

Disgusting. The ticket scanner demanded that I wait in line twice: once to scan my ticket and the other time to scan my membership card. She was too busy chatting with a colleague to note the fact that I had already scanned my ticket and she screamed for me to queue again. The staff is poorly trained and attentive. Guardians are riveted to their cell phones, unable to give any information, even when speaking in French, and seem to dispense with the need to monitor collections.

Hot & Crowded With Inadequate Toilet Facilities Inside & Out. I would never visit the Louvre again. We had 10am tickets but still queued for over an hour to get in. I asked an employee if there was a toilet nearby I could use and was told the only available toilets were inside. How crazy with such a huge amount of people they keep waiting, to have no facilities available. Once inside the toilet queues were (not surprisingly) eye-wateringly long. In the majority of areas, the crowds were so dense it was impossible to appreciate the artwork. Everyone is intent on getting selfies with the Mona Lisa and with so much body-heat, it was swelteringly hot throughout. This is mass-tourism at its very worst (and the irony is not lost that I was one of those tourists adding to the throng). I’m fit and healthy but was practically swooning with fatigue from overheating and mental overload from the crowds and bustle. It was a very unpleasant experience I would never repeat.

No refund on a non guided tour?. I was very excited, and attempted to book an admission only ticket to the Louvre, but I accidentally entered the wrong date! I thought; no problem, I will change the date. I could not, so I purchased the correct date and requested a refund for the wrong date.

They denied my request because the “tour guide doesn’t allow for money back”…it’s not a guided tour. It’s strictly an admission ticket. Ridiculous.

Terrible licensed tour guide. We purchased tickets on Louvre Website with a tour guide which turned out to be a terrible experience. Our tour in English was scheduled on September 4, 2025 at 15:00 with a licensed Louvre guide. After 15 minute long unnecessary presentation in the Conference Room, our Guide, Cosme De Lustrac, proceeded with the tour. He spoke poor English (mumbling), was rushing, and instead of proving relevant information regarding the art, was talking about his and his family interests. All four of us felt that we wasted our time and money on this worthless guided tour! It was really disappointing that Louvre Museum hires such terrible guides.

Terrible long queues. Conflicting advise. Impossible to gain entry without ticket. Impossible to BUY tickets as online. I have waited for 2 1/2 hours in 2 different queues. Some staff are on strike. I was in the long queue that had tickets but noonevaround 2cadvise so then went into the ICKETLESS queue as directed. When I got to the front of THAT queue there is NO entry without a ticket. It is IMPOSSIBLE to buy a ticket online because of poor Internet reception. Their website should CLEARLY Cstate NO ENTRY WITHOUT a TICKET . ALWGEDLY the most popular museum in the world ??? Not with me it ain't

Very disappointed. It may be the best museum in the world, but it may also be the worst experience, for its massing and dismal organization.

They force you to buy the ticket online by time slots, but what you find on the outside to enter is a kilometer queue that prevents you from meeting your time of entry, what is the value of the previous reservation?

It is true that once inside you can see unique works, but between elbows, rooms so crowded that I doubt anyone enjoys art like this.

Really sorry, I'm not coming back.
Total chaos. Too chaotic, disorganized as if there were no tomorrow, not to mention that if you pee you better go back to Italy that at the Autogrill the bathrooms are cleaner.

Louvre Museum, a bittersweet and disappointing experience. If you're thinking of visiting the Louvre as a family, especially in high season, prepare for a two-sided experience.

We went on a Wednesday afternoon in August, trusting that the museum would remain open until 9pm and that in the afternoon there would be fewer people (the rooms would have to be closed from 8:30pm). Unfortunately, the expectation of a relaxing visit was shattered by a number of issues that made the experience decidedly frustrating.

At first glance, the organization seemed chaotic. The paper maps were small and unclear, making it difficult to orient oneself, also because many rooms did not have the corresponding number. To make matters worse, the museum looked very dirty: dust accumulated on the works and on the floor, the display cases were full of fingers and the bathrooms were in poor condition, often without soap or toilet paper, and with non-working flushes.

The most disappointing and irritating moment came in the late afternoon. Despite having booked admission for 15:30, we saw the museum begin to close the rooms as early as 17:00. Staff, without providing explanations or warnings, began letting people out and blocking elevators and stairs. When we tried to ask for an explanation, we got no answers, just rude looks.

The most amazing thing is that they literally kicked us out of the theaters while we were admiring the works. It was a humiliating and incredibly frustrating situation. As early as 19:00, large sections of the museum were inaccessible, including level 0, level 2, the Sully wing and part of the Richelieu wing. Only later did we learn that the cause was a lack of staff.

It is unacceptable that such a renowned museum, especially in high season, is unable to guarantee the opening of its main areas until official hours. We paid the ticket for a Service that was not provided to us, seeing our visit drastically reduce due to a problem that management should have prevented.

In conclusion, despite the vastness and beauty of the works, our visit to the Louvre was marred by poor management and an obvious lack of staff and cleanliness. A real shame, which turned an opportunity to admire a unique artistic heritage into a disappointing and stressful experience.

Dreadful experience. Massive queue even though you have timed entry. Incredibly crowded and entrances once inside randomly closed so you can’t follow route you planned in advance. Appalling gallery map and rooms without numbers or numbers you can’t find. Toilets grim, and very limited in number. Most staff spoke English which helped but their directions were often not right. Saw very little of what I had planned to see. A dreadful experience. Not much chance of getting anywhere near the Mona Lisa!

French culture and economic interests.. I could not travel due to being interned in my country of residence “Argentina” with the possibility of having a liver transplant.

I sent all medical information and person to the Louvre and my application was rejected.

I emailed the museum again.

Culture in the world is all complexes that include knowledge, beliefs, art, morals, law, customs, and any other habits and capabilities acquired by man.

Culture shapes people’s identities and reinforces social cohesion – – and divides societies. It creates bridges between groups and people, creates peace and is a significant economic agent. Culture encompasses ways of life, religion, law and science, tradition and innovation, art and music, language and literature.

The situation that I stated through medical documentation is not covered either in the essence of culture or in the ethics of people.

I request the possibility of using the entry to this icon of French culture at another time of my life, if she offers me the possibility of doing so.

Kind regards

Diana Capanna

Outright rejection

Louvre is not recommended!!!. No tickets to buy locally, only online bookable with time slot. This is what I did and yet I was given a wrong date. Complaints on site were rejected as Ticket Online was booked. Several people stood at the ticket counter with exactly the same problem.

Unfriendly staff. Online support hides behind the terms and conditions.

Pure rip-off of visitors!!!

Great disappointment!!!. Not everyone goes to the Louvre to see the Mona Lisa... There are those who, like me, wait a lifetime to go there and see the Egyptian antiquities section (the third most important in the world) and find themselves with the entrance CLOSED and a simple maintenance sign was really bad!! Huge disappointment also because it is right to inform paying visitors if there are sections closed or under maintenance! I probably would have gone there at another time or I should have had the choice anyway.

Annalisa from Florence

DO NOT BUY TICKETS BY VIATOR THAT IS TRIPADVISOR. Viator sold me three tickets to the Louvre museum at more than twice the price and they turned me down.

• Reservation number: 1681477619

\- Booking reference number: 1297778677

• Amount paid: USD 158.97

Date of purchase: 05/08/2025

• Date and time of rejected access: 07/08/2025 at 9:30hs

• Product purchased: Tickets to the Louvre Museum (no queues / priority access / depending on the exact title of your booking)

So far it did not solve the problem!!!

Never again!. Too bad! Unfortunately, I cannot recommend visiting this intrinsically excellent museum. Apparently, it has now become an example of the effects of overtourism. The timeslot tickets are only there to trade the masses - no sign of admission limitation. We arrived 1 hour later than our timeslot. That was lucky. We were able to go through. The people who had a ticket for this time found themselves in a very long line. Indoors dense crowding and a very high Noise level. A well-intentioned but cumbersome audio guide.

Unfortunately, I think we were here for the last time.

Extremely disappointed.. Everything I thought of the louvre was shattered in 30 seconds.

Firsy timed entrance to manager flow of visitors... Didn't so anything to keep anyone in line, lots of pushing and shoving and way too many people at once. Very crowded especially around the mons Lisa, tiktok influences ( what ever they are) hogging the spaces in front having to be moved along by staff. Way too complicated to find what you're looking for. Too easy to miss parts important to you. No organisation it's just a free for all. I think properly managed system. Should be put in place so that EVERY visitor has a great experience. I was literally pushed out of there due to others behaviour. Couldn't take photos always walking in front and stopping dead in front of you so you'd bang into them. I won't come back here if michelangelo came bake and gave a demonstration and I love him to pieces, the louvre has some of the best artifacts in the world, but you wouldn't know it with the system it has now. Better flow and less allowed in at any one time would make it better for all.

Bad experience. Too many people. Poorly organized around Mona Lisa. Stressed atmosphere everywhere. Completely wild with so many visitors that they can't organize a queue and a better introduction to Mona Lisa and other important works. My kids say they're never gonna come here again and I have the same feeling. So many other lovely places in this city.

Terrible company, beware - they will scam you out of your money.. Terrible business practices, scam, rude on phone, DO NOT PURCHASE TICKETS FOR THE LOUVRE FROM VIATOR!!!! You will lose your money, they are a SCAM!!!!

I will never see Mona Lisa now in my life time!. Booked in advance as advised. Got there on last day of our holiday to excitedly go in at 11am. Told with no compassion that we was not allowed in, only 11.15am onwards were allowed and to go away and try and get refund of 88 euros! Still not got after filling form in. I walk with a stick I wont be able to attend Paris again as my condition is getting worse this was a bucket list opportunity I will never see Mona Lisa now in my life time, I was very sad and even cried.

Terrible Experience- We had tickets, but the Louvre closed exceptionally.. and didn’t inform us! Beware!. A truly awful experience. We booked 2 months in advance to visit the Louvre on Thursday 3rd July at 11:00am only to travel for an hour across Paris to find that the Louvre had had an exceptional closure that morning until 11:15. We were told that we should have received an email about the closure … we hadn’t received anything! We tried to queue as 11:30 tickets were being allowed entry, but we were still not allowed entry. It was very chaotic as others with earlier tickets were doing the same! No signs were on display, just 2 members of staff trying to deal with the situation. There was no apology, or explanation for the closure when I contacted the Museum and again we were bluntly informed we should have received an email about the closure. Categorically no email in my Inbox or Junk! Nearly a week on we still have not received a refund for our tickets! Not sure how often this happens, but be sure to check the website before travelling! There were rumours that the closure was due to staff striking? We enjoyed Paris, but not our experience of the Louvre….the Musée d’Ornay was superb the next day… so well run! I hate posting negative reviews, but just felt I had to after this!

The Louvre was a terrible disappointment!. The Louvre was a terrible disappointment because in many ways it is not very accessible. We did not use a tour and maybe it would be better with one. You may have heard about the recent strike by workers there. We were there shortly before that but it did not surprise us when we heard about it. The whole process is confusing from the website/app to the lines. Even just trying to figure out where you were supposed to queue outside was a challenge. The people running it are disorganized and it was pretty clear to us that just having better signage would have been at least some help. We had to show our tickets (on our phones) to get into the line which was a trial in and of itself because the app was balky; we were told by a worker it was because of poor connectivity near the pyramid! Really?! And what is being done to fix that?! We thought we were good to go, however, we had to queue to get into a gallery only to find out that we were supposed to have first stopped somewhere else after entering the building to get another ticket. It was a sort of two-step process that should not have been so easy to miss. The crowds were unreal. At times, you could barely turn in place without touching another person. It began to feel dangerous. We spent very little time there because we were just tired and exasperated by the business of getting into the building. On this trip we also visited the Rijksmuseum in Amsterdam and the British Museum in London and saw plenty of crowds but nothing like the disorganization at the Louvre. Perhaps we will live long enough to try again during winter months. We wish President Macron Godspeed in his plan to improve things but right now, it is sort of a mess. You will need fortitude to make this sojourn. Do not go in summer.

A cultural icon… with the soul of a bureaucratic nightmare 🏛️☀️❌. We were excited to visit the Louvre — but the reality was anything but enjoyable.

First, you need to book well in advance. Then you’re locked into a fixed time slot, which feels more like a business appointment than a cultural outing.

But the worst part? Even with a reservation, we ended up in a long, chaotic queue under the sun, with no shade and no real explanation.

After waiting endlessly, we gave up and left.

The whole thing felt poorly organized and completely disconnected from the experience you expect at such a world-class museum.

In the end, just a big waste of time and money. Disappointing.

Non existant customer service. Disgusted.

Purchased two tickets from their own website and today was refused entry at the door as it was for VIPs only😡

We had a contract and they were happy to take my money and then refuse us entry with no notification at all.

Non existent customer service, suggest you go to the Musee d'Orsay instead.

My experience from the Louvre. The museum is unique!

The exhibits are perfect!

But at 12:30 we had an entrance with our 10-year-old son, we were there from noon.

For 2 pairs of headphones we were around me until 2 and rude told us not complain when you bought the ticket you should have bought and our headphones are done....we will have at 2!

We take the headsets 12€ we enter.....

I didn't work "old Nintendo" my little, when I returned them giving her employee I say they don't work and answered all the irony I know!

And he was laughing!

Also inside because we wanted to go to the apartments of Napoleon an employee told us that they are closed due to heat....we asked another employee below showed us them....

These...

Thank you!

Sistema de comprar entradas pésimo.. The ticket system for this museum is terrible. Yesterday we went to the Louvre to buy tickets, but we were told that tickets could only be purchased online. This is terrible for tourists coming from other countries because, in France, if you don't have a phone made or from France, you can't have your own Wi-Fi. This means you have to look for a Wi-Fi with a password you know, or look for a general, free Wi-Fi without a password. There, they told us there was Wi-Fi, but although we finally found it and connected, the connection wasn't good and it was practically impossible to buy tickets. Then they told us that today we could buy tickets directly at the museum in the morning; this was told to us by a museum employee. We didn't buy the tickets online at home, as they told us they could be bought at the museum. Today we went to the museum in the morning at 9:30 and they told us that tickets could ONLY be purchased online, which led us to having to buy them online and spending an hour and a half just to buy some tickets. We finally got into the museum. It was very nice, but the actual ticket buying process was AWFUL.

Get ready to Mona-lotta …. The Louvre itself is worth a visit - even if it’s to tick off the tourist list of ‘I did it and I saw the Mona Lisa’ however, what’s incredibly disappointing is the long queue times (even with a ticket booked in advance!) to get into the Louvre, the disorganised chaos outside the main attraction and the incredibly rude staff that herd you like cats a full 30 minutes prior to closure, sending you back and fourth to get out, despite questioning the rude member on the team of her colleagues clear mistake. I’d recommend, much like some of the other reviews, add 3hrs onto planning and perhaps booking the earliest ticket you can. We had booked the 16:30 ticket, and after the queuing to get into the Louvre and queuing to see the Mona Lisa, we only realistically had 15-20mins to view what was left open due to the early closure. For those that don’t enjoy chaotic crowds, or who don’t want to be part of a cattle bus, I’ve attached the Mona Lisa for ease. 😊

Nope I'm good. I would just go to other galeries and museums.

First of all, there is no leniency on tickets. I booked a ticket online weeks in advance. I was going through the process and they want you to make an account which is annoying but I picked the 19th at the beginning and then when I got the tickets in email it was for the 14th (before I arrived amd not the same day of the week which makes me think it was a technical error not my mistake) and so immediately contacted the museums by phone. The person on the phone said they could do that and told me what form to submit. So, I submitted the form probably 10 minutes after the purchase. When they got back, they said they could not reschedule for later. Frustrating.

So I booked another ticket for the day I wanted and just ate the cost.

When I got there, I did not stand in line for long 15 min (maybe because it was Thursday at 12)

Museums it's self: not my favorite. I think the concept of the mona lisa is dump. It is a fine painting but only popular because it was stolen. Either way, trying to see it is claustrophobic. The rest of the museums is not bad, but there is not as much variety as I was hoping from the largest collection. Also, I wish there was a small section on more recent artists to make comparisons to. I know it is a museums so "old" is common, but I guess I was expecting more of a gallery. As well as what was there, I feel like was poorly organized.

I did enjoy the art and there was a section on fashion which was cool.

Staff and building: staff are good I had no problems. Yes, some bathrooms have a long line, but just don't go to the one in the main entrance or by the cafeteria, I fear that is common knowledge.

In the end, I will not be returning. I went to another museum in Paris and it had similar art and was free. Carnavalet Museum, Musée Carnavalet - Histoire de Paris. I think unless you're desperate to barely see the mona lisa, just go to some other museums and art galleries.

100% disappointing. Extremely disappointed tour in the Museum.

It’s a world famous museum no doubt about it, but the service of the museum is worse than a bus station.

1) Potential Visitors, please don’t believe on their website, it’s full of lies.

2) If you are coming out side of France, it’s means you are going to entrance in an Amazon jungle, no one will understand you and you won’t understand them.

3) First of all Staff ( what is extremely minimum compared with the size of the museum). They will give you feeling that you are entrancing to the airport immigration and you are a criminal.

4) Map: they kept guide map ( if you are lucky, then you will find it). Where no any inter national standard chronological information. Even the structure plan is wrong, if the authority is brave enough to, please, challenge me, I’ll proof that you miss guided the visitors.

5) If you are on wheel chair or you have baby on push chair, then you are entering in hell, where you won’t find lift, no assistance guide, and if you are lucky to find any guide , they won’t understand you and give you wrong information.

6) Every level doesn’t have lift facilities to go for the next, it means you can’t enjoy the whole museum. And to find the lift you need to hire “Sherlock Holmes”. Very few of them, what are nearly at invisible corner even behind the no entry ⛔️ or no signs behind white doors.

7) description of most of the subject with their own languages or some of them has no English (Pathetic).

8) I’m a history lover, any history made value to me, but if you are not then it’s a waste of your money and time.

9) Finally, extremely poor management: if you are lucky enough and reach to the room 711 where is the Leonardo de Vinci’s immortal creation “Mona Lisa”. You will see the staff will treat you that you are in council office came to collect your weekly benefit money from them.

Humble request to the management team:

Please, be modern with modern technology, be kind with visitors who give your salary. Keep your all lift in one designated place, use international language for foreigners,

Your mismanagement is putting down the countries value and honour.

Thank you

An undignified treatment for the tourist. What happened at the Louvre today is hard to explain. We bought the ticket three months ago. On Thursday we are writing from the Louvre to inform us that there could be delays in entering. Our schedule was nine. But we haven't gotten in. For two and a half hours the employees of the Louvre who mysteriously claimed not to be from Louvre and having no information about what was happening have not let us pass. And the museum hasn’t opened until half past three: five hours after the normal opening time.

We have been told that the only options were to wait in the sun of justice without water or bath or to return in the afternoon because we were not returned. When the atmosphere has warmed up because there were thousands of people queuing in the sun (including children and the elderly) and without a single explanation, (they were just claiming that we hoped they would open soon) we have been given a QR to request the return.

In the end, they have claimed a serious security problem. And those of us who left and came back when they reopened couldn’t get through because the queues were hours and the museum closed at six.

All we've been told was a lie. Actually, it was a covert strike allegedly over the overexploitation of the Louvre and allegedly spontaneous. And how can it be something spontaneous and that they know it since Thursday? What if they knew why not strike with minimum services? Or why not warn the thousands of people we were there to leave instead of keeping them waiting 5 1/2 hours in the sun? Because then we wouldn't have been in the news. And we wouldn't have been hostage to their claims.

Is it really because of overexploitation or because of their working conditions?

In any case an undignified treatment for thousands of tourists who have paid admission and have gone to Paris to see a wonderful museum and have turned without seeing it and after spending many hours in the sun.
No admission. 95 minutes waited in the blazing sun without any info. Total failure from management. Rewinding tickets is additional hassle.

Terrible. I booked online,didn’t discover the booking was wrong date til rec’d email. Called customer service. They said they fixed the wrong date and actually a date a month later for a date we would be back in US for a month. Ended up having to pay again at the Louvre, which turned out to be 75% less than was charged by TripAdvisor! Will never use their service again!

Disappointment due to lack of flexibility in changing date. We had planned very much to visit the Louvre during our trip to Paris, but we made a mistake with the date of the tickets, buying them for June 8 and showing up on the 9th. Upon arrival, we could not enter as the tickets were not valid for that day.

We tried to contact the service to request at least one change or some kind of solution, but we were informed that there is no possibility to modify or refund tickets, according to their policies. We understand that these rules exist, but it was disappointing that there is no flexibility, especially considering the cost of tickets and the importance to us of this visit.

This experience left us feeling that the commercial aspect carries more weight than the cultural mission that should have such an emblematic place. Unfortunately, given what happened, we are not sure we will try again.

We recommend to be very careful with the date of tickets to avoid inconvenience.

Deplorable. With my ticket in hand for the Haute Couture exhibition I arrive in front of the Pyramid of the Louvre blackened with people. I ask where I should go for the exhibition, they send me in a queue that has nothing to do with my ticket!! I finally find after personal research the right line. I arrive in the crowded lobby of the Louvre, I go up to the exhibition and there, surprised, I am forced to take a ticket for the entire Louvre, I am taken from above saying that I must go through the museum for the exhibition but we do not have the plans of the Louvre, a separate entrance could very well exist! Once relieved of these extra euros I look for the guide for my visit, I ask the staff, it lasts 20 minutes and in fact you have to come downstairs in a corner of the hall. So no guide and 12€ more lost ... brief website unclear, obligation to pay more, incompetent and hyper unpleasant staff or "how to pump money to tourists treating them like ...". CONGRATULATIONS LE LOUVRE for this magnificent image of France that you give and this stress when of moment should be pleasant!

No air flow or A/C in whole building. For one of the most famous and important buildings in the world, and one of the most busy, you would expect a form of A/C and airflow! But no, everyone just trudges along, sweating, coughing, and sneezing. I got heat exhaustion from the greenhouse effect of so many people under a glass roof. I don’t want to imagine what it’d be like in the peak of summer! Install A/C!!

Disappointed with the Louvre.. Travel agent booked our tickets as museum entry only. After we entered the museum, we wished to get audio guides but they were all sold out. This was at 10am. First disappointment.

The map of the Louvre is very difficult to navigate, so it was frustrating trying to find things we wanted to see and we spent a long time walking around trying to find and constantly getting lost! Asked staff for assistance with directions and still got lost. Second disappointment.

Then trying to find the toilets, again map directions were useless, and again asking staff for directions to closest toilets also proved to be useless. Third disappointment.

When we did eventually find the items we were interested to look at, the information accompanying the items were written in French with no English descriptions. Fourth disappointment.

Of all our time spent in Paris, our visit to the Louvre was by far our worst experience.

TOURIST SCAM, OVERRATED, ZERO RECOMMENDED.. It is an overrated museum, with hordes of people, badly marked, everything in French nothing in English is not marked the rooms (it is a mess to walk) and worst of all you are thrown out 30 minutes before. Do not buy tickets after 16 hrs because at 17:30 you literally run and at 17 hrs they close the Gioconda so it was not possible to see it, I expressed my dissatisfaction and they told me that at the reception I could claim my refund of the ticket and oh surprise there were no staff with which to make the claim, I came home and made the claim and they tell me that it is not appropriate. Which I consider a big scam, your ticket limit should be 2 pm, as 90 minutes is insufficient to appreciate it. I do not recommend it the best way to enjoy Paris is through its monuments and gardens.

Disgrace to the Louvre. ルーブル美術館は自体は素晴らしい。でもルーブルの恥と言えるのが、アンジェリーナだ。席はいっぱい空いているのに、長く列で待たされる。無理やり有料の水を注文させられ、チップも強要される。ルーブルに行っても絶対ここには行かないように。ルーブルの恥だ！ルーブルもなんとかすべきでは？

The Louvre itself is wonderful. But Angelina is a disgrace to the Louvre. There are plenty of seats available, but you have to wait in a long line. You are forced to order water for a fee, and they also demand tips. If you go to the Louvre, don't go here. It's a disgrace to the Louvre! Shouldn't the Louvre do something about it?

Give it a miss.. Booked a time but still had to queue for 30mins. Far to many people squashed in at once. So crowed very difficult to see anything. Was there 2 hours and saw Mona Lisa and some other paintings. Very warm inside and a lack of toilets. Had to queue again for 20mins. We just had to leave. Waste of money and time.
Terrible experience. Terrible experience 2 hours queue despite having booked the ticket. Dear French lacking organization, a shame to be the most visited museum in the world

Shocking!. Got our tickets at home before visiting, didn't make a difference to queuing here! Arrived an hour before our time and our 2pm queue had already started to form, so we joined the queue. Eventually got in after priority to see queues everywere again! At every escalator there was a large queue to enter an area then once up to escalator another queue. We hadn't got our tickets beforehand to queue like this. We was only staying for 3 days in France so didn't want to waste time queuing, didn't see any art/ painting in the end. Just ended up leaving. Toilets, bars everything had a massive queue! It's unacceptable to expect people to queue like that. It's not an enjoyable experience for anyone. Just getting as many people in a possible then no one can enjoy!

We definitely don't recommend coming here and won't visit again!

The juice ain't worth the squeeze.. The Louvre has one of the best collections of art and artifacts in the world. It also has one of the absolutely worst managed user experiences in the world. I'm older and have been going to museums and exhibitions on all seven continents for over 60 years, so I have a pretty decent experience base. We were a group of five, all experience travelers who'd prebooked an 11:00 entrance ticket. Where to line up? Great question. Abysmal lack of directional signage. Finally found the correct line. Enter on time? Forget about it. Once inside, it was chaos. We were told we couldn't enter the part the museum we wished to see because it was too crowded.

I won't bore you with any more of the horrid experience.

But the Museum's management needs to decide if it just wants to pack in more people than the Museum can physically accommodate or cut the daily visitor numbers to give them a more-pleasant experience.

Catastrophic!!!. Catastropfic. Don’t bother coming here. Overcrowded. With a reserve time to enter you eait one hour in front of the entrence. You can’t see anything. You can just struggle for air and exit.

A blight on humanity. Terrible crowd management. Rude and unhelpful staff who gave inconsistent advice.

You will queue for (literally) hours, even if you have pre-booked.

And if you wanted to appreciate any art whilst you’re there, you won’t really be able to because swarms of people block you from seeing most of the paintings.

We were genuinely shocked that more was not done to safely manage the crowd. It is particularly dangerous for the young, elderly (we saw some near accidents on the very crowded steps), fragile. It is a tragic accident waiting to happen.

Do not go.

Simply fraud and empty promises. We have reserved tickets and the day we were already at the Louvre we received an email saying sorry that we cannot send them the tickets. When we asked a member of staff, they explained to us that the tickets are sold three times, for example. When we claimed damages, they laughed at us. I would not recommend buying tickets through this platform! It was also stipulated that the platform can't help it and the Louvre was to blame!

Skip. Order tickets in advance, stand in line and then join another queue. Total 1 hour. Once we were inside, there were so many people that you could not look at the paintings, always ngn in the way.

A disaster!. I come regularly to the Louvre and the situation is constantly deteriorating as regards visitor management. Concrete example , at the entrance located at the inverted pyramid there are in theory two lines , one for groups and one for individuals ... and all the mass of visitors meet to pass the same portal of detection in the most remarkable slow!!

The hourly reservation is simple theory insofar as it allows only the entrance and not at all the time when we will cross the entrance!!

In short , Parisians like me , go elsewhere , tourists , visit other museums much more welcoming!!!
